revision.comment: 新規作成・與喜天満神社（よきてんまんじんじゃ）は、奈良県桜井市初瀬にある神社。
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 與喜天満神社 |画像 = [[File:Yoki-tenman-jinja honden.jpg|250px]]
本殿 |所在地 = [[奈良県]][[桜井市]][[初瀬]]14 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 32 | 緯度秒 = 05.9 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 54 | 経度秒 = 41.6 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP | 地図 = Japan Nara | ラベル位置 = left |祭神 = [[菅原道真]] |社格 = 旧[[郷社]] |創建 = 946年（天慶9年） |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = |神事 = }} '''與喜天満神社'''（よきてんまんじんじゃ）は、[[奈良県]][[桜井市]][[初瀬]]にある[[神社]]。旧社格は[[郷社]]。 ==祭神== ;主祭神 *[[菅原道真]]（すがわら の みちざね） ;配祀神 *[[天照大神]]（あまてらすおおかみ） *[[大倉姫神]]（おおくらひめのかみ） == 歴史 == 神社の所在地にある[[初瀬]]は、道真の先祖にあたる[[野見宿禰]]の出身地とされており、次いで[[土師氏]]となり、菅原氏にとってゆかりある土地であったとされている[https://sakurai-kankou.jimdo.com/%E5%90%8D%E6%89%80%E6%97%A7%E8%B7%A1/%E5%88%9D%E7%80%AC%E9%81%93%E5%91%A8%E8%BE%BA/%E8%88%87%E5%96%9C%E5%A4%A9%E6%BA%80%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/　與喜天満神社（桜井市 初瀬） - 一般社団法人 桜井市観光協会]2019年4月23日　閲覧。『[[長谷寺験記]]』によると、創建は946年（天慶9年）とされており、後に初瀬の産土神としても信仰されるようになった。 == 摂末社 == *瀧蔵権現社 （祭神：[[速玉命]]、[[伊弉諾尊]]、[[伊弉冉尊]]） *白太夫社（祭神：[[白太夫|度会春彦]]） *櫻葉社 （祭神：[[伊予親王]]） *八王子社 （祭神：[[天照大神]]、[[素戔鳴尊]]） == 文化財 == === 重要文化財 === * 木造天神坐像 : 衣冠束帯姿に「怒り天神」とも呼ばれる引き締めたような表情をした[[寄木造]]の坐像であり、像内に「正元元年」(1259年)の銘があることから、現存する国内最古の天神像の彫刻とされている[https://www.nara-np.co.jp/news/20120421085721.html　重文、新たに2件 - 弥勒仏坐像（弥勒寺）、天神坐像（與喜天満） - 奈良新聞]2019年4月23日　閲覧。これまでは本殿にて人目に触れぬよう祀られていたが、現在は[[奈良国立博物館]]に保存されている。 == 現地情報 == ;所在地 *[[奈良県]][[桜井市]][[初瀬]]14 ;交通アクセス * 最寄駅：[[近鉄大阪線]][[長谷寺駅]]下車後、徒歩約25分 == 脚注 == === 出典 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} ==外部リンク== {{commonscat|Yoki-tenman-jinja}} *[https://www.yokitenmanjinja.jp/ 與喜天満神社（公式サイト）] {{菅公聖蹟二十五拝}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:よきてんまんしんしや}} [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧郷社]] [[Category:桜井市の歴史]] [[Category:桜井市の建築物]] [[Category:天満宮]]

{{神社 |名称 = 與喜天満神社 |画像 = [[File:Yoki-tenman-jinja honden.jpg|250px]]
本殿 |所在地 = [[奈良県]][[桜井市]][[初瀬]]14 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 32 | 緯度秒 = 05.9 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 54 | 経度秒 = 41.6 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP | 地図 = Japan Nara | ラベル位置 = left |祭神 = [[菅原道真]] |社格 = 旧[[郷社]] |創建 = 946年（天慶9年） |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = |神事 = }} '''與喜天満神社'''（よきてんまんじんじゃ）は、[[奈良県]][[桜井市]][[初瀬]]にある[[神社]]。旧社格は[[郷社]]。 ==祭神== ;主祭神 *[[菅原道真]]（すがわら の みちざね） ;配祀神 *[[天照大神]]（あまてらすおおかみ） *[[大倉姫神]]（おおくらひめのかみ） == 歴史 == 神社の所在地にある[[初瀬]]は、道真の先祖にあたる[[野見宿禰]]の出身地とされており、次いで[[土師氏]]となり、菅原氏にとってゆかりある土地であったとされている[https://sakurai-kankou.jimdo.com/%E5%90%8D%E6%89%80%E6%97%A7%E8%B7%A1/%E5%88%9D%E7%80%AC%E9%81%93%E5%91%A8%E8%BE%BA/%E8%88%87%E5%96%9C%E5%A4%A9%E6%BA%80%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/　與喜天満神社（桜井市 初瀬） - 一般社団法人 桜井市観光協会]2019年4月23日　閲覧。『[[長谷寺験記]]』によると、創建は[[946年]]（[[天慶]]9年）とされており、後に初瀬の産土神としても信仰されるようになった。 == 摂末社 == *瀧蔵権現社 （祭神：[[速玉命]]、[[伊弉諾尊]]、[[伊弉冉尊]]） *白太夫社（祭神：[[白太夫|度会春彦]]） *櫻葉社 （祭神：[[伊予親王]]） *八王子社 （祭神：[[天照大神]]、[[素戔鳴尊]]） == 文化財 == === 重要文化財 === * 木造天神坐像 : 衣冠束帯姿に「怒り天神」とも呼ばれる引き締めたような表情をした[[寄木造]]の坐像であり、像内に「正元元年」(1259年)の銘があることから、現存する国内最古の天神像の彫刻とされている[https://www.nara-np.co.jp/news/20120421085721.html　重文、新たに2件 - 弥勒仏坐像（弥勒寺）、天神坐像（與喜天満） - 奈良新聞]2019年4月23日　閲覧。これまでは本殿にて人目に触れぬよう祀られていたが、現在は[[奈良国立博物館]]に寄託されている。 === その他 === [[File:Samurai O-Yoroi 2.jpg|thumb|200px|白糸威鎧（長谷寺所蔵、[[東京国立博物館]]にて展示）。当初は與喜天満神社に伝来した。]] [[長谷寺]]が所蔵する重要文化財の甲冑5点（白糸威鎧（大袖付）・赤糸威鎧（大袖付）・鷹羽威鎧（大袖付）・三目札鎧・藍韋威肩赤大袖）は、かつては同寺の[[鎮守神|鎮守社]]であった與喜天満神社に伝来しており、[[1560年]]（[[永禄]]3年）に北畠教謙が廊坊宗賢へ、[[松永久秀]]との合戦における戦功を賞して自分の鎧を与えたものを[[1592年]]（[[天正]]20年）に同社へ奉納したとされる{{国指定文化財等データベース|201|7051|白糸威鎧〈大袖付／〉}}、2019年12月1日閲覧。{{国指定文化財等データベース|201|7047|赤糸威鎧〈大袖付／〉}}、2019年12月1日閲覧。{{国指定文化財等データベース|201|7050|鷹羽威鎧〈大袖付／〉}}、2019年12月1日閲覧。{{国指定文化財等データベース|201|7049|三目札鎧}}、2019年12月1日閲覧。{{国指定文化財等データベース|201|7048|藍韋威肩赤大袖}}、2019年12月1日閲覧。。 == 現地情報 == ;交通アクセス * 最寄駅：[[近鉄大阪線]][[長谷寺駅]]下車後、徒歩約25分 == 脚注 == === 出典 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} ==外部リンク== *[https://www.yokitenmanjinja.jp/ 與喜天満神社（公式サイト）] *{{Commonscat-inline}} {{菅公聖蹟二十五拝}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:よきてんまんしんしや}} [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧郷社]] [[Category:桜井市の歴史]] [[Category:桜井市の建築物]] [[Category:天満宮]]

{{神社 |名称 = 與喜天満神社 |画像 = [[File:Yoki-tenman-jinja honden.jpg|250px]]
本殿 |所在地 = [[奈良県]][[桜井市]][[初瀬]]14 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 32 | 緯度秒 = 05.9 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 54 | 経度秒 = 41.6 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP | 地図 = Japan Nara | ラベル位置 = left |祭神 = [[菅原道真]] |社格 = 旧[[郷社]] |創建 = 946年（天慶9年） |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = |神事 = }} '''與喜天満神社'''（よきてんまんじんじゃ）は、[[奈良県]][[桜井市]][[初瀬]]にある[[神社]]。旧社格は[[郷社]]。 ==祭神== ;主祭神 *[[菅原道真]]（すがわら の みちざね） ;配祀神 *[[天照大神]]（あまてらすおおかみ） *[[大倉姫神]]（おおくらひめのかみ） == 歴史 == 神社の所在地にある[[初瀬]]は、道真の先祖にあたる[[野見宿禰]]の出身地とされており、次いで[[土師氏]]となり、菅原氏にとってゆかりある土地であったとされている[https://sakurai-kankou.jimdo.com/%E5%90%8D%E6%89%80%E6%97%A7%E8%B7%A1/%E5%88%9D%E7%80%AC%E9%81%93%E5%91%A8%E8%BE%BA/%E8%88%87%E5%96%9C%E5%A4%A9%E6%BA%80%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/　與喜天満神社（桜井市 初瀬） - 一般社団法人 桜井市観光協会]2019年4月23日　閲覧。『[[長谷寺験記]]』によると、創建は[[946年]]（[[天慶]]9年）とされており、後に初瀬の産土神としても信仰されるようになった。 == 摂末社 == *瀧蔵権現社 （祭神：[[速玉命]]、[[伊弉諾尊]]、[[伊弉冉尊]]） *白太夫社（祭神：[[白太夫|度会春彦]]） *櫻葉社 （祭神：[[伊予親王]]） *八王子社 （祭神：[[天照大神]]、[[素戔鳴尊]]） == 文化財 == === 重要文化財 === * 木造天神坐像 : 衣冠束帯姿に「怒り天神」とも呼ばれる引き締めたような表情をした[[寄木造]]の坐像であり、像内に「正元元年」(1259年)の銘があることから、現存する国内最古の天神像の彫刻とされている[https://www.nara-np.co.jp/news/20120421085721.html　重文、新たに2件 - 弥勒仏坐像（弥勒寺）、天神坐像（與喜天満） - 奈良新聞]2019年4月23日　閲覧。像の頭部内に十一面観音鏡像（鏡面に[[十一面観音]]像を刻んだ銅鏡）を納めており、天神の本地が十一面観音であることを示している文化庁文化財部「新指定の文化財」『月刊文化財』585号、p.17、第一法規、2012。かつては本殿にて人目に触れぬよう祀られていたが、現在は[[奈良国立博物館]]に寄託されている。 === その他 === [[File:Samurai O-Yoroi 2.jpg|thumb|200px|（参考画像）白糸威鎧（長谷寺所蔵、[[東京国立博物館]]にて展示）。当初は與喜天満神社に伝来した。]] [[長谷寺]]が所蔵する重要文化財の甲冑5点（白糸威鎧（大袖付）・赤糸威鎧（大袖付）・鷹羽威鎧（大袖付）・三目札鎧・藍韋威肩赤大袖）は、かつては同寺の[[鎮守神|鎮守社]]であった與喜天満神社に伝来しており、[[1560年]]（[[永禄]]3年）に北畠教謙が廊坊宗賢へ、[[松永久秀]]との合戦における戦功を賞して自分の鎧を与えたものを[[1592年]]（[[天正]]20年）に同社へ奉納したとされる{{国指定文化財等データベース|201|7051|白糸威鎧〈大袖付／〉}}、2019年12月1日閲覧。{{国指定文化財等データベース|201|7047|赤糸威鎧〈大袖付／〉}}、2019年12月1日閲覧。{{国指定文化財等データベース|201|7050|鷹羽威鎧〈大袖付／〉}}、2019年12月1日閲覧。{{国指定文化財等データベース|201|7049|三目札鎧}}、2019年12月1日閲覧。{{国指定文化財等データベース|201|7048|藍韋威肩赤大袖}}、2019年12月1日閲覧。。 == 現地情報 == ;交通アクセス * 最寄駅：[[近鉄大阪線]][[長谷寺駅]]下車後、徒歩約25分 == 脚注 == === 出典 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} ==外部リンク== {{Commonscat|Yoki-tenman-jinja}} *[https://www.yokitenmanjinja.jp/ 與喜天満神社（公式サイト）] {{菅公聖蹟二十五拝}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:よきてんまんしんしや}} [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧郷社]] [[Category:奈良県の重要文化財]] [[Category:桜井市の歴史]] [[Category:桜井市の建築物]] [[Category:天満宮]]

revision.text:
{{Shinto Shrine |Name= Yoki Tenman Shrine (與喜天満神社) |Image= [[File:Yoki-tenman-jinja honden.jpg|250px]]
Main Hall |Location= [[Sakurai, Nara|桜井市]], [[Nara Prefecture|奈良県]], [[Hase, Sakurai|初瀬]] 14 |Latitude = 34 | LatMinutes = 32 | LatSeconds = 05.9 | DirectionN = N |Longitude = 135 | LongMinutes = 54 | LongSeconds = 41.6 | DirectionE = E |MapCode = JP |Map= Japan Nara |LabelPosition= left |Deity= {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} |ShrineRank= Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |Founded= 946 CE (Tenkei 9) |MainHall= |AlternateName= |AnnualFestival= |Rituals= }} '''Yoki Tenman Shrine''' (與喜天満神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in [[Hase, Sakurai|初瀬]], {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|ja|桜井市}}, [[Nara Prefecture|奈良県]]. Its former rank was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. == Deities == ; Main Enshrined Deity * [[Sugawara no Michizane]] (菅原道真) ; Additional Deities * [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]] (天照大神) * [[Ōkurahime no Kami]] (大倉姫神) == History == The shrine is located in [[Hase, Sakurai|初瀬]], believed to be the birthplace of {{ill|Nomino Sukune|ja|野見宿禰}}, the ancestor of the {{ill|Haji Clan|ja|土師氏}}, later known as the Sugawara Clan. This historical connection made the area significant for the Sugawara family.{{Cite web|url=https://sakurai-kankou.jimdo.com/%E5%90%8D%E6%89%80%E6%97%A7%E8%B7%A1/%E5%88%9D%E7%80%AC%E9%81%93%E5%91%A8%E8%BE%BA/%E8%88%87%E5%96%9C%E5%A4%A9%E6%BA%80%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/|title=Yoki Tenman Shrine (Sakurai, Hase)|publisher=Sakurai City Tourism Association|access-date=April 23, 2019}} According to the ''{{ill|Hasedera Genki|ja|長谷寺験記}}'', the shrine was founded in 946 CE (Tenkei 9) and later became venerated as the guardian shrine of Hase. == Sub-Shrines == * Taki-no-Kura Gongen Shrine (祭神: [[Hayatama-no-Mikoto]], [[Izanagi]], [[Izanami]]) * Shiradaijin Shrine (祭神: {{ill|Watarai Haruhiko|ja|度会春彦}}) * Sakuraha Shrine (祭神: {{ill|Iyo Shinnō|ja|伊予親王}}) * Hachioji Shrine (祭神: [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]) == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Wooden Seated Statue of Tenjin : Known as "Wrathful Tenjin," this seated statue in court attire is made of {{ill|yosegi-zukuri|ja|寄木造}} and features an intense expression. It bears an inscription from Shogen 1 (1259), making it the oldest surviving sculpture of Tenjin in Japan.{{Cite web|url=https://www.nara-np.co.jp/news/20120421085721.html|title=Important Cultural Properties Newly Designated: Tenjin Seated Statue (Yoki Tenman Shrine)|publisher=Nara Newspaper|access-date=April 23, 2019}} Within the statue's head is a mirror engraved with an image of {{ill|Jūichimen Kannon|ja|十一面観音}}, symbolizing Tenjin's connection to the deity.{{Cite journal|title=New Cultural Property Designations|journal=Monthly Cultural Properties|issue=585|page=17|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|year=2012}} Previously enshrined in the main hall, it is now housed in the {{ill|Nara National Museum|ja|奈良国立博物館}}. === Additional Cultural Assets === [[File:Samurai O-Yoroi 2.jpg|thumb|200px|Reference: White-Threaded Yoroi (owned by {{ill|Hasedera|ja|長谷寺}}, displayed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}})]] Five sets of important cultural property armor (White-Threaded Yoroi, Red-Threaded Yoroi, Hawk Feather Yoroi, Three-Eyed Plate Yoroi, Blue-Laced Armor) were once associated with Yoki Tenman Shrine, which served as {{ill|Hasedera|ja|長谷寺}}'s {{ill|Chinjugami|ja|鎮守神}}. These were gifted by {{ill|Kitabatake Norikata|ja|北畠教謙}} in 1560 (Eiroku 3) for services rendered in battles against {{ill|Matsunaga Hisahide|ja|松永久秀}}.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/7051|title=White-Threaded Yoroi (Detailed Listing)|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|access-date=December 1, 2019}}{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/7047|title=Red-Threaded Yoroi (Detailed Listing)|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|access-date=December 1, 2019}} == Access == * Nearest station: 25 minutes on foot from {{ill|Hasedera Station|ja|長谷寺駅}}, {{ill|Kintetsu Osaka Line|ja|近鉄大阪線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yoki-tenman-jinja}} * [https://www.yokitenmanjinja.jp/ Official Website of Yoki Tenman Shrine] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gōsha shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Sakurai, Nara]] [[Category:Tenman-gū]]

revision.text:
{{Shinto Shrine |Name= Yoki Tenman Shrine (與喜天満神社) |Image= [[File:Yoki-tenman-jinja honden.jpg|250px]]
Main Hall |Location= [[Sakurai, Nara|桜井市]], [[Nara Prefecture|奈良県]], [[Hase, Sakurai|初瀬]] 14 |Latitude = 34 | LatMinutes = 32 | LatSeconds = 05.9 | DirectionN = N |Longitude = 135 | LongMinutes = 54 | LongSeconds = 41.6 | DirectionE = E |MapCode = JP |Map= Japan Nara |LabelPosition= left |Deity= {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} |ShrineRank= Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |Founded= 946 CE (Tenkei 9) |MainHall= |AlternateName= |AnnualFestival= |Rituals= }} '''Yoki Tenman Shrine''' (與喜天満神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in [[Hase, Sakurai|初瀬]], {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|ja|桜井市}}, [[Nara Prefecture|奈良県]]. Its former rank was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. == Deities == ; Main Enshrined Deity * [[Sugawara no Michizane]] (菅原道真) ; Additional Deities * [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]] (天照大神) * [[Ōkurahime no Kami]] (大倉姫神) == History == The shrine is located in [[Hase, Sakurai|初瀬]], believed to be the birthplace of {{ill|Nomino Sukune|ja|野見宿禰}}, the ancestor of the {{ill|Haji Clan|ja|土師氏}}, later known as the Sugawara Clan. This historical connection made the area significant for the Sugawara family.{{Cite web|url=https://sakurai-kankou.jimdo.com/%E5%90%8D%E6%89%80%E6%97%A7%E8%B7%A1/%E5%88%9D%E7%80%AC%E9%81%93%E5%91%A8%E8%BE%BA/%E8%88%87%E5%96%9C%E5%A4%A9%E6%BA%80%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/|title=Yoki Tenman Shrine (Sakurai, Hase)|publisher=Sakurai City Tourism Association|access-date=April 23, 2019}} According to the ''{{ill|Hasedera Genki|ja|長谷寺験記}}'', the shrine was founded in 946 CE (Tenkei 9) and later became venerated as the guardian shrine of Hase. == Sub-Shrines == * Taki-no-Kura Gongen Shrine (祭神: [[Hayatama-no-Mikoto]], [[Izanagi]], [[Izanami]]) * Shiradaijin Shrine (祭神: {{ill|Watarai Haruhiko|ja|度会春彦}}) * Sakuraha Shrine (祭神: {{ill|Iyo Shinnō|ja|伊予親王}}) * Hachioji Shrine (祭神: [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]) == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Wooden Seated Statue of Tenjin : Known as "Wrathful Tenjin," this seated statue in court attire is made of {{ill|yosegi-zukuri|ja|寄木造}} and features an intense expression. It bears an inscription from Shogen 1 (1259), making it the oldest surviving sculpture of Tenjin in Japan.{{Cite web|url=https://www.nara-np.co.jp/news/20120421085721.html|title=Important Cultural Properties Newly Designated: Tenjin Seated Statue (Yoki Tenman Shrine)|publisher=Nara Newspaper|access-date=April 23, 2019}} Within the statue's head is a mirror engraved with an image of {{ill|Jūichimen Kannon|ja|十一面観音}}, symbolizing Tenjin's connection to the deity.{{Cite journal|title=New Cultural Property Designations|journal=Monthly Cultural Properties|issue=585|page=17|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|year=2012}} Previously enshrined in the main hall, it is now housed in the {{ill|Nara National Museum|ja|奈良国立博物館}}. === Additional Cultural Assets === [[File:Samurai O-Yoroi 2.jpg|thumb|200px|Reference: White-Threaded Yoroi (owned by {{ill|Hasedera|ja|長谷寺}}, displayed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}})]] Five sets of important cultural property armor (White-Threaded Yoroi, Red-Threaded Yoroi, Hawk Feather Yoroi, Three-Eyed Plate Yoroi, Blue-Laced Armor) were once associated with Yoki Tenman Shrine, which served as {{ill|Hasedera|ja|長谷寺}}'s {{ill|Chinjugami|ja|鎮守神}}. These were gifted by {{ill|Kitabatake Norikata|ja|北畠教謙}} in 1560 (Eiroku 3) for services rendered in battles against {{ill|Matsunaga Hisahide|ja|松永久秀}}.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/7051|title=White-Threaded Yoroi (Detailed Listing)|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|access-date=December 1, 2019}}{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/7047|title=Red-Threaded Yoroi (Detailed Listing)|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|access-date=December 1, 2019}} == Access == * Nearest station: 25 minutes on foot from {{ill|Hasedera Station|ja|長谷寺駅}}, {{ill|Kintetsu Osaka Line|ja|近鉄大阪線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yoki-tenman-jinja}} * [https://www.yokitenmanjinja.jp/ Official Website of Yoki Tenman Shrine] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gōsha shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Sakurai, Nara]] [[Category:Tenman-gū]] [[Category:Original pages]][[ja:與喜天満神社]]

Main Hall |Location= [[Sakurai, Nara|桜井市]], [[Nara Prefecture|奈良県]], [[Hase, Sakurai|初瀬]] 14 |Latitude = 34 | LatMinutes = 32 | LatSeconds = 05.9 | DirectionN = N |Longitude = 135 | LongMinutes = 54 | LongSeconds = 41.6 | DirectionE = E |MapCode = JP |Map= Japan Nara |LabelPosition= left |Deity= {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} |ShrineRank= Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |Founded= 946 CE (Tenkei 9) |MainHall= |AlternateName= |AnnualFestival= |Rituals= }} '''Yoki Tenman Shrine''' (與喜天満神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in [[Hase, Sakurai|初瀬]], {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|ja|桜井市}}, [[Nara Prefecture|奈良県]]. Its former rank was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. == Deities == ; Main Enshrined Deity * [[Sugawara no Michizane]] (菅原道真) ; Additional Deities * [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]] (天照大神) * [[Ōkurahime no Kami]] (大倉姫神) == History == The shrine is located in [[Hase, Sakurai|初瀬]], believed to be the birthplace of {{ill|Nomino Sukune|ja|野見宿禰}}, the ancestor of the {{ill|Haji Clan|ja|土師氏}}, later known as the Sugawara Clan. This historical connection made the area significant for the Sugawara family.{{Cite web|url=https://sakurai-kankou.jimdo.com/%E5%90%8D%E6%89%80%E6%97%A7%E8%B7%A1/%E5%88%9D%E7%80%AC%E9%81%93%E5%91%A8%E8%BE%BA/%E8%88%87%E5%96%9C%E5%A4%A9%E6%BA%80%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/|title=Yoki Tenman Shrine (Sakurai, Hase)|publisher=Sakurai City Tourism Association|access-date=April 23, 2019}} According to the ''{{ill|Hasedera Genki|ja|長谷寺験記}}'', the shrine was founded in 946 CE (Tenkei 9) and later became venerated as the guardian shrine of Hase. == Sub-Shrines == * Taki-no-Kura Gongen Shrine (祭神: [[Hayatama-no-Mikoto]], [[Izanagi]], [[Izanami]]) * Shiradaijin Shrine (祭神: {{ill|Watarai Haruhiko|ja|度会春彦}}) * Sakuraha Shrine (祭神: {{ill|Iyo Shinnō|ja|伊予親王}}) * Hachioji Shrine (祭神: [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]) == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Wooden Seated Statue of Tenjin : Known as "Wrathful Tenjin," this seated statue in court attire is made of {{ill|yosegi-zukuri|ja|寄木造}} and features an intense expression. It bears an inscription from Shogen 1 (1259), making it the oldest surviving sculpture of Tenjin in Japan.{{Cite web|url=https://www.nara-np.co.jp/news/20120421085721.html|title=Important Cultural Properties Newly Designated: Tenjin Seated Statue (Yoki Tenman Shrine)|publisher=Nara Newspaper|access-date=April 23, 2019}} Within the statue's head is a mirror engraved with an image of {{ill|Jūichimen Kannon|ja|十一面観音}}, symbolizing Tenjin's connection to the deity.{{Cite journal|title=New Cultural Property Designations|journal=Monthly Cultural Properties|issue=585|page=17|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|year=2012}} Previously enshrined in the main hall, it is now housed in the {{ill|Nara National Museum|ja|奈良国立博物館}}. === Additional Cultural Assets === [[File:Samurai O-Yoroi 2.jpg|thumb|200px|Reference: White-Threaded Yoroi (owned by {{ill|Hasedera|ja|長谷寺}}, displayed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}})]] Five sets of important cultural property armor (White-Threaded Yoroi, Red-Threaded Yoroi, Hawk Feather Yoroi, Three-Eyed Plate Yoroi, Blue-Laced Armor) were once associated with Yoki Tenman Shrine, which served as {{ill|Hasedera|ja|長谷寺}}'s {{ill|Chinjugami|ja|鎮守神}}. These were gifted by {{ill|Kitabatake Norikata|ja|北畠教謙}} in 1560 (Eiroku 3) for services rendered in battles against {{ill|Matsunaga Hisahide|ja|松永久秀}}.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/7051|title=White-Threaded Yoroi (Detailed Listing)|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|access-date=December 1, 2019}}{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/7047|title=Red-Threaded Yoroi (Detailed Listing)|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|access-date=December 1, 2019}} == Access == * Nearest station: 25 minutes on foot from {{ill|Hasedera Station|ja|長谷寺駅}}, {{ill|Kintetsu Osaka Line|ja|近鉄大阪線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yoki-tenman-jinja}} * Its former rank was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. == Deities == ; Main Enshrined Deity * [[Sugawara no Michizane]] (菅原道真) ; Additional Deities * [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]] (天照大神) * [[Ōkurahime no Kami]] (大倉姫神) == History == The shrine is located in [[Hase, Sakurai|初瀬]], believed to be the birthplace of {{ill|Nomino Sukune|ja|野見宿禰}}, the ancestor of the {{ill|Haji Clan|ja|土師氏}}, later known as the Sugawara Clan. This historical connection made the area significant for the Sugawara family.{{Cite web|url=https://sakurai-kankou.jimdo.com/%E5%90%8D%E6%89%80%E6%97%A7%E8%B7%A1/%E5%88%9D%E7%80%AC%E9%81%93%E5%91%A8%E8%BE%BA/%E8%88%87%E5%96%9C%E5%A4%A9%E6%BA%80%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/|title=Yoki Tenman Shrine (Sakurai, Hase)|publisher=Sakurai City Tourism Association|access-date=April 23, 2019}} According to the ''{{ill|Hasedera Genki|ja|長谷寺験記}}'', the shrine was founded in 946 CE (Tenkei 9) and later became venerated as the guardian shrine of Hase. == Sub-Shrines == * Taki-no-Kura Gongen Shrine (祭神: [[Hayatama-no-Mikoto]], [[Izanagi]], [[Izanami]]) * Shiradaijin Shrine (祭神: {{ill|Watarai Haruhiko|ja|度会春彦}}) * Sakuraha Shrine (祭神: {{ill|Iyo Shinnō|ja|伊予親王}}) * Hachioji Shrine (祭神: [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]) == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Wooden Seated Statue of Tenjin : Known as "Wrathful Tenjin," this seated statue in court attire is made of {{ill|yosegi-zukuri|ja|寄木造}} and features an intense expression. It bears an inscription from Shogen 1 (1259), making it the oldest surviving sculpture of Tenjin in Japan.{{Cite web|url=https://www.nara-np.co.jp/news/20120421085721.html|title=Important Cultural Properties Newly Designated: Tenjin Seated Statue (Yoki Tenman Shrine)|publisher=Nara Newspaper|access-date=April 23, 2019}} Within the statue's head is a mirror engraved with an image of {{ill|Jūichimen Kannon|ja|十一面観音}}, symbolizing Tenjin's connection to the deity.{{Cite journal|title=New Cultural Property Designations|journal=Monthly Cultural Properties|issue=585|page=17|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|year=2012}} Previously enshrined in the main hall, it is now housed in the {{ill|Nara National Museum|ja|奈良国立博物館}}. === Additional Cultural Assets === [[File:Samurai O-Yoroi 2.jpg|thumb|200px|Reference: White-Threaded Yoroi (owned by {{ill|Hasedera|ja|長谷寺}}, displayed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}})]] Five sets of important cultural property armor (White-Threaded Yoroi, Red-Threaded Yoroi, Hawk Feather Yoroi, Three-Eyed Plate Yoroi, Blue-Laced Armor) were once associated with Yoki Tenman Shrine, which served as {{ill|Hasedera|ja|長谷寺}}'s {{ill|Chinjugami|ja|鎮守神}}. These were gifted by {{ill|Kitabatake Norikata|ja|北畠教謙}} in 1560 (Eiroku 3) for services rendered in battles against {{ill|Matsunaga Hisahide|ja|松永久秀}}.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/7051|title=White-Threaded Yoroi (Detailed Listing)|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|access-date=December 1, 2019}}{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/7047|title=Red-Threaded Yoroi (Detailed Listing)|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|access-date=December 1, 2019}} == Access == * Nearest station: 25 minutes on foot from {{ill|Hasedera Station|ja|長谷寺駅}}, {{ill|Kintetsu Osaka Line|ja|近鉄大阪線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yoki-tenman-jinja}} * {{Shinto Shrine |Name= Yoki Tenman Shrine (與喜天満神社) |Image= [[File:Yoki-tenman-jinja honden.jpg|250px]]
Main Hall |Location= [[Sakurai, Nara|桜井市]], [[Nara Prefecture|奈良県]], [[Hase, Sakurai|初瀬]] 14 |Latitude = 34 | LatMinutes = 32 | LatSeconds = 05.9 | DirectionN = N |Longitude = 135 | LongMinutes = 54 | LongSeconds = 41.6 | DirectionE = E |MapCode = JP |Map= Japan Nara |LabelPosition= left |Deity= {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} |ShrineRank= Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |Founded= 946 CE (Tenkei 9) |MainHall= |AlternateName= |AnnualFestival= |Rituals= }} '''Yoki Tenman Shrine''' (與喜天満神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in [[Hase, Sakurai|初瀬]], {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|ja|桜井市}}, [[Nara Prefecture|奈良県]]. Its former rank was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. == Deities == ; Main Enshrined Deity * [[Sugawara no Michizane]] (菅原道真) ; Additional Deities * [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]] (天照大神) * [[Ōkurahime no Kami]] (大倉姫神) == History == The shrine is located in [[Hase, Sakurai|初瀬]], believed to be the birthplace of {{ill|Nomino Sukune|ja|野見宿禰}}, the ancestor of the {{ill|Haji Clan|ja|土師氏}}, later known as the Sugawara Clan. This historical connection made the area significant for the Sugawara family.{{Cite web|url=https://sakurai-kankou.jimdo.com/%E5%90%8D%E6%89%80%E6%97%A7%E8%B7%A1/%E5%88%9D%E7%80%AC%E9%81%93%E5%91%A8%E8%BE%BA/%E8%88%87%E5%96%9C%E5%A4%A9%E6%BA%80%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/|title=Yoki Tenman Shrine (Sakurai, Hase)|publisher=Sakurai City Tourism Association|access-date=April 23, 2019}} According to the ''{{ill|Hasedera Genki|ja|長谷寺験記}}'', the shrine was founded in 946 CE (Tenkei 9) and later became venerated as the guardian shrine of Hase. == Sub-Shrines == * Taki-no-Kura Gongen Shrine (祭神: [[Hayatama-no-Mikoto]], [[Izanagi]], [[Izanami]]) * Shiradaijin Shrine (祭神: {{ill|Watarai Haruhiko|ja|度会春彦}}) * Sakuraha Shrine (祭神: {{ill|Iyo Shinnō|ja|伊予親王}}) * Hachioji Shrine (祭神: [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]) == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Wooden Seated Statue of Tenjin : Known as "Wrathful Tenjin," this seated statue in court attire is made of {{ill|yosegi-zukuri|ja|寄木造}} and features an intense expression. It bears an inscription from Shogen 1 (1259), making it the oldest surviving sculpture of Tenjin in Japan.{{Cite web|url=https://www.nara-np.co.jp/news/20120421085721.html|title=Important Cultural Properties Newly Designated: Tenjin Seated Statue (Yoki Tenman Shrine)|publisher=Nara Newspaper|access-date=April 23, 2019}} Within the statue's head is a mirror engraved with an image of {{ill|Jūichimen Kannon|ja|十一面観音}}, symbolizing Tenjin's connection to the deity.{{Cite journal|title=New Cultural Property Designations|journal=Monthly Cultural Properties|issue=585|page=17|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|year=2012}} Previously enshrined in the main hall, it is now housed in the {{ill|Nara National Museum|ja|奈良国立博物館}}. === Additional Cultural Assets === [[File:Samurai O-Yoroi 2.jpg|thumb|200px|Reference: White-Threaded Yoroi (owned by {{ill|Hasedera|ja|長谷寺}}, displayed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}})]] Five sets of important cultural property armor (White-Threaded Yoroi, Red-Threaded Yoroi, Hawk Feather Yoroi, Three-Eyed Plate Yoroi, Blue-Laced Armor) were once associated with Yoki Tenman Shrine, which served as {{ill|Hasedera|ja|長谷寺}}'s {{ill|Chinjugami|ja|鎮守神}}. These were gifted by {{ill|Kitabatake Norikata|ja|北畠教謙}} in 1560 (Eiroku 3) for services rendered in battles against {{ill|Matsunaga Hisahide|ja|松永久秀}}.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/7051|title=White-Threaded Yoroi (Detailed Listing)|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|access-date=December 1, 2019}}{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/7047|title=Red-Threaded Yoroi (Detailed Listing)|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|access-date=December 1, 2019}} == Access == * Nearest station: 25 minutes on foot from {{ill|Hasedera Station|ja|長谷寺駅}}, {{ill|Kintetsu Osaka Line|ja|近鉄大阪線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yoki-tenman-jinja}} * {{Shinto Shrine |Name= Yoki Tenman Shrine (與喜天満神社) |Image= [[File:Yoki-tenman-jinja honden.jpg|250px]]
Main Hall |Location= [[Sakurai, Nara|桜井市]], [[Nara Prefecture|奈良県]], [[Hase, Sakurai|初瀬]] 14 |Latitude = 34 | LatMinutes = 32 | LatSeconds = 05.9 | DirectionN = N |Longitude = 135 | LongMinutes = 54 | LongSeconds = 41.6 | DirectionE = E |MapCode = JP |Map= Japan Nara |LabelPosition= left |Deity= {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} |ShrineRank= Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |Founded= 946 CE (Tenkei 9) |MainHall= |AlternateName= |AnnualFestival= |Rituals= }} '''Yoki Tenman Shrine''' (與喜天満神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in [[Hase, Sakurai|初瀬]], {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|ja|桜井市}}, [[Nara Prefecture|奈良県]]. Its former rank was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. == Deities == ; Main Enshrined Deity * [[Sugawara no Michizane]] (菅原道真) ; Additional Deities * [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]] (天照大神) * [[Ōkurahime no Kami]] (大倉姫神) == History == The shrine is located in [[Hase, Sakurai|初瀬]], believed to be the birthplace of {{ill|Nomino Sukune|ja|野見宿禰}}, the ancestor of the {{ill|Haji Clan|ja|土師氏}}, later known as the Sugawara Clan. This historical connection made the area significant for the Sugawara family.{{Cite web|url=https://sakurai-kankou.jimdo.com/%E5%90%8D%E6%89%80%E6%97%A7%E8%B7%A1/%E5%88%9D%E7%80%AC%E9%81%93%E5%91%A8%E8%BE%BA/%E8%88%87%E5%96%9C%E5%A4%A9%E6%BA%80%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/|title=Yoki Tenman Shrine (Sakurai, Hase)|publisher=Sakurai City Tourism Association|access-date=April 23, 2019}} According to the ''{{ill|Hasedera Genki|ja|長谷寺験記}}'', the shrine was founded in 946 CE (Tenkei 9) and later became venerated as the guardian shrine of Hase. == Sub-Shrines == * Taki-no-Kura Gongen Shrine (祭神: [[Hayatama-no-Mikoto]], [[Izanagi]], [[Izanami]]) * Shiradaijin Shrine (祭神: {{ill|Watarai Haruhiko|ja|度会春彦}}) * Sakuraha Shrine (祭神: {{ill|Iyo Shinnō|ja|伊予親王}}) * Hachioji Shrine (祭神: [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]) == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Wooden Seated Statue of Tenjin : Known as "Wrathful Tenjin," this seated statue in court attire is made of {{ill|yosegi-zukuri|ja|寄木造}} and features an intense expression. It bears an inscription from Shogen 1 (1259), making it the oldest surviving sculpture of Tenjin in Japan.{{Cite web|url=https://www.nara-np.co.jp/news/20120421085721.html|title=Important Cultural Properties Newly Designated: Tenjin Seated Statue (Yoki Tenman Shrine)|publisher=Nara Newspaper|access-date=April 23, 2019}} Within the statue's head is a mirror engraved with an image of {{ill|Jūichimen Kannon|ja|十一面観音}}, symbolizing Tenjin's connection to the deity.{{Cite journal|title=New Cultural Property Designations|journal=Monthly Cultural Properties|issue=585|page=17|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|year=2012}} Previously enshrined in the main hall, it is now housed in the {{ill|Nara National Museum|ja|奈良国立博物館}}. === Additional Cultural Assets === [[File:Samurai O-Yoroi 2.jpg|thumb|200px|Reference: White-Threaded Yoroi (owned by {{ill|Hasedera|ja|長谷寺}}, displayed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}})]] Five sets of important cultural property armor (White-Threaded Yoroi, Red-Threaded Yoroi, Hawk Feather Yoroi, Three-Eyed Plate Yoroi, Blue-Laced Armor) were once associated with Yoki Tenman Shrine, which served as {{ill|Hasedera|ja|長谷寺}}'s {{ill|Chinjugami|ja|鎮守神}}. These were gifted by {{ill|Kitabatake Norikata|ja|北畠教謙}} in 1560 (Eiroku 3) for services rendered in battles against {{ill|Matsunaga Hisahide|ja|松永久秀}}.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/7051|title=White-Threaded Yoroi (Detailed Listing)|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|access-date=December 1, 2019}}{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/7047|title=Red-Threaded Yoroi (Detailed Listing)|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|access-date=December 1, 2019}} == Access == * Nearest station: 25 minutes on foot from {{ill|Hasedera Station|ja|長谷寺駅}}, {{ill|Kintetsu Osaka Line|ja|近鉄大阪線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yoki-tenman-jinja}} * {{Shinto Shrine |Name= Yoki Tenman Shrine (與喜天満神社) |Image= [[File:Yoki-tenman-jinja honden.jpg|250px]]
Main Hall |Location= [[Sakurai, Nara|桜井市]], [[Nara Prefecture|奈良県]], [[Hase, Sakurai|初瀬]] 14 |Latitude = 34 | LatMinutes = 32 | LatSeconds = 05.9 | DirectionN = N |Longitude = 135 | LongMinutes = 54 | LongSeconds = 41.6 | DirectionE = E |MapCode = JP |Map= Japan Nara |LabelPosition= left |Deity= {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} |ShrineRank= Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |Founded= 946 CE (Tenkei 9) |MainHall= |AlternateName= |AnnualFestival= |Rituals= }} '''Yoki Tenman Shrine''' (與喜天満神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in [[Hase, Sakurai|初瀬]], {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|ja|桜井市}}, [[Nara Prefecture|奈良県]]. Its former rank was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. == Deities == ; Main Enshrined Deity * [[Sugawara no Michizane]] (菅原道真) ; Additional Deities * [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]] (天照大神) * [[Ōkurahime no Kami]] (大倉姫神) == History == The shrine is located in [[Hase, Sakurai|初瀬]], believed to be the birthplace of {{ill|Nomino Sukune|ja|野見宿禰}}, the ancestor of the {{ill|Haji Clan|ja|土師氏}}, later known as the Sugawara Clan. This historical connection made the area significant for the Sugawara family.{{Cite web|url=https://sakurai-kankou.jimdo.com/%E5%90%8D%E6%89%80%E6%97%A7%E8%B7%A1/%E5%88%9D%E7%80%AC%E9%81%93%E5%91%A8%E8%BE%BA/%E8%88%87%E5%96%9C%E5%A4%A9%E6%BA%80%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/|title=Yoki Tenman Shrine (Sakurai, Hase)|publisher=Sakurai City Tourism Association|access-date=April 23, 2019}} According to the ''{{ill|Hasedera Genki|ja|長谷寺験記}}'', the shrine was founded in 946 CE (Tenkei 9) and later became venerated as the guardian shrine of Hase. == Sub-Shrines == * Taki-no-Kura Gongen Shrine (祭神: [[Hayatama-no-Mikoto]], [[Izanagi]], [[Izanami]]) * Shiradaijin Shrine (祭神: {{ill|Watarai Haruhiko|ja|度会春彦}}) * Sakuraha Shrine (祭神: {{ill|Iyo Shinnō|ja|伊予親王}}) * Hachioji Shrine (祭神: [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]) == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Wooden Seated Statue of Tenjin : Known as "Wrathful Tenjin," this seated statue in court attire is made of {{ill|yosegi-zukuri|ja|寄木造}} and features an intense expression. It bears an inscription from Shogen 1 (1259), making it the oldest surviving sculpture of Tenjin in Japan.{{Cite web|url=https://www.nara-np.co.jp/news/20120421085721.html|title=Important Cultural Properties Newly Designated: Tenjin Seated Statue (Yoki Tenman Shrine)|publisher=Nara Newspaper|access-date=April 23, 2019}} Within the statue's head is a mirror engraved with an image of {{ill|Jūichimen Kannon|ja|十一面観音}}, symbolizing Tenjin's connection to the deity.{{Cite journal|title=New Cultural Property Designations|journal=Monthly Cultural Properties|issue=585|page=17|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|year=2012}} Previously enshrined in the main hall, it is now housed in the {{ill|Nara National Museum|ja|奈良国立博物館}}. === Additional Cultural Assets === [[File:Samurai O-Yoroi 2.jpg|thumb|200px|Reference: White-Threaded Yoroi (owned by {{ill|Hasedera|ja|長谷寺}}, displayed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}})]] Five sets of important cultural property armor (White-Threaded Yoroi, Red-Threaded Yoroi, Hawk Feather Yoroi, Three-Eyed Plate Yoroi, Blue-Laced Armor) were once associated with Yoki Tenman Shrine, which served as {{ill|Hasedera|ja|長谷寺}}'s {{ill|Chinjugami|ja|鎮守神}}. These were gifted by {{ill|Kitabatake Norikata|ja|北畠教謙}} in 1560 (Eiroku 3) for services rendered in battles against {{ill|Matsunaga Hisahide|ja|松永久秀}}.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/7051|title=White-Threaded Yoroi (Detailed Listing)|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|access-date=December 1, 2019}}{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/7047|title=Red-Threaded Yoroi (Detailed Listing)|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|access-date=December 1, 2019}} == Access == * Nearest station: 25 minutes on foot from {{ill|Hasedera Station|ja|長谷寺駅}}, {{ill|Kintetsu Osaka Line|ja|近鉄大阪線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yoki-tenman-jinja}} * [https://www.yokitenmanjinja.jp/ Official Website of Yoki Tenman Shrine] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yoiki Tenma Shrine}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:original {{Shinto Shrine |Name= Yoki Tenman Shrine (與喜天満神社) |Image= [[File:Yoki-tenman-jinja honden.jpg|250px]]
Main Hall |Location= [[Sakurai, Nara|桜井市]], [[Nara Prefecture|奈良県]], [[Hase, Sakurai|初瀬]] 14 |Latitude = 34 | LatMinutes = 32 | LatSeconds = 05.9 | DirectionN = N |Longitude = 135 | LongMinutes = 54 | LongSeconds = 41.6 | DirectionE = E |MapCode = JP |Map= Japan Nara |LabelPosition= left |Deity= {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} |ShrineRank= Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |Founded= 946 CE (Tenkei 9) |MainHall= |AlternateName= |AnnualFestival= |Rituals= }} '''Yoki Tenman Shrine''' (與喜天満神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in [[Hase, Sakurai|初瀬]], {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|ja|桜井市}}, [[Nara Prefecture|奈良県]]. Its former rank was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. == Deities == ; Main Enshrined Deity * [[Sugawara no Michizane]] (菅原道真) ; Additional Deities * [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]] (天照大神) * [[Ōkurahime no Kami]] (大倉姫神) == History == The shrine is located in [[Hase, Sakurai|初瀬]], believed to be the birthplace of {{ill|Nomino Sukune|ja|野見宿禰}}, the ancestor of the {{ill|Haji Clan|ja|土師氏}}, later known as the Sugawara Clan. This historical connection made the area significant for the Sugawara family.{{Cite web|url=https://sakurai-kankou.jimdo.com/%E5%90%8D%E6%89%80%E6%97%A7%E8%B7%A1/%E5%88%9D%E7%80%AC%E9%81%93%E5%91%A8%E8%BE%BA/%E8%88%87%E5%96%9C%E5%A4%A9%E6%BA%80%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/|title=Yoki Tenman Shrine (Sakurai, Hase)|publisher=Sakurai City Tourism Association|access-date=April 23, 2019}} According to the ''{{ill|Hasedera Genki|ja|長谷寺験記}}'', the shrine was founded in 946 CE (Tenkei 9) and later became venerated as the guardian shrine of Hase. == Sub-Shrines == * Taki-no-Kura Gongen Shrine (祭神: [[Hayatama-no-Mikoto]], [[Izanagi]], [[Izanami]]) * Shiradaijin Shrine (祭神: {{ill|Watarai Haruhiko|ja|度会春彦}}) * Sakuraha Shrine (祭神: {{ill|Iyo Shinnō|ja|伊予親王}}) * Hachioji Shrine (祭神: [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]) == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Wooden Seated Statue of Tenjin : Known as "Wrathful Tenjin," this seated statue in court attire is made of {{ill|yosegi-zukuri|ja|寄木造}} and features an intense expression. It bears an inscription from Shogen 1 (1259), making it the oldest surviving sculpture of Tenjin in Japan.{{Cite web|url=https://www.nara-np.co.jp/news/20120421085721.html|title=Important Cultural Properties Newly Designated: Tenjin Seated Statue (Yoki Tenman Shrine)|publisher=Nara Newspaper|access-date=April 23, 2019}} Within the statue's head is a mirror engraved with an image of {{ill|Jūichimen Kannon|ja|十一面観音}}, symbolizing Tenjin's connection to the deity.{{Cite journal|title=New Cultural Property Designations|journal=Monthly Cultural Properties|issue=585|page=17|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|year=2012}} Previously enshrined in the main hall, it is now housed in the {{ill|Nara National Museum|ja|奈良国立博物館}}. === Additional Cultural Assets === [[File:Samurai O-Yoroi 2.jpg|thumb|200px|Reference: White-Threaded Yoroi (owned by {{ill|Hasedera|ja|長谷寺}}, displayed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}})]] Five sets of important cultural property armor (White-Threaded Yoroi, Red-Threaded Yoroi, Hawk Feather Yoroi, Three-Eyed Plate Yoroi, Blue-Laced Armor) were once associated with Yoki Tenman Shrine, which served as {{ill|Hasedera|ja|長谷寺}}'s {{ill|Chinjugami|ja|鎮守神}}. These were gifted by {{ill|Kitabatake Norikata|ja|北畠教謙}} in 1560 (Eiroku 3) for services rendered in battles against {{ill|Matsunaga Hisahide|ja|松永久秀}}.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/7051|title=White-Threaded Yoroi (Detailed Listing)|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|access-date=December 1, 2019}}{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/7047|title=Red-Threaded Yoroi (Detailed Listing)|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|access-date=December 1, 2019}} == Access == * Nearest station: 25 minutes on foot from {{ill|Hasedera Station|ja|長谷寺駅}}, {{ill|Kintetsu Osaka Line|ja|近鉄大阪線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yoki-tenman-jinja}} * Main Hall |Location= [[Sakurai, Nara|桜井市]], [[Nara Prefecture|奈良県]], [[Hase, Sakurai|初瀬]] 14 |Latitude = 34 | LatMinutes = 32 | LatSeconds = 05.9 | DirectionN = N |Longitude = 135 | LongMinutes = 54 | LongSeconds = 41.6 | DirectionE = E |MapCode = JP |Map= Japan Nara |LabelPosition= left |Deity= {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} |ShrineRank= Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |Founded= 946 CE (Tenkei 9) |MainHall= |AlternateName= |AnnualFestival= |Rituals= }} '''Yoki Tenman Shrine''' (與喜天満神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in [[Hase, Sakurai|初瀬]], {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|ja|桜井市}}, [[Nara Prefecture|奈良県]]. Its former rank was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. == Deities == ; Main Enshrined Deity * [[Sugawara no Michizane]] (菅原道真) ; Additional Deities * [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]] (天照大神) * [[Ōkurahime no Kami]] (大倉姫神) == History == The shrine is located in [[Hase, Sakurai|初瀬]], believed to be the birthplace of {{ill|Nomino Sukune|ja|野見宿禰}}, the ancestor of the {{ill|Haji Clan|ja|土師氏}}, later known as the Sugawara Clan. This historical connection made the area significant for the Sugawara family.{{Cite web|url=https://sakurai-kankou.jimdo.com/%E5%90%8D%E6%89%80%E6%97%A7%E8%B7%A1/%E5%88%9D%E7%80%AC%E9%81%93%E5%91%A8%E8%BE%BA/%E8%88%87%E5%96%9C%E5%A4%A9%E6%BA%80%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/|title=Yoki Tenman Shrine (Sakurai, Hase)|publisher=Sakurai City Tourism Association|access-date=April 23, 2019}} According to the ''{{ill|Hasedera Genki|ja|長谷寺験記}}'', the shrine was founded in 946 CE (Tenkei 9) and later became venerated as the guardian shrine of Hase. == Sub-Shrines == * Taki-no-Kura Gongen Shrine (祭神: [[Hayatama-no-Mikoto]], [[Izanagi]], [[Izanami]]) * Shiradaijin Shrine (祭神: {{ill|Watarai Haruhiko|ja|度会春彦}}) * Sakuraha Shrine (祭神: {{ill|Iyo Shinnō|ja|伊予親王}}) * Hachioji Shrine (祭神: [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]) == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Wooden Seated Statue of Tenjin : Known as "Wrathful Tenjin," this seated statue in court attire is made of {{ill|yosegi-zukuri|ja|寄木造}} and features an intense expression. It bears an inscription from Shogen 1 (1259), making it the oldest surviving sculpture of Tenjin in Japan.{{Cite web|url=https://www.nara-np.co.jp/news/20120421085721.html|title=Important Cultural Properties Newly Designated: Tenjin Seated Statue (Yoki Tenman Shrine)|publisher=Nara Newspaper|access-date=April 23, 2019}} Within the statue's head is a mirror engraved with an image of {{ill|Jūichimen Kannon|ja|十一面観音}}, symbolizing Tenjin's connection to the deity.{{Cite journal|title=New Cultural Property Designations|journal=Monthly Cultural Properties|issue=585|page=17|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|year=2012}} Previously enshrined in the main hall, it is now housed in the {{ill|Nara National Museum|ja|奈良国立博物館}}. === Additional Cultural Assets === [[File:Samurai O-Yoroi 2.jpg|thumb|200px|Reference: White-Threaded Yoroi (owned by {{ill|Hasedera|ja|長谷寺}}, displayed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}})]] Five sets of important cultural property armor (White-Threaded Yoroi, Red-Threaded Yoroi, Hawk Feather Yoroi, Three-Eyed Plate Yoroi, Blue-Laced Armor) were once associated with Yoki Tenman Shrine, which served as {{ill|Hasedera|ja|長谷寺}}'s {{ill|Chinjugami|ja|鎮守神}}. These were gifted by {{ill|Kitabatake Norikata|ja|北畠教謙}} in 1560 (Eiroku 3) for services rendered in battles against {{ill|Matsunaga Hisahide|ja|松永久秀}}.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/7051|title=White-Threaded Yoroi (Detailed Listing)|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|access-date=December 1, 2019}}{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/7047|title=Red-Threaded Yoroi (Detailed Listing)|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|access-date=December 1, 2019}} == Access == * Nearest station: 25 minutes on foot from {{ill|Hasedera Station|ja|長谷寺駅}}, {{ill|Kintetsu Osaka Line|ja|近鉄大阪線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yoki-tenman-jinja}} * revision.text:
{{Shinto Shrine |Name= Yoki Tenman Shrine (與喜天満神社) |Image= [[File:Yoki-tenman-jinja honden.jpg|250px]]
Main Hall |Location= [[Sakurai, Nara|桜井市]], [[Nara Prefecture|奈良県]], [[Hase, Sakurai|初瀬]] 14 |Latitude = 34 | LatMinutes = 32 | LatSeconds = 05.9 | DirectionN = N |Longitude = 135 | LongMinutes = 54 | LongSeconds = 41.6 | DirectionE = E |MapCode = JP |Map= Japan Nara |LabelPosition= left |Deity= {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} |ShrineRank= Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |Founded= 946 CE (Tenkei 9) |MainHall= |AlternateName= |AnnualFestival= |Rituals= }} '''Yoki Tenman Shrine''' (與喜天満神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in [[Hase, Sakurai|初瀬]], {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|ja|桜井市}}, [[Nara Prefecture|奈良県]]. Its former rank was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. == Deities == ; Main Enshrined Deity * [[Sugawara no Michizane]] (菅原道真) ; Additional Deities * [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]] (天照大神) * [[Ōkurahime no Kami]] (大倉姫神) == History == The shrine is located in [[Hase, Sakurai|初瀬]], believed to be the birthplace of {{ill|Nomino Sukune|ja|野見宿禰}}, the ancestor of the {{ill|Haji Clan|ja|土師氏}}, later known as the Sugawara Clan. This historical connection made the area significant for the Sugawara family.{{Cite web|url=https://sakurai-kankou.jimdo.com/%E5%90%8D%E6%89%80%E6%97%A7%E8%B7%A1/%E5%88%9D%E7%80%AC%E9%81%93%E5%91%A8%E8%BE%BA/%E8%88%87%E5%96%9C%E5%A4%A9%E6%BA%80%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/|title=Yoki Tenman Shrine (Sakurai, Hase)|publisher=Sakurai City Tourism Association|access-date=April 23, 2019}} According to the ''{{ill|Hasedera Genki|ja|長谷寺験記}}'', the shrine was founded in 946 CE (Tenkei 9) and later became venerated as the guardian shrine of Hase. == Sub-Shrines == * Taki-no-Kura Gongen Shrine (祭神: [[Hayatama-no-Mikoto]], [[Izanagi]], [[Izanami]]) * Shiradaijin Shrine (祭神: {{ill|Watarai Haruhiko|ja|度会春彦}}) * Sakuraha Shrine (祭神: {{ill|Iyo Shinnō|ja|伊予親王}}) * Hachioji Shrine (祭神: [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]) == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Wooden Seated Statue of Tenjin : Known as "Wrathful Tenjin," this seated statue in court attire is made of {{ill|yosegi-zukuri|ja|寄木造}} and features an intense expression. It bears an inscription from Shogen 1 (1259), making it the oldest surviving sculpture of Tenjin in Japan.{{Cite web|url=https://www.nara-np.co.jp/news/20120421085721.html|title=Important Cultural Properties Newly Designated: Tenjin Seated Statue (Yoki Tenman Shrine)|publisher=Nara Newspaper|access-date=April 23, 2019}} Within the statue's head is a mirror engraved with an image of {{ill|Jūichimen Kannon|ja|十一面観音}}, symbolizing Tenjin's connection to the deity.{{Cite journal|title=New Cultural Property Designations|journal=Monthly Cultural Properties|issue=585|page=17|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|year=2012}} Previously enshrined in the main hall, it is now housed in the {{ill|Nara National Museum|ja|奈良国立博物館}}. === Additional Cultural Assets === [[File:Samurai O-Yoroi 2.jpg|thumb|200px|Reference: White-Threaded Yoroi (owned by {{ill|Hasedera|ja|長谷寺}}, displayed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}})]] Five sets of important cultural property armor (White-Threaded Yoroi, Red-Threaded Yoroi, Hawk Feather Yoroi, Three-Eyed Plate Yoroi, Blue-Laced Armor) were once associated with Yoki Tenman Shrine, which served as {{ill|Hasedera|ja|長谷寺}}'s {{ill|Chinjugami|ja|鎮守神}}. These were gifted by {{ill|Kitabatake Norikata|ja|北畠教謙}} in 1560 (Eiroku 3) for services rendered in battles against {{ill|Matsunaga Hisahide|ja|松永久秀}}.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/7051|title=White-Threaded Yoroi (Detailed Listing)|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|access-date=December 1, 2019}}{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/7047|title=Red-Threaded Yoroi (Detailed Listing)|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|access-date=December 1, 2019}} == Access == * Nearest station: 25 minutes on foot from {{ill|Hasedera Station|ja|長谷寺駅}}, {{ill|Kintetsu Osaka Line|ja|近鉄大阪線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yoki-tenman-jinja}} * {{Shinto Shrine |Name= Yoki Tenman Shrine (與喜天満神社) |Image= [[File:Yoki-tenman-jinja honden.jpg|250px]]
Main Hall |Location= [[Sakurai, Nara|桜井市]], [[Nara Prefecture|奈良県]], [[Hase, Sakurai|初瀬]] 14 |Latitude = 34 | LatMinutes = 32 | LatSeconds = 05.9 | DirectionN = N |Longitude = 135 | LongMinutes = 54 | LongSeconds = 41.6 | DirectionE = E |MapCode = JP |Map= Japan Nara |LabelPosition= left |Deity= {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} |ShrineRank= Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |Founded= 946 CE (Tenkei 9) |MainHall= |AlternateName= |AnnualFestival= |Rituals= }} '''Yoki Tenman Shrine''' (與喜天満神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in [[Hase, Sakurai|初瀬]], {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|ja|桜井市}}, [[Nara Prefecture|奈良県]]. Its former rank was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. == Deities == ; Main Enshrined Deity * [[Sugawara no Michizane]] (菅原道真) ; Additional Deities * [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]] (天照大神) * [[Ōkurahime no Kami]] (大倉姫神) == History == The shrine is located in [[Hase, Sakurai|初瀬]], believed to be the birthplace of {{ill|Nomino Sukune|ja|野見宿禰}}, the ancestor of the {{ill|Haji Clan|ja|土師氏}}, later known as the Sugawara Clan. This historical connection made the area significant for the Sugawara family.{{Cite web|url=https://sakurai-kankou.jimdo.com/%E5%90%8D%E6%89%80%E6%97%A7%E8%B7%A1/%E5%88%9D%E7%80%AC%E9%81%93%E5%91%A8%E8%BE%BA/%E8%88%87%E5%96%9C%E5%A4%A9%E6%BA%80%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/|title=Yoki Tenman Shrine (Sakurai, Hase)|publisher=Sakurai City Tourism Association|access-date=April 23, 2019}} According to the ''{{ill|Hasedera Genki|ja|長谷寺験記}}'', the shrine was founded in 946 CE (Tenkei 9) and later became venerated as the guardian shrine of Hase. == Sub-Shrines == * Taki-no-Kura Gongen Shrine (祭神: [[Hayatama-no-Mikoto]], [[Izanagi]], [[Izanami]]) * Shiradaijin Shrine (祭神: {{ill|Watarai Haruhiko|ja|度会春彦}}) * Sakuraha Shrine (祭神: {{ill|Iyo Shinnō|ja|伊予親王}}) * Hachioji Shrine (祭神: [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]) == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Wooden Seated Statue of Tenjin : Known as "Wrathful Tenjin," this seated statue in court attire is made of {{ill|yosegi-zukuri|ja|寄木造}} and features an intense expression. It bears an inscription from Shogen 1 (1259), making it the oldest surviving sculpture of Tenjin in Japan.{{Cite web|url=https://www.nara-np.co.jp/news/20120421085721.html|title=Important Cultural Properties Newly Designated: Tenjin Seated Statue (Yoki Tenman Shrine)|publisher=Nara Newspaper|access-date=April 23, 2019}} Within the statue's head is a mirror engraved with an image of {{ill|Jūichimen Kannon|ja|十一面観音}}, symbolizing Tenjin's connection to the deity.{{Cite journal|title=New Cultural Property Designations|journal=Monthly Cultural Properties|issue=585|page=17|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|year=2012}} Previously enshrined in the main hall, it is now housed in the {{ill|Nara National Museum|ja|奈良国立博物館}}. === Additional Cultural Assets === [[File:Samurai O-Yoroi 2.jpg|thumb|200px|Reference: White-Threaded Yoroi (owned by {{ill|Hasedera|ja|長谷寺}}, displayed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}})]] Five sets of important cultural property armor (White-Threaded Yoroi, Red-Threaded Yoroi, Hawk Feather Yoroi, Three-Eyed Plate Yoroi, Blue-Laced Armor) were once associated with Yoki Tenman Shrine, which served as {{ill|Hasedera|ja|長谷寺}}'s {{ill|Chinjugami|ja|鎮守神}}. These were gifted by {{ill|Kitabatake Norikata|ja|北畠教謙}} in 1560 (Eiroku 3) for services rendered in battles against {{ill|Matsunaga Hisahide|ja|松永久秀}}.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/7051|title=White-Threaded Yoroi (Detailed Listing)|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|access-date=December 1, 2019}}{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/7047|title=Red-Threaded Yoroi (Detailed Listing)|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|access-date=December 1, 2019}} == Access == * Nearest station: 25 minutes on foot from {{ill|Hasedera Station|ja|長谷寺駅}}, {{ill|Kintetsu Osaka Line|ja|近鉄大阪線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yoki-tenman-jinja}} * {{Shinto Shrine |Name= Yoki Tenman Shrine (與喜天満神社) |Image= [[File:Yoki-tenman-jinja honden.jpg|250px]]
Main Hall |Location= [[Sakurai, Nara|桜井市]], [[Nara Prefecture|奈良県]], [[Hase, Sakurai|初瀬]] 14 |Latitude = 34 | LatMinutes = 32 | LatSeconds = 05.9 | DirectionN = N |Longitude = 135 | LongMinutes = 54 | LongSeconds = 41.6 | DirectionE = E |MapCode = JP |Map= Japan Nara |LabelPosition= left |Deity= {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} |ShrineRank= Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |Founded= 946 CE (Tenkei 9) |MainHall= |AlternateName= |AnnualFestival= |Rituals= }} '''Yoki Tenman Shrine''' (與喜天満神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in [[Hase, Sakurai|初瀬]], {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|ja|桜井市}}, [[Nara Prefecture|奈良県]]. Its former rank was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. == Deities == ; Main Enshrined Deity * [[Sugawara no Michizane]] (菅原道真) ; Additional Deities * [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]] (天照大神) * [[Ōkurahime no Kami]] (大倉姫神) == History == The shrine is located in [[Hase, Sakurai|初瀬]], believed to be the birthplace of {{ill|Nomino Sukune|ja|野見宿禰}}, the ancestor of the {{ill|Haji Clan|ja|土師氏}}, later known as the Sugawara Clan. This historical connection made the area significant for the Sugawara family.{{Cite web|url=https://sakurai-kankou.jimdo.com/%E5%90%8D%E6%89%80%E6%97%A7%E8%B7%A1/%E5%88%9D%E7%80%AC%E9%81%93%E5%91%A8%E8%BE%BA/%E8%88%87%E5%96%9C%E5%A4%A9%E6%BA%80%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/|title=Yoki Tenman Shrine (Sakurai, Hase)|publisher=Sakurai City Tourism Association|access-date=April 23, 2019}} According to the ''{{ill|Hasedera Genki|ja|長谷寺験記}}'', the shrine was founded in 946 CE (Tenkei 9) and later became venerated as the guardian shrine of Hase. == Sub-Shrines == * Taki-no-Kura Gongen Shrine (祭神: [[Hayatama-no-Mikoto]], [[Izanagi]], [[Izanami]]) * Shiradaijin Shrine (祭神: {{ill|Watarai Haruhiko|ja|度会春彦}}) * Sakuraha Shrine (祭神: {{ill|Iyo Shinnō|ja|伊予親王}}) * Hachioji Shrine (祭神: [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]) == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Wooden Seated Statue of Tenjin : Known as "Wrathful Tenjin," this seated statue in court attire is made of {{ill|yosegi-zukuri|ja|寄木造}} and features an intense expression. It bears an inscription from Shogen 1 (1259), making it the oldest surviving sculpture of Tenjin in Japan.{{Cite web|url=https://www.nara-np.co.jp/news/20120421085721.html|title=Important Cultural Properties Newly Designated: Tenjin Seated Statue (Yoki Tenman Shrine)|publisher=Nara Newspaper|access-date=April 23, 2019}} Within the statue's head is a mirror engraved with an image of {{ill|Jūichimen Kannon|ja|十一面観音}}, symbolizing Tenjin's connection to the deity.{{Cite journal|title=New Cultural Property Designations|journal=Monthly Cultural Properties|issue=585|page=17|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|year=2012}} Previously enshrined in the main hall, it is now housed in the {{ill|Nara National Museum|ja|奈良国立博物館}}. === Additional Cultural Assets === [[File:Samurai O-Yoroi 2.jpg|thumb|200px|Reference: White-Threaded Yoroi (owned by {{ill|Hasedera|ja|長谷寺}}, displayed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}})]] Five sets of important cultural property armor (White-Threaded Yoroi, Red-Threaded Yoroi, Hawk Feather Yoroi, Three-Eyed Plate Yoroi, Blue-Laced Armor) were once associated with Yoki Tenman Shrine, which served as {{ill|Hasedera|ja|長谷寺}}'s {{ill|Chinjugami|ja|鎮守神}}. These were gifted by {{ill|Kitabatake Norikata|ja|北畠教謙}} in 1560 (Eiroku 3) for services rendered in battles against {{ill|Matsunaga Hisahide|ja|松永久秀}}.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/7051|title=White-Threaded Yoroi (Detailed Listing)|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|access-date=December 1, 2019}}{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/7047|title=Red-Threaded Yoroi (Detailed Listing)|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|access-date=December 1, 2019}} == Access == * Nearest station: 25 minutes on foot from {{ill|Hasedera Station|ja|長谷寺駅}}, {{ill|Kintetsu Osaka Line|ja|近鉄大阪線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yoki-tenman-jinja}} * {{Shinto Shrine |Name= Yoki Tenman Shrine (與喜天満神社) |image= [[File:Yoki-tenman-jinja honden.jpg|250px]]
Main Hall |location= [[Sakurai, Nara|桜井市]], [[Nara Prefecture|奈良県]], [[Hase, Sakurai|初瀬]] 14 |Latitude = 34 | LatMinutes = 32 | LatSeconds = 05.9 | DirectionN = N |Longitude = 135 | LongMinutes = 54 | LongSeconds = 41.6 | DirectionE = E |MapCode = JP |Map= Japan Nara |LabelPosition= left |Deity= {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} |ShrineRank= Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |Founded= 946 CE (Tenkei 9) |MainHall= |AlternateName= |AnnualFestival= |Rituals= }} '''Yoki Tenman Shrine''' (與喜天満神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in [[Hase, Sakurai|初瀬]], {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|ja|桜井市}}, [[Nara Prefecture|奈良県]]. Its former rank was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. == Deities == ; Main deity * [[Sugawara no Michizane]] (菅原道真) ; Additional Deities * [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]] (天照大神) * [[Ōkurahime no Kami]] (大倉姫神) == History == The shrine is located in [[Hase, Sakurai|初瀬]], believed to be the birthplace of {{ill|Nomino Sukune|ja|野見宿禰}}, the ancestor of the {{ill|Haji Clan|ja|土師氏}}, later known as the Sugawara Clan. This historical connection made the area significant for the Sugawara family.{{Cite web|url=https://sakurai-kankou.jimdo.com/%E5%90%8D%E6%89%80%E6%97%A7%E8%B7%A1/%E5%88%9D%E7%80%AC%E9%81%93%E5%91%A8%E8%BE%BA/%E8%88%87%E5%96%9C%E5%A4%A9%E6%BA%80%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/|title=Yoki Tenman Shrine (Sakurai, Hase)|publisher=Sakurai City Tourism Association|access-date=April 23, 2019}} According to the ''{{ill|Hasedera Genki|ja|長谷寺験記}}'', the shrine was founded in 946 CE (Tenkei 9) and later became venerated as the guardian shrine of Hase. == Sub-Shrines == * Taki-no-Kura Gongen Shrine (祭神: [[Hayatama-no-Mikoto]], [[Izanagi]], [[Izanami]]) * Shiradaijin Shrine (祭神: {{ill|Watarai Haruhiko|ja|度会春彦}}) * Sakuraha Shrine (祭神: {{ill|Iyo Shinnō|ja|伊予親王}}) * Hachioji Shrine (祭神: [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]) == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Wooden Seated Statue of Tenjin : Known as "Wrathful Tenjin," this seated statue in court attire is made of {{ill|yosegi-zukuri|ja|寄木造}} and features an intense expression. It bears an inscription from Shogen 1 (1259), making it the oldest surviving sculpture of Tenjin in Japan.{{Cite web|url=https://www.nara-np.co.jp/news/20120421085721.html|title=Important Cultural Properties Newly Designated: Tenjin Seated Statue (Yoki Tenman Shrine)|publisher=Nara Newspaper|access-date=April 23, 2019}} Within the statue's head is a mirror engraved with an image of {{ill|Jūichimen Kannon|ja|十一面観音}}, symbolizing Tenjin's connection to the deity.{{Cite journal|title=New Cultural Property Designations|journal=Monthly Cultural Properties|issue=585|page=17|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|year=2012}} Previously enshrined in the main hall, it is now housed in the {{ill|Nara National Museum|ja|奈良国立博物館}}. === Additional Cultural Assets === [[File:Samurai O-Yoroi 2.jpg|thumb|200px|Reference: White-Threaded Yoroi (owned by {{ill|Hasedera|ja|長谷寺}}, displayed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}})]] Five sets of important cultural property armor (White-Threaded Yoroi, Red-Threaded Yoroi, Hawk Feather Yoroi, Three-Eyed Plate Yoroi, Blue-Laced Armor) were once associated with Yoki Tenman Shrine, which served as {{ill|Hasedera|ja|長谷寺}}'s {{ill|Chinjugami|ja|鎮守神}}. These were gifted by {{ill|Kitabatake Norikata|ja|北畠教謙}} in 1560 (Eiroku 3) for services rendered in battles against {{ill|Matsunaga Hisahide|ja|松永久秀}}.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/7051|title=White-Threaded Yoroi (Detailed Listing)|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|access-date=December 1, 2019}}{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/7047|title=Red-Threaded Yoroi (Detailed Listing)|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|access-date=December 1, 2019}} == Access == * Nearest station: 25 minutes on foot from {{ill|Hasedera Station|ja|長谷寺駅}}, {{ill|Kintetsu Osaka Line|ja|近鉄大阪線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yoki-tenman-jinja}} * {{Shinto Shrine |name= Yoki Tenman Shrine (與喜天満神社) |image= [[File:Yoki-tenman-jinja honden.jpg|250px]]
Main Hall |location= [[Sakurai, Nara|桜井市]], [[Nara Prefecture|奈良県]], [[Hase, Sakurai|初瀬]] 14 |Latitude = 34 | LatMinutes = 32 | LatSeconds = 05.9 | DirectionN = N |Longitude = 135 | LongMinutes = 54 | LongSeconds = 41.6 | DirectionE = E |MapCode = JP |Map= Japan Nara |LabelPosition= left |Deity= {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} |ShrineRank= Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |consecration_year= 946 CE (Tenkei 9) |MainHall= |Alternatename= |AnnualFestival= |Rituals= }} '''Yoki Tenman Shrine''' (與喜天満神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in [[Hase, Sakurai|初瀬]], {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|ja|桜井市}}, [[Nara Prefecture|奈良県]]. Its former rank was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. == Deities == ; Main deity * [[Sugawara no Michizane]] (菅原道真) ; Additional Deities * [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]] (天照大神) * [[Ōkurahime no Kami]] (大倉姫神) == History == The shrine is located in [[Hase, Sakurai|初瀬]], believed to be the birthplace of {{ill|Nomino Sukune|ja|野見宿禰}}, the ancestor of the {{ill|Haji Clan|ja|土師氏}}, later known as the Sugawara Clan. This historical connection made the area significant for the Sugawara family.{{Cite web|url=https://sakurai-kankou.jimdo.com/%E5%90%8D%E6%89%80%E6%97%A7%E8%B7%A1/%E5%88%9D%E7%80%AC%E9%81%93%E5%91%A8%E8%BE%BA/%E8%88%87%E5%96%9C%E5%A4%A9%E6%BA%80%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/|title=Yoki Tenman Shrine (Sakurai, Hase)|publisher=Sakurai City Tourism Association|access-date=April 23, 2019}} According to the ''{{ill|Hasedera Genki|ja|長谷寺験記}}'', the shrine was founded in 946 CE (Tenkei 9) and later became venerated as the guardian shrine of Hase. == Sub-Shrines == * Taki-no-Kura Gongen Shrine (祭神: [[Hayatama-no-Mikoto]], [[Izanagi]], [[Izanami]]) * Shiradaijin Shrine (祭神: {{ill|Watarai Haruhiko|ja|度会春彦}}) * Sakuraha Shrine (祭神: {{ill|Iyo Shinnō|ja|伊予親王}}) * Hachioji Shrine (祭神: [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]) == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Wooden Seated Statue of Tenjin : Known as "Wrathful Tenjin," this seated statue in court attire is made of {{ill|yosegi-zukuri|ja|寄木造}} and features an intense expression. It bears an inscription from Shogen 1 (1259), making it the oldest surviving sculpture of Tenjin in Japan.{{Cite web|url=https://www.nara-np.co.jp/news/20120421085721.html|title=Important Cultural Properties Newly Designated: Tenjin Seated Statue (Yoki Tenman Shrine)|publisher=Nara Newspaper|access-date=April 23, 2019}} Within the statue's head is a mirror engraved with an image of {{ill|Jūichimen Kannon|ja|十一面観音}}, symbolizing Tenjin's connection to the deity.{{Cite journal|title=New Cultural Property Designations|journal=Monthly Cultural Properties|issue=585|page=17|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|year=2012}} Previously enshrined in the main hall, it is now housed in the {{ill|Nara National Museum|ja|奈良国立博物館}}. === Additional Cultural Assets === [[File:Samurai O-Yoroi 2.jpg|thumb|200px|Reference: White-Threaded Yoroi (owned by {{ill|Hasedera|ja|長谷寺}}, displayed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}})]] Five sets of important cultural property armor (White-Threaded Yoroi, Red-Threaded Yoroi, Hawk Feather Yoroi, Three-Eyed Plate Yoroi, Blue-Laced Armor) were once associated with Yoki Tenman Shrine, which served as {{ill|Hasedera|ja|長谷寺}}'s {{ill|Chinjugami|ja|鎮守神}}. These were gifted by {{ill|Kitabatake Norikata|ja|北畠教謙}} in 1560 (Eiroku 3) for services rendered in battles against {{ill|Matsunaga Hisahide|ja|松永久秀}}.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/7051|title=White-Threaded Yoroi (Detailed Listing)|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|access-date=December 1, 2019}}{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/7047|title=Red-Threaded Yoroi (Detailed Listing)|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|access-date=December 1, 2019}} == Access == * Nearest station: 25 minutes on foot from {{ill|Hasedera Station|ja|長谷寺駅}}, {{ill|Kintetsu Osaka Line|ja|近鉄大阪線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yoki-tenman-jinja}} * [https://www.yokitenmanjinja.jp/ Official Website of Yoki Tenman Shrine] {{citation needed|Yoiki Tenma Shrine}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] It bears an inscription from Shogen 1 (1259), making it the oldest surviving sculpture of Tenjin in Japan.{{Cite web|url=https://www.nara-np.co.jp/news/20120421085721.html|title=Important Cultural Properties Newly Designated: Tenjin Seated Statue (Yoki Tenman Shrine)|publisher=Nara Newspaper|access-date=April 23, 2019}} Within the statue's head is a mirror engraved with an image of {{ill|Jūichimen Kannon|ja|十一面観音}}, symbolizing Tenjin's connection to the deity.{{Cite journal|title=New Cultural Property Designations|journal=Monthly Cultural Properties|issue=585|page=17|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|year=2012}} Previously enshrined in the main hall, it is now housed in the {{ill|Nara National Museum|ja|奈良国立博物館}}. === Additional Cultural Assets === [[File:Samurai O-Yoroi 2.jpg|thumb|200px|Reference: White-Threaded Yoroi (owned by {{ill|Hasedera|ja|長谷寺}}, displayed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}})]] Five sets of important cultural property armor (White-Threaded Yoroi, Red-Threaded Yoroi, Hawk Feather Yoroi, Three-Eyed Plate Yoroi, Blue-Laced Armor) were once associated with Yoki Tenman Shrine, which served as {{ill|Hasedera|ja|長谷寺}}'s {{ill|Chinjugami|ja|鎮守神}}. These were gifted by {{ill|Kitabatake Norikata|ja|北畠教謙}} in 1560 (Eiroku 3) for services rendered in battles against {{ill|Matsunaga Hisahide|ja|松永久秀}}.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/7051|title=White-Threaded Yoroi (Detailed Listing)|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|access-date=December 1, 2019}}{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/7047|title=Red-Threaded Yoroi (Detailed Listing)|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|access-date=December 1, 2019}} == Access == * Nearest station: 25 minutes on foot from {{ill|Hasedera Station|ja|長谷寺駅}}, {{ill|Kintetsu Osaka Line|ja|近鉄大阪線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yoki-tenman-jinja}} * {{Shinto Shrine |name= Yoki Tenman Shrine (與喜天満神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Yoki-tenman-jinja honden.jpg|250px]]
Main Hall |location= [[Sakurai, Nara|桜井市]], [[Nara Prefecture|奈良県]], {{ill|Hase, Sakurai|ja|初瀬}} 14 |Latitude = 34 | LatMinutes = 32 | LatSeconds = 05.9 | DirectionN = N |Longitude = 135 | LongMinutes = 54 | LongSeconds = 41.6 | DirectionE = E |MapCode = JP |Map= Japan Nara |LabelPosition= left |Deity= {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} |ShrineRank= Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |consecration_year= 946 CE (Tenkei 9) |MainHall= |Alternatename= |AnnualFestival= |Rituals= }} '''Yoki Tenman Shrine''' (與喜天満神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Hase, Sakurai|ja|初瀬}}, {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|ja|桜井市}}, [[Nara Prefecture|奈良県]]. Its former rank was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. == Deities == ; Main deity * [[Sugawara no Michizane]] (菅原道真) ; Additional Deities * [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]] (天照大神) * [[Ōkurahime no Kami]] (大倉姫神) == History == The shrine is located in {{ill|Hase, Sakurai|ja|初瀬}}, believed to be the birthplace of {{ill|Nomino Sukune|ja|野見宿禰}}, the ancestor of the {{ill|Haji clan|ja|土師氏}}, later known as the Sugawara Clan. This historical connection made the area significant for the Sugawara family.{{Cite web|url=https://sakurai-kankou.jimdo.com/%E5%90%8D%E6%89%80%E6%97%A7%E8%B7%A1/%E5%88%9D%E7%80%AC%E9%81%93%E5%91%A8%E8%BE%BA/%E8%88%87%E5%96%9C%E5%A4%A9%E6%BA%80%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/|title=Yoki Tenman Shrine (Sakurai, Hase)|publisher=Sakurai City Tourism Association|access-date=April 23, 2019}} According to the ''{{ill|Hasedera Genki|ja|長谷寺験記}}'', the shrine was founded in 946 CE (Tenkei 9) and later became venerated as the guardian shrine of Hase. == Sub-Shrines == * Taki-no-Kura Gongen Shrine (祭神: [[Hayatama-no-Mikoto]], [[Izanagi]], [[Izanami]]) * Shiradaijin Shrine (祭神: {{ill|Watarai Haruhiko|ja|度会春彦}}) * Sakuraha Shrine (祭神: {{ill|Iyo Shinnō|ja|伊予親王}}) * Hachioji Shrine (祭神: [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]) == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Wooden Seated Statue of Tenjin : Known as "Wrathful Tenjin," this seated statue in court attire is made of {{ill|yosegi-zukuri|ja|寄木造}} and features an intense expression. It bears an inscription from Shogen 1 (1259), making it the oldest surviving sculpture of Tenjin in Japan.{{Cite web|url=https://www.nara-np.co.jp/news/20120421085721.html|title=Important Cultural Properties Newly Designated: Tenjin Seated Statue (Yoki Tenman Shrine)|publisher=Nara Newspaper|access-date=April 23, 2019}} Within the statue's head is a mirror engraved with an image of {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|ja|十一面観音|Ekādaśamukha=Ekādaśamukha|lt=Jūichimen Kannon}}, symbolizing Tenjin's connection to the deity.{{Cite journal|title=New Cultural Property Designations|journal=Monthly Cultural Properties|issue=585|page=17|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|year=2012}} Previously enshrined in the main hall, it is now housed in the {{ill|Nara National Museum|ja|奈良国立博物館}}. === Additional Cultural Assets === [[File:Samurai O-Yoroi 2.jpg|thumb|200px|Reference: White-Threaded Yoroi (owned by {{ill|Hasedera|ja|長谷寺}}, displayed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}})]] Five sets of important cultural property armor (White-Threaded Yoroi, Red-Threaded Yoroi, Hawk Feather Yoroi, Three-Eyed Plate Yoroi, Blue-Laced Armor) were once associated with Yoki Tenman Shrine, which served as {{ill|Hasedera|ja|長谷寺}}'s {{ill|Chinjugami|ja|鎮守神}}. These were gifted by {{ill|Kitabatake Norikata|ja|北畠教謙}} in 1560 (Eiroku 3) for services rendered in battles against {{ill|Matsunaga Hisahide|ja|松永久秀}}.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/7051|title=White-Threaded Yoroi (Detailed Listing)|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|access-date=December 1, 2019}}{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/7047|title=Red-Threaded Yoroi (Detailed Listing)|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|access-date=December 1, 2019}} == Access == * Nearest station: 25 minutes on foot from {{ill|Hasedera Station|ja|長谷寺駅}}, {{ill|Kintetsu Osaka Line|ja|近鉄大阪線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yoki-tenman-jinja}} * {{Shinto Shrine |name= Yoki Tenman Shrine (與喜天満神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Yoki-tenman-jinja honden.jpg|250px]]
Main Hall |location= [[Sakurai, Nara|桜井市]], [[Nara Prefecture|奈良県]], {{ill|Hase, Sakurai|ja|初瀬}} 14 |Latitude = 34 | LatMinutes = 32 | LatSeconds = 05.9 | DirectionN = N |Longitude = 135 | LongMinutes = 54 | LongSeconds = 41.6 | DirectionE = E |MapCode = JP |Map= Japan Nara |LabelPosition= left |Deity= {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} |ShrineRank= Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |consecration_year= 946 CE (Tenkei 9) |MainHall= |Alternatename= |AnnualFestival= |Rituals= }} '''Yoki Tenman Shrine''' (與喜天満神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Hase, Sakurai|ja|初瀬}}, {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|ja|桜井市}}, [[Nara Prefecture|奈良県]]. Its former rank was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. == Deities == ; Main deity * [[Sugawara no Michizane]] (菅原道真) ; Additional Deities * [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]] (天照大神) * [[Ōkurahime no Kami]] (大倉姫神) == History == The shrine is located in {{ill|Hase, Sakurai|ja|初瀬}}, believed to be the birthplace of {{ill|Nomino Sukune|ja|野見宿禰}}, the ancestor of the {{ill|Haji clan|ja|土師氏}}, later known as the Sugawara Clan. This historical connection made the area significant for the Sugawara family.{{Cite web|url=https://sakurai-kankou.jimdo.com/%E5%90%8D%E6%89%80%E6%97%A7%E8%B7%A1/%E5%88%9D%E7%80%AC%E9%81%93%E5%91%A8%E8%BE%BA/%E8%88%87%E5%96%9C%E5%A4%A9%E6%BA%80%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/|title=Yoki Tenman Shrine (Sakurai, Hase)|publisher=Sakurai City Tourism Association|access-date=April 23, 2019}} According to the ''{{ill|Hasedera Genki|ja|長谷寺験記}}'', the shrine was founded in 946 CE (Tenkei 9) and later became venerated as the guardian shrine of Hase. == Sub-Shrines == * Taki-no-Kura Gongen Shrine (祭神: [[Hayatama-no-Mikoto]], [[Izanagi]], [[Izanami]]) * Shiradaijin Shrine (祭神: {{ill|Watarai Haruhiko|ja|度会春彦}}) * Sakuraha Shrine (祭神: {{ill|Iyo Shinnō|ja|伊予親王}}) * Hachioji Shrine (祭神: [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]) == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Wooden Seated Statue of Tenjin : Known as "Wrathful Tenjin," this seated statue in court attire is made of {{ill|yosegi-zukuri|ja|寄木造}} and features an intense expression. It bears an inscription from Shogen 1 (1259), making it the oldest surviving sculpture of Tenjin in Japan.{{Cite web|url=https://www.nara-np.co.jp/news/20120421085721.html|title=Important Cultural Properties Newly Designated: Tenjin Seated Statue (Yoki Tenman Shrine)|publisher=Nara Newspaper|access-date=April 23, 2019}} Within the statue's head is a mirror engraved with an image of {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|ja|十一面観音|Ekādaśamukha=Ekādaśamukha|lt=Jūichimen Kannon}}, symbolizing Tenjin's connection to the deity.{{Cite journal|title=New Cultural Property Designations|journal=Monthly Cultural Properties|issue=585|page=17|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|year=2012}} Previously enshrined in the main hall, it is now housed in the {{ill|Nara National Museum|ja|奈良国立博物館}}. === Additional Cultural Assets === [[File:Samurai O-Yoroi 2.jpg|thumb|200px|Reference: White-Threaded Yoroi (owned by {{ill|Hasedera|ja|長谷寺}}, displayed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}})]] Five sets of important cultural property armor (White-Threaded Yoroi, Red-Threaded Yoroi, Hawk Feather Yoroi, Three-Eyed Plate Yoroi, Blue-Laced Armor) were once associated with Yoki Tenman Shrine, which served as {{ill|Hasedera|ja|長谷寺}}'s {{ill|Chinjugami|ja|鎮守神}}. These were gifted by {{ill|Kitabatake Norikata|ja|北畠教謙}} in 1560 (Eiroku 3) for services rendered in battles against {{ill|Matsunaga Hisahide|ja|松永久秀}}.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/7051|title=White-Threaded Yoroi (Detailed Listing)|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|access-date=December 1, 2019}}{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/7047|title=Red-Threaded Yoroi (Detailed Listing)|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|access-date=December 1, 2019}} == Access == * Nearest station: 25 minutes on foot from {{ill|Hasedera Station|ja|長谷寺駅}}, {{ill|Kintetsu Osaka Line|ja|近鉄大阪線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yoki-tenman-jinja}} * [https://www.yokitenmanjinja.jp/ Official Website of Yoki Tenman Shrine] {{citation needed|Yoiki Tenma Shrine}} [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Yoiki Tenma Its former rank was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. == Deities == ; Main deity * [[Sugawara no Michizane]] (菅原道真) ; Additional Deities * [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]] (天照大神) * [[Ōkurahime no Kami]] (大倉姫神) == History == The shrine is located in {{ill|Hase, Sakurai|ja|初瀬}}, believed to be the birthplace of {{ill|Nomino Sukune|ja|野見宿禰}}, the ancestor of the {{ill|Haji clan|ja|土師氏}}, later known as the Sugawara Clan. It bears an inscription from Shogen 1 (1259), making it the oldest surviving sculpture of Tenjin in Japan.{{Cite web|url=https://www.nara-np.co.jp/news/20120421085721.html|title=Important Cultural Properties Newly Designated: Tenjin Seated Statue (Yoki Tenman Shrine)|publisher=Nara Newspaper|access-date=April 23, 2019}} Within the statue's head is a mirror engraved with an image of {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|ja|十一面観音|Ekādaśamukha=Ekādaśamukha|lt=Jūichimen Kannon}}, symbolizing Tenjin's connection to the deity.{{Cite journal|title=New Cultural Property Designations|journal=Monthly Cultural Properties|issue=585|page=17|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|year=2012}} Previously enshrined in the main hall, it is now housed in the {{ill|Nara National Museum|ja|奈良国立博物館}}. === Additional Cultural Assets === [[File:Samurai O-Yoroi 2.jpg|thumb|200px|Reference: White-Threaded Yoroi (owned by {{ill|Hasedera|ja|長谷寺}}, displayed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}})]] Five sets of important cultural property armor (White-Threaded Yoroi, Red-Threaded Yoroi, Hawk Feather Yoroi, Three-Eyed Plate Yoroi, Blue-Laced Armor) were once associated with Yoki Tenman Shrine, which served as {{ill|Hasedera|ja|長谷寺}}'s {{ill|Chinjugami|ja|鎮守神}}. These were gifted by {{ill|Kitabatake Norikata|ja|北畠教謙}} in 1560 (Eiroku 3) for services rendered in battles against {{ill|Matsunaga Hisahide|ja|松永久秀}}.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/7051|title=White-Threaded Yoroi (Detailed Listing)|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|access-date=December 1, 2019}}{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/7047|title=Red-Threaded Yoroi (Detailed Listing)|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|access-date=December 1, 2019}} == Access == * Nearest station: 25 minutes on foot from {{ill|Hasedera Station|ja|長谷寺駅}}, {{ill|Kintetsu Osaka Line|ja|近鉄大阪線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yoki-tenman-jinja}} *
