revision.text:
{{Shinto Shrine |name= Yoki Tenman Shrine (與喜天満神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Yoki-tenman-jinja honden.jpg|250px]]
Main Hall |location= [[Sakurai, Nara|桜井市]], [[Nara Prefecture|奈良県]], {{ill|Hase, Sakurai|ja|初瀬}} 14 |Latitude = 34 | LatMinutes = 32 | LatSeconds = 05.9 | DirectionN = N |Longitude = 135 | LongMinutes = 54 | LongSeconds = 41.6 | DirectionE = E |MapCode = JP |Map= Japan Nara |LabelPosition= left |deity= {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} |ShrineRank= Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |consecration_year= 946 CE (Tenkei 9) |MainHall= |Alternatename= |AnnualFestival= |Rituals= }} '''Yoki Tenman Shrine''' (與喜天満神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Hase, Sakurai|ja|初瀬}}, {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|ja|桜井市}}, [[Nara Prefecture|奈良県]]. Its former rank was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. == Deities == ; Main deity * [[Sugawara no Michizane]] (菅原道真) ; Additional Deities * [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]] (天照大神) * [[Ōkurahime no Kami]] (大倉姫神) == History == The shrine is located in {{ill|Hase, Sakurai|ja|初瀬}}, believed to be the birthplace of {{ill|Nomino Sukune|ja|野見宿禰}}, the ancestor of the {{ill|Haji clan|ja|土師氏}}, later known as the Sugawara Clan. This historical connection made the area significant for the Sugawara family.{{Cite web|url=https://sakurai-kankou.jimdo.com/%E5%90%8D%E6%89%80%E6%97%A7%E8%B7%A1/%E5%88%9D%E7%80%AC%E9%81%93%E5%91%A8%E8%BE%BA/%E8%88%87%E5%96%9C%E5%A4%A9%E6%BA%80%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/|title=Yoki Tenman Shrine (Sakurai, Hase)|publisher=Sakurai City Tourism Association|access-date=April 23, 2019}} According to the ''{{ill|Hasedera Genki|ja|長谷寺験記}}'', the shrine was founded in 946 CE (Tenkei 9) and later became venerated as the guardian shrine of Hase. == Sub-Shrines == * Taki-no-Kura Gongen Shrine (deity: [[Hayatama-no-Mikoto]], [[Izanagi]], [[Izanami]]) * Shiradaijin Shrine (deity: {{ill|Watarai Haruhiko|ja|度会春彦}}) * Sakuraha Shrine (deity: {{ill|Iyo Shinnō|ja|伊予親王}}) * Hachioji Shrine (deity: [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]) == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Wooden Seated Statue of Tenjin : Known as "Wrathful Tenjin," this seated statue in court attire is made of {{ill|yosegi-zukuri|ja|寄木造}} and features an intense expression. It bears an inscription from Shogen 1 (1259), making it the oldest surviving sculpture of Tenjin in Japan.{{Cite web|url=https://www.nara-np.co.jp/news/20120421085721.html|title=Important Cultural Properties Newly Designated: Tenjin Seated Statue (Yoki Tenman Shrine)|publisher=Nara Newspaper|access-date=April 23, 2019}} Within the statue's head is a mirror engraved with an image of {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|ja|十一面観音|Ekādaśamukha=Ekādaśamukha|lt=Jūichimen Kannon}}, symbolizing Tenjin's connection to the deity.{{Cite journal|title=New Cultural Property Designations|journal=Monthly Cultural Properties|issue=585|page=17|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|year=2012}} Previously enshrined in the main hall, it is now housed in the {{ill|Nara National Museum|ja|奈良国立博物館}}. === Additional Cultural Assets === [[File:Samurai O-Yoroi 2.jpg|thumb|200px|Reference: White-Threaded Yoroi (owned by {{ill|Hasedera|ja|長谷寺}}, displayed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}})]] Five sets of important cultural property armor (White-Threaded Yoroi, Red-Threaded Yoroi, Hawk Feather Yoroi, Three-Eyed Plate Yoroi, Blue-Laced Armor) were once associated with Yoki Tenman Shrine, which served as {{ill|Hasedera|ja|長谷寺}}'s {{ill|Chinjugami|ja|鎮守神}}. These were gifted by {{ill|Kitabatake Norikata|ja|北畠教謙}} in 1560 (Eiroku 3) for services rendered in battles against {{ill|Matsunaga Hisahide|ja|松永久秀}}.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/7051|title=White-Threaded Yoroi (Detailed Listing)|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|access-date=December 1, 2019}}{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/7047|title=Red-Threaded Yoroi (Detailed Listing)|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|access-date=December 1, 2019}} == Access == * Nearest station: 25 minutes on foot from {{ill|Hasedera Station|ja|長谷寺駅}}, {{ill|Kintetsu Osaka Line|ja|近鉄大阪線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yoki-tenman-jinja}} * [https://www.yokitenmanjinja.jp/ Official Website of Yoki Tenman Shrine] {{citation needed|Yoiki Tenma Shrine}} [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Yoiki Tenma It bears an inscription from Shogen 1 (1259), making it the oldest surviving sculpture of Tenjin in Japan.{{Cite web|url=https://www.nara-np.co.jp/news/20120421085721.html|title=Important Cultural Properties Newly Designated: Tenjin Seated Statue (Yoki Tenman Shrine)|publisher=Nara Newspaper|access-date=April 23, 2019}} Within the statue's head is a mirror engraved with an image of {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|ja|十一面観音|Ekādaśamukha=Ekādaśamukha|lt=Jūichimen Kannon}}, symbolizing Tenjin's connection to the deity.{{Cite journal|title=New Cultural Property Designations|journal=Monthly Cultural Properties|issue=585|page=17|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|year=2012}} Previously enshrined in the main hall, it is now housed in the {{ill|Nara National Museum|ja|奈良国立博物館}}. === Additional Cultural Assets === [[File:Samurai O-Yoroi 2.jpg|thumb|200px|Reference: White-Threaded Yoroi (owned by {{ill|Hasedera|ja|長谷寺}}, displayed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}})]] Five sets of important cultural property armor (White-Threaded Yoroi, Red-Threaded Yoroi, Hawk Feather Yoroi, Three-Eyed Plate Yoroi, Blue-Laced Armor) were once associated with Yoki Tenman Shrine, which served as {{ill|Hasedera|ja|長谷寺}}'s {{ill|Chinjugami|ja|鎮守神}}. These were gifted by {{ill|Kitabatake Norikata|ja|北畠教謙}} in 1560 (Eiroku 3) for services rendered in battles against {{ill|Matsunaga Hisahide|ja|松永久秀}}.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/7051|title=White-Threaded Yoroi (Detailed Listing)|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|access-date=December 1, 2019}}{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/7047|title=Red-Threaded Yoroi (Detailed Listing)|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|access-date=December 1, 2019}} == Access == * Nearest station: 25 minutes on foot from {{ill|Hasedera Station|ja|長谷寺駅}}, {{ill|Kintetsu Osaka Line|ja|近鉄大阪線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yoki-tenman-jinja}} * {{Shinto Shrine |name= Yoki Tenman Shrine (與喜天満神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Yoki-tenman-jinja honden.jpg|250px]]
Main Hall |location= [[Sakurai, Nara|桜井市]], [[Nara Prefecture|奈良県]], {{ill|Hase, Sakurai|ja|初瀬}} 14 |Latitude = 34 | LatMinutes = 32 | LatSeconds = 05.9 | DirectionN = N |Longitude = 135 | LongMinutes = 54 | LongSeconds = 41.6 | DirectionE = E |MapCode = JP |Map= Japan Nara |LabelPosition= left |deity= {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} |ShrineRank= Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |consecration_year= 946 CE (Tenkei 9) |MainHall= |Alternatename= |AnnualFestival= |Rituals= }} '''Yoki Tenman Shrine''' (與喜天満神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Hase, Sakurai|ja|初瀬}}, {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|ja|桜井市}}, [[Nara Prefecture|奈良県]]. Its former rank was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. == Deities == ; Main deity * [[Sugawara no Michizane]] (菅原道真) ; Additional Deities * [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]] (天照大神) * [[Ōkurahime no Kami]] (大倉姫神) == History == The shrine is located in {{ill|Hase, Sakurai|ja|初瀬}}, believed to be the birthplace of {{ill|Nomino Sukune|ja|野見宿禰}}, the ancestor of the {{ill|Haji clan|ja|土師氏}}, later known as the Sugawara Clan. This historical connection made the area significant for the Sugawara family.{{Cite web|url=https://sakurai-kankou.jimdo.com/%E5%90%8D%E6%89%80%E6%97%A7%E8%B7%A1/%E5%88%9D%E7%80%AC%E9%81%93%E5%91%A8%E8%BE%BA/%E8%88%87%E5%96%9C%E5%A4%A9%E6%BA%80%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/|title=Yoki Tenman Shrine (Sakurai, Hase)|publisher=Sakurai City Tourism Association|access-date=April 23, 2019}} According to the ''{{ill|Hasedera Genki|ja|長谷寺験記}}'', the shrine was founded in 946 CE (Tenkei 9) and later became venerated as the guardian shrine of Hase. == Sub-Shrines == * Taki-no-Kura Gongen Shrine (deity: [[Hayatama-no-Mikoto]], [[Izanagi]], [[Izanami]]) * Shiradaijin Shrine (deity: {{ill|Watarai Haruhiko|ja|度会春彦}}) * Sakuraha Shrine (deity: {{ill|Iyo Shinnō|ja|伊予親王}}) * Hachioji Shrine (deity: [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]) == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Wooden Seated Statue of Tenjin : Known as "Wrathful Tenjin," this seated statue in court attire is made of {{ill|yosegi-zukuri|ja|寄木造}} and features an intense expression. It bears an inscription from Shogen 1 (1259), making it the oldest surviving sculpture of Tenjin in Japan.{{Cite web|url=https://www.nara-np.co.jp/news/20120421085721.html|title=Important Cultural Properties Newly Designated: Tenjin Seated Statue (Yoki Tenman Shrine)|publisher=Nara Newspaper|access-date=April 23, 2019}} Within the statue's head is a mirror engraved with an image of {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|ja|十一面観音|Ekādaśamukha=Ekādaśamukha|lt=Jūichimen Kannon}}, symbolizing Tenjin's connection to the deity.{{Cite journal|title=New Cultural Property Designations|journal=Monthly Cultural Properties|issue=585|page=17|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|year=2012}} Previously enshrined in the main hall, it is now housed in the {{ill|Nara National Museum|ja|奈良国立博物館}}. === Additional Cultural Assets === [[File:Samurai O-Yoroi 2.jpg|thumb|200px|Reference: White-Threaded Yoroi (owned by {{ill|Hasedera|ja|長谷寺}}, displayed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}})]] Five sets of important cultural property armor (White-Threaded Yoroi, Red-Threaded Yoroi, Hawk Feather Yoroi, Three-Eyed Plate Yoroi, Blue-Laced Armor) were once associated with Yoki Tenman Shrine, which served as {{ill|Hasedera|ja|長谷寺}}'s {{ill|Chinjugami|ja|鎮守神}}. These were gifted by {{ill|Kitabatake Norikata|ja|北畠教謙}} in 1560 (Eiroku 3) for services rendered in battles against {{ill|Matsunaga Hisahide|ja|松永久秀}}.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/7051|title=White-Threaded Yoroi (Detailed Listing)|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|access-date=December 1, 2019}}{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/7047|title=Red-Threaded Yoroi (Detailed Listing)|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|access-date=December 1, 2019}} == Access == * Nearest station: 25 minutes on foot from {{ill|Hasedera Station|ja|長谷寺駅}}, {{ill|Kintetsu Osaka Line|ja|近鉄大阪線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yoki-tenman-jinja}} * {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Yoki Tenman Shrine (與喜天満神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Yoki-tenman-jinja honden.jpg|250px]]
Main Hall |location= [[Sakurai, Nara|桜井市]], [[Nara Prefecture|奈良県]], {{ill|Hase, Sakurai|ja|初瀬}} 14 |Latitude = 34 | LatMinutes = 32 | LatSeconds = 05.9 | DirectionN = N |Longitude = 135 | LongMinutes = 54 | LongSeconds = 41.6 | DirectionE = E |MapCode = JP |Map= Japan Nara |LabelPosition= left |deity= {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} |ShrineRank= Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |consecration_year= 946 CE (Tenkei 9) |MainHall= |Alternatename= |AnnualFestival= |Rituals= }} '''Yoki Tenman Shrine''' (與喜天満神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Hase, Sakurai|ja|初瀬}}, {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|ja|桜井市}}, [[Nara Prefecture|奈良県]]. Its former rank was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. == Deities == ; Main deity * [[Sugawara no Michizane]] (菅原道真) ; Additional Deities * [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]] (天照大神) * [[Ōkurahime no Kami]] (大倉姫神) == History == The shrine is located in {{ill|Hase, Sakurai|ja|初瀬}}, believed to be the birthplace of {{ill|Nomino Sukune|ja|野見宿禰}}, the ancestor of the {{ill|Haji clan|ja|土師氏}}, later known as the Sugawara Clan. This historical connection made the area significant for the Sugawara family.{{Cite web|url=https://sakurai-kankou.jimdo.com/%E5%90%8D%E6%89%80%E6%97%A7%E8%B7%A1/%E5%88%9D%E7%80%AC%E9%81%93%E5%91%A8%E8%BE%BA/%E8%88%87%E5%96%9C%E5%A4%A9%E6%BA%80%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/|title=Yoki Tenman Shrine (Sakurai, Hase)|publisher=Sakurai City Tourism Association|access-date=April 23, 2019}} According to the ''{{ill|Hasedera Genki|ja|長谷寺験記}}'', the shrine was founded in 946 CE (Tenkei 9) and later became venerated as the guardian shrine of Hase. == Sub-Shrines == * Taki-no-Kura Gongen Shrine (deity: [[Hayatama-no-Mikoto]], [[Izanagi]], [[Izanami]]) * Shiradaijin Shrine (deity: {{ill|Watarai Haruhiko|ja|度会春彦}}) * Sakuraha Shrine (deity: {{ill|Iyo Shinnō|ja|伊予親王}}) * Hachioji Shrine (deity: [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]) == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Wooden Seated Statue of Tenjin : Known as "Wrathful Tenjin," this seated statue in court attire is made of {{ill|yosegi-zukuri|ja|寄木造}} and features an intense expression. It bears an inscription from Shogen 1 (1259), making it the oldest surviving sculpture of Tenjin in Japan.{{Cite web|url=https://www.nara-np.co.jp/news/20120421085721.html|title=Important Cultural Properties Newly Designated: Tenjin Seated Statue (Yoki Tenman Shrine)|publisher=Nara Newspaper|access-date=April 23, 2019}} Within the statue's head is a mirror engraved with an image of {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|ja|十一面観音|Ekādaśamukha=Ekādaśamukha|lt=Jūichimen Kannon}}, symbolizing Tenjin's connection to the deity.{{Cite journal|title=New Cultural Property Designations|journal=Monthly Cultural Properties|issue=585|page=17|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|year=2012}} Previously enshrined in the main hall, it is now housed in the {{ill|Nara National Museum|ja|奈良国立博物館}}. === Additional Cultural Assets === [[File:Samurai O-Yoroi 2.jpg|thumb|200px|Reference: White-Threaded Yoroi (owned by {{ill|Hasedera|ja|長谷寺}}, displayed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}})]] Five sets of important cultural property armor (White-Threaded Yoroi, Red-Threaded Yoroi, Hawk Feather Yoroi, Three-Eyed Plate Yoroi, Blue-Laced Armor) were once associated with Yoki Tenman Shrine, which served as {{ill|Hasedera|ja|長谷寺}}'s {{ill|Chinjugami|ja|鎮守神}}. These were gifted by {{ill|Kitabatake Norikata|ja|北畠教謙}} in 1560 (Eiroku 3) for services rendered in battles against {{ill|Matsunaga Hisahide|ja|松永久秀}}.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/7051|title=White-Threaded Yoroi (Detailed Listing)|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|access-date=December 1, 2019}}{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/7047|title=Red-Threaded Yoroi (Detailed Listing)|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|access-date=December 1, 2019}} == Access == * Nearest station: 25 minutes on foot from {{ill|Hasedera Station|ja|長谷寺駅}}, {{ill|Kintetsu Osaka Line|ja|近鉄大阪線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yoki-tenman-jinja}} * Its former rank was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. == Deities == ; Main deity * [[Sugawara no Michizane]] (菅原道真) ; Additional Deities * [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]] (天照大神) * [[Ōkurahime no Kami]] (大倉姫神) == History == The shrine is located in {{ill|Hase, Sakurai|ja|初瀬}}, believed to be the birthplace of {{ill|Nomino Sukune|ja|野見宿禰}}, the ancestor of the {{ill|Haji clan|ja|土師氏}}, later known as the Sugawara Clan. This historical connection made the area significant for the Sugawara family.{{Cite web|url=https://sakurai-kankou.jimdo.com/%E5%90%8D%E6%89%80%E6%97%A7%E8%B7%A1/%E5%88%9D%E7%80%AC%E9%81%93%E5%91%A8%E8%BE%BA/%E8%88%87%E5%96%9C%E5%A4%A9%E6%BA%80%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/|title=Yoki Tenman Shrine (Sakurai, Hase)|publisher=Sakurai City Tourism Association|access-date=April 23, 2019}} According to the ''{{ill|Hasedera Genki|ja|長谷寺験記}}'', the shrine was founded in 946 CE (Tenkei 9) and later became venerated as the guardian shrine of Hase. == Sub-Shrines == * Taki-no-Kura Gongen Shrine (deity: [[Hayatama-no-Mikoto]], [[Izanagi]], [[Izanami]]) * Shiradaijin Shrine (deity: {{ill|Watarai Haruhiko|ja|度会春彦}}) * Sakuraha Shrine (deity: {{ill|Iyo Shinnō|ja|伊予親王}}) * Hachioji Shrine (deity: [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]) == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Wooden Seated Statue of Tenjin : Known as "Wrathful Tenjin," this seated statue in court attire is made of {{ill|yosegi-zukuri|ja|寄木造}} and features an intense expression. It bears an inscription from Shogen 1 (1259), making it the oldest surviving sculpture of Tenjin in Japan.{{Cite web|url=https://www.nara-np.co.jp/news/20120421085721.html|title=Important Cultural Properties Newly Designated: Tenjin Seated Statue (Yoki Tenman Shrine)|publisher=Nara Newspaper|access-date=April 23, 2019}} Within the statue's head is a mirror engraved with an image of {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|ja|十一面観音|Ekādaśamukha=Ekādaśamukha|lt=Jūichimen Kannon}}, symbolizing Tenjin's connection to the deity.{{Cite journal|title=New Cultural Property Designations|journal=Monthly Cultural Properties|issue=585|page=17|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|year=2012}} Previously enshrined in the main hall, it is now housed in the {{ill|Nara National Museum|ja|奈良国立博物館}}. === Additional Cultural Assets === [[File:Samurai O-Yoroi 2.jpg|thumb|200px|Reference: White-Threaded Yoroi (owned by {{ill|Hasedera|ja|長谷寺}}, displayed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}})]] Five sets of important cultural property armor (White-Threaded Yoroi, Red-Threaded Yoroi, Hawk Feather Yoroi, Three-Eyed Plate Yoroi, Blue-Laced Armor) were once associated with Yoki Tenman Shrine, which served as {{ill|Hasedera|ja|長谷寺}}'s {{ill|Chinjugami|ja|鎮守神}}. These were gifted by {{ill|Kitabatake Norikata|ja|北畠教謙}} in 1560 (Eiroku 3) for services rendered in battles against {{ill|Matsunaga Hisahide|ja|松永久秀}}.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/7051|title=White-Threaded Yoroi (Detailed Listing)|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|access-date=December 1, 2019}}{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/7047|title=Red-Threaded Yoroi (Detailed Listing)|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|access-date=December 1, 2019}} == Access == * Nearest station: 25 minutes on foot from {{ill|Hasedera Station|ja|長谷寺駅}}, {{ill|Kintetsu Osaka Line|ja|近鉄大阪線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yoki-tenman-jinja}} * Its former rank was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. == Deities == ; Main deity * [[Sugawara no Michizane]] (菅原道真) ; Additional Deities * [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]] (天照大神) * [[Ōkurahime no Kami]] (大倉姫神) == History == The shrine is located in {{ill|Hase, Sakurai|ja|初瀬}}, believed to be the birthplace of {{ill|Nomino Sukune|ja|野見宿禰}}, the ancestor of the {{ill|Haji clan|ja|土師氏}}, later known as the Sugawara Clan. This historical connection made the area significant for the Sugawara family.{{Cite web|url=https://sakurai-kankou.jimdo.com/%E5%90%8D%E6%89%80%E6%97%A7%E8%B7%A1/%E5%88%9D%E7%80%AC%E9%81%93%E5%91%A8%E8%BE%BA/%E8%88%87%E5%96%9C%E5%A4%A9%E6%BA%80%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/|title=Yoki Tenman Shrine (Sakurai, Hase)|publisher=Sakurai City Tourism Association|access-date=April 23, 2019}} According to the ''{{ill|Hasedera Genki|ja|長谷寺験記}}'', the shrine was founded in 946 CE (Tenkei 9) and later became venerated as the guardian shrine of Hase. == Sub-Shrines == * Taki-no-Kura Gongen Shrine (deity: [[Hayatama-no-Mikoto]], [[Izanagi]], [[Izanami]]) * Shiradaijin Shrine (deity: {{ill|Watarai Haruhiko|ja|度会春彦}}) * Sakuraha Shrine (deity: {{ill|Iyo Shinnō|ja|伊予親王}}) * Hachioji Shrine (deity: [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]) == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Wooden Seated Statue of Tenjin : Known as "Wrathful Tenjin," this seated statue in court attire is made of {{ill|yosegi-zukuri|ja|寄木造}} and features an intense expression. It bears an inscription from Shogen 1 (1259), making it the oldest surviving sculpture of Tenjin in Japan.{{Cite web|url=https://www.nara-np.co.jp/news/20120421085721.html|title=Important Cultural Properties Newly Designated: Tenjin Seated Statue (Yoki Tenman Shrine)|publisher=Nara Newspaper|access-date=April 23, 2019}} Within the statue's head is a mirror engraved with an image of {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|ja|十一面観音|Ekādaśamukha=Ekādaśamukha|lt=Jūichimen Kannon}}, symbolizing Tenjin's connection to the deity.{{Cite journal|title=New Cultural Property Designations|journal=Monthly Cultural Properties|issue=585|page=17|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|year=2012}} Previously enshrined in the main hall, it is now housed in the {{ill|Nara National Museum|ja|奈良国立博物館}}. === Additional Cultural Assets === [[File:Samurai O-Yoroi 2.jpg|thumb|200px|Reference: White-Threaded Yoroi (owned by {{ill|Hasedera|ja|長谷寺}}, displayed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}})]] Five sets of important cultural property armor (White-Threaded Yoroi, Red-Threaded Yoroi, Hawk Feather Yoroi, Three-Eyed Plate Yoroi, Blue-Laced Armor) were once associated with Yoki Tenman Shrine, which served as {{ill|Hasedera|ja|長谷寺}}'s {{ill|Chinjugami|ja|鎮守神}}. These were gifted by {{ill|Kitabatake Norikata|ja|北畠教謙}} in 1560 (Eiroku 3) for services rendered in battles against {{ill|Matsunaga Hisahide|ja|松永久秀}}.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/7051|title=White-Threaded Yoroi (Detailed Listing)|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|access-date=December 1, 2019}}{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/7047|title=Red-Threaded Yoroi (Detailed Listing)|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|access-date=December 1, 2019}} == Access == * Nearest {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Yoki Tenman Shrine (與喜天満神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Yoki-tenman-jinja honden.jpg|250px]]
Main Hall |location= [[Sakurai, Nara|桜井市]], [[Nara Prefecture|奈良県]], {{ill|Hase, Sakurai|ja|初瀬}} 14 |Latitude = 34 | LatMinutes = 32 | LatSeconds = 05.9 | DirectionN = N |Longitude = 135 | LongMinutes = 54 | LongSeconds = 41.6 | DirectionE = E |MapCode = JP |Map= Japan Nara |LabelPosition= left |deity= {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} |ShrineRank= Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |consecration_year= 946 CE (Tenkei 9) |MainHall= |Alternatename= |AnnualFestival= |Rituals= }} '''Yoki Tenman Shrine''' (與喜天満神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Hase, Sakurai|ja|初瀬}}, {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|ja|桜井市}}, [[Nara Prefecture|奈良県]]. Its former rank was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. == Deities == ; Main deity * [[Sugawara no Michizane]] (菅原道真) ; Additional Deities * [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]] (天照大神) * [[Ōkurahime no Kami]] (大倉姫神) == History == The shrine is located in {{ill|Hase, Sakurai|ja|初瀬}}, believed to be the birthplace of {{ill|Nomino Sukune|ja|野見宿禰}}, the ancestor of the {{ill|Haji clan|ja|土師氏}}, later known as the Sugawara Clan. This historical connection made the area significant for the Sugawara family.{{Cite web|url=https://sakurai-kankou.jimdo.com/%E5%90%8D%E6%89%80%E6%97%A7%E8%B7%A1/%E5%88%9D%E7%80%AC%E9%81%93%E5%91%A8%E8%BE%BA/%E8%88%87%E5%96%9C%E5%A4%A9%E6%BA%80%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/|title=Yoki Tenman Shrine (Sakurai, Hase)|publisher=Sakurai City Tourism Association|access-date=April 23, 2019}} According to the ''{{ill|Hasedera Genki|ja|長谷寺験記}}'', the shrine was founded in 946 CE (Tenkei 9) and later became venerated as the guardian shrine of Hase. == Sub-Shrines == * Taki-no-Kura Gongen Shrine (deity: [[Hayatama-no-Mikoto]], [[Izanagi]], [[Izanami]]) * Shiradaijin Shrine (deity: {{ill|Watarai Haruhiko|ja|度会春彦}}) * Sakuraha Shrine (deity: {{ill|Iyo Shinnō|ja|伊予親王}}) * Hachioji Shrine (deity: [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]) == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Wooden Seated Statue of Tenjin : Known as "Wrathful Tenjin," this seated statue in court attire is made of {{ill|yosegi-zukuri|ja|寄木造}} and features an intense expression. It bears an inscription from Shogen 1 (1259), making it the oldest surviving sculpture of Tenjin in Japan.{{Cite web|url=https://www.nara-np.co.jp/news/20120421085721.html|title=Important Cultural Properties Newly Designated: Tenjin Seated Statue (Yoki Tenman Shrine)|publisher=Nara Newspaper|access-date=April 23, 2019}} Within the statue's head is a mirror engraved with an image of {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|ja|十一面観音|Ekādaśamukha=Ekādaśamukha|lt=Jūichimen Kannon}}, symbolizing Tenjin's connection to the deity.{{Cite journal|title=New Cultural Property Designations|journal=Monthly Cultural Properties|issue=585|page=17|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|year=2012}} Previously enshrined in the main hall, it is now housed in the {{ill|Nara National Museum|ja|奈良国立博物館}}. === Additional Cultural Assets === [[File:Samurai O-Yoroi 2.jpg|thumb|200px|Reference: White-Threaded Yoroi (owned by {{ill|Hasedera|ja|長谷寺}}, displayed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}})]] Five sets of important cultural property armor (White-Threaded Yoroi, Red-Threaded Yoroi, Hawk Feather Yoroi, Three-Eyed Plate Yoroi, Blue-Laced Armor) were once associated with Yoki Tenman Shrine, which served as {{ill|Hasedera|ja|長谷寺}}'s {{ill|Chinjugami|ja|鎮守神}}. These were gifted by {{ill|Kitabatake Norikata|ja|北畠教謙}} in 1560 (Eiroku 3) for services rendered in battles against {{ill|Matsunaga Hisahide|ja|松永久秀}}.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/7051|title=White-Threaded Yoroi (Detailed Listing)|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|access-date=December 1, 2019}}{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/7047|title=Red-Threaded Yoroi (Detailed Listing)|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|access-date=December 1, 2019}} == Access == * Nearest station: 25 minutes on foot from {{ill|Hasedera Station|ja|長谷寺駅}}, {{ill|Kintetsu Osaka Line|ja|近鉄大阪線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yoki-tenman-jinja}} * {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Yoki Tenman Shrine (與喜天満神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Yoki-tenman-jinja honden.jpg|250px]]
Main Hall |location= [[Sakurai, Nara|桜井市]], [[Nara Prefecture|奈良県]], {{ill|Hase, Sakurai|ja|初瀬}} 14 |Latitude = 34 | LatMinutes = 32 | LatSeconds = 05.9 | DirectionN = N |Longitude = 135 | LongMinutes = 54 | LongSeconds = 41.6 | DirectionE = E |MapCode = JP |Map= Japan Nara |LabelPosition= left |deity= {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} |ShrineRank= Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |consecration_year= 946 CE (Tenkei 9) |MainHall= |Alternatename= |AnnualFestival= |Rituals= }} '''Yoki Tenman Shrine''' (與喜天満神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Hase, Sakurai|ja|初瀬}}, {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|ja|桜井市}}, [[Nara Prefecture|奈良県]]. Its former rank was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. == Deities == ; Main deity * [[Sugawara no Michizane]] (菅原道真) ; Additional Deities * [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]] (天照大神) * [[Ōkurahime no Kami]] (大倉姫神) == History == The shrine is located in {{ill|Hase, Sakurai|ja|初瀬}}, believed to be the birthplace of {{ill|Nomino Sukune|ja|野見宿禰}}, the ancestor of the {{ill|Haji clan|ja|土師氏}}, later known as the Sugawara Clan. This historical connection made the area significant for the Sugawara family.{{Cite web|url=https://sakurai-kankou.jimdo.com/%E5%90%8D%E6%89%80%E6%97%A7%E8%B7%A1/%E5%88%9D%E7%80%AC%E9%81%93%E5%91%A8%E8%BE%BA/%E8%88%87%E5%96%9C%E5%A4%A9%E6%BA%80%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/|title=Yoki Tenman Shrine (Sakurai, Hase)|publisher=Sakurai City Tourism Association|access-date=April 23, 2019}} According to the ''{{ill|Hasedera Genki|ja|長谷寺験記}}'', the shrine was founded in 946 CE (Tenkei 9) and later became venerated as the guardian shrine of Hase. == Sub-Shrines == * Taki-no-Kura Gongen Shrine (deity: [[Hayatama-no-Mikoto]], [[Izanagi]], [[Izanami]]) * Shiradaijin Shrine (deity: {{ill|Watarai Haruhiko|ja|度会春彦}}) * Sakuraha Shrine (deity: {{ill|Iyo Shinnō|ja|伊予親王}}) * Hachioji Shrine (deity: [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]) == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Wooden Seated Statue of Tenjin : Known as "Wrathful Tenjin," this seated statue in court attire is made of {{ill|yosegi-zukuri|ja|寄木造}} and features an intense expression. It bears an inscription from Shogen 1 (1259), making it the oldest surviving sculpture of Tenjin in Japan.{{Cite web|url=https://www.nara-np.co.jp/news/20120421085721.html|title=Important Cultural Properties Newly Designated: Tenjin Seated Statue (Yoki Tenman Shrine)|publisher=Nara Newspaper|access-date=April 23, 2019}} Within the statue's head is a mirror engraved with an image of {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|ja|十一面観音|Ekādaśamukha=Ekādaśamukha|lt=Jūichimen Kannon}}, symbolizing Tenjin's connection to the deity.{{Cite journal|title=New Cultural Property Designations|journal=Monthly Cultural Properties|issue=585|page=17|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|year=2012}} Previously enshrined in the main hall, it is now housed in the {{ill|Nara National Museum|ja|奈良国立博物館}}. === Additional Cultural Assets === [[File:Samurai O-Yoroi 2.jpg|thumb|200px|Reference: White-Threaded Yoroi (owned by {{ill|Hasedera|ja|長谷寺}}, displayed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}})]] Five sets of important cultural property armor (White-Threaded Yoroi, Red-Threaded Yoroi, Hawk Feather Yoroi, Three-Eyed Plate Yoroi, Blue-Laced Armor) were once associated with Yoki Tenman Shrine, which served as {{ill|Hasedera|ja|長谷寺}}'s {{ill|Chinjugami|ja|鎮守神}}. These were gifted by {{ill|Kitabatake Norikata|ja|北畠教謙}} in 1560 (Eiroku 3) for services rendered in battles against {{ill|Matsunaga Hisahide|ja|松永久秀}}.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/7051|title=White-Threaded Yoroi (Detailed Listing)|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|access-date=December 1, 2019}}{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/7047|title=Red-Threaded Yoroi (Detailed Listing)|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|access-date=December 1, 2019}} == Access == * Nearest station: 25 minutes on foot from {{ill|Hasedera Station|ja|長谷寺駅}}, {{ill|Kintetsu Osaka Line|ja|近鉄大阪線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yoki-tenman-jinja}} * It bears an inscription from Shogen 1 (1259), making it the oldest surviving sculpture of Tenjin in Japan.{{Cite web|url=https://www.nara-np.co.jp/news/20120421085721.html|title=Important Cultural Properties Newly Designated: Tenjin Seated Statue (Yoki Tenman Shrine)|publisher=Nara Newspaper|access-date=April 23, 2019}} Within the statue's head is a mirror engraved with an image of {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|ja|十一面観音|Ekādaśamukha=Ekādaśamukha|lt=Jūichimen Kannon}}, symbolizing Tenjin's connection to the deity.{{Cite journal|title=New Cultural Property Designations|journal=Monthly Cultural Properties|issue=585|page=17|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|year=2012}} Previously enshrined in the main hall, it is now housed in the {{ill|Nara National Museum|ja|奈良国立博物館}}. === Additional Cultural Assets === [[File:Samurai O-Yoroi 2.jpg|thumb|200px|Reference: White-Threaded Yoroi (owned by {{ill|Hasedera|ja|長谷寺}}, displayed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}})]] Five sets of important cultural property armor (White-Threaded Yoroi, Red-Threaded Yoroi, Hawk Feather Yoroi, Three-Eyed Plate Yoroi, Blue-Laced Armor) were once associated with Yoki Tenman Shrine, which served as {{ill|Hasedera|ja|長谷寺}}'s {{ill|Chinjugami|ja|鎮守神}}. These were gifted by {{ill|Kitabatake Norikata|ja|北畠教謙}} in 1560 (Eiroku 3) for services rendered in battles against {{ill|Matsunaga Hisahide|ja|松永久秀}}.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/7051|title=White-Threaded Yoroi (Detailed Listing)|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|access-date=December 1, 2019}}{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/7047|title=Red-Threaded Yoroi (Detailed Listing)|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|access-date=December 1, 2019}} == Access == * Nearest station: 25 minutes on foot from {{ill|Hasedera Station|ja|長谷寺駅}}, {{ill|Kintetsu Osaka Line|ja|近鉄大阪線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yoki-tenman-jinja}} * [https://www.yokitenmanjinja.jp/ Official Website of Yoki Tenman Shrine] {{citation needed|Yoiki Tenma Shrine}} [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Yoiki Tenma Shrine}} [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category:tier 2]] [[Category:deprecated qq open]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Yoki Tenman Shrine (與喜天満神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Yoki-tenman-jinja honden.jpg|250px]]
Main Hall |location= [[Sakurai, Nara|桜井市]], [[Nara Prefecture|奈良県]], {{ill|Hase, Sakurai|ja|初瀬}} 14 |Latitude = 34 | LatMinutes = 32 | LatSeconds = 05.9 | DirectionN = N |Longitude = 135 | LongMinutes = 54 | LongSeconds = 41.6 | DirectionE = E |MapCode = JP |Map= Japan Nara |LabelPosition= left |deity= {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} |ShrineRank= Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |consecration_year= 946 CE (Tenkei 9) |MainHall= |Alternatename= |AnnualFestival= |Rituals= }} '''Yoki Tenman Shrine''' (與喜天満神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Hase, Sakurai|ja|初瀬}}, {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|ja|桜井市}}, [[Nara Prefecture|奈良県]]. Its former rank was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. == Deities == ; Main deity * [[Sugawara no Michizane]] (菅原道真) ; Additional Deities * [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]] (天照大神) * [[Ōkurahime no Kami]] (大倉姫神) == History == The shrine is located in {{ill|Hase, Sakurai|ja|初瀬}}, believed to be the birthplace of {{ill|Nomino Sukune|ja|野見宿禰}}, the ancestor of the {{ill|Haji clan|ja|土師氏}}, later known as the Sugawara Clan. This historical connection made the area significant for the Sugawara family.{{Cite web|url=https://sakurai-kankou.jimdo.com/%E5%90%8D%E6%89%80%E6%97%A7%E8%B7%A1/%E5%88%9D%E7%80%AC%E9%81%93%E5%91%A8%E8%BE%BA/%E8%88%87%E5%96%9C%E5%A4%A9%E6%BA%80%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/|title=Yoki Tenman Shrine (Sakurai, Hase)|publisher=Sakurai City Tourism Association|access-date=April 23, 2019}} According to the ''{{ill|Hasedera Genki|ja|長谷寺験記}}'', the shrine was founded in 946 CE (Tenkei 9) and later became venerated as the guardian shrine of Hase. == Sub-Shrines == * Taki-no-Kura Gongen Shrine (deity: [[Hayatama-no-Mikoto]], [[Izanagi]], [[Izanami]]) * Shiradaijin Shrine (deity: {{ill|Watarai Haruhiko|ja|度会春彦}}) * Sakuraha Shrine (deity: {{ill|Iyo Shinnō|ja|伊予親王}}) * Hachioji Shrine (deity: [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]) == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Wooden Seated Statue of Tenjin : Known as "Wrathful Tenjin," this seated statue in court attire is made of {{ill|yosegi-zukuri|ja|寄木造}} and features an intense expression. It bears an inscription from Shogen 1 (1259), making it the oldest surviving sculpture of Tenjin in Japan.{{Cite web|url=https://www.nara-np.co.jp/news/20120421085721.html|title=Important Cultural Properties Newly Designated: Tenjin Seated Statue (Yoki Tenman Shrine)|publisher=Nara Newspaper|access-date=April 23, 2019}} Within the statue's head is a mirror engraved with an image of {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|ja|十一面観音|Ekādaśamukha=Ekādaśamukha|lt=Jūichimen Kannon}}, symbolizing Tenjin's connection to the deity.{{Cite journal|title=New Cultural Property Designations|journal=Monthly Cultural Properties|issue=585|page=17|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|year=2012}} Previously enshrined in the main hall, it is now housed in the {{ill|Nara National Museum|ja|奈良国立博物館}}. === Additional Cultural Assets === [[File:Samurai O-Yoroi 2.jpg|thumb|200px|Reference: White-Threaded Yoroi (owned by {{ill|Hasedera|ja|長谷寺}}, displayed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}})]] Five sets of important cultural property armor (White-Threaded Yoroi, Red-Threaded Yoroi, Hawk Feather Yoroi, Three-Eyed Plate Yoroi, Blue-Laced Armor) were once associated with Yoki Tenman Shrine, which served as {{ill|Hasedera|ja|長谷寺}}'s {{ill|Chinjugami|ja|鎮守神}}. These were gifted by {{ill|Kitabatake Norikata|ja|北畠教謙}} in 1560 (Eiroku 3) for services rendered in battles against {{ill|Matsunaga Hisahide|ja|松永久秀}}.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/7051|title=White-Threaded Yoroi (Detailed Listing)|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|access-date=December 1, 2019}}{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/7047|title=Red-Threaded Yoroi (Detailed Listing)|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|access-date=December 1, 2019}} == Access == * Nearest station: 25 minutes on foot from {{ill|Hasedera Station|ja|長谷寺駅}}, {{ill|Kintetsu Osaka Line|ja|近鉄大阪線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yoki-tenman-jinja}} * {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Yoki Tenman Shrine (與喜天満神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Yoki-tenman-jinja honden.jpg|250px]]
Main Hall |location= [[Sakurai, Nara|桜井市]], [[Nara Prefecture|奈良県]], {{ill|Hase, Sakurai|ja|初瀬}} 14 |Latitude = 34 | LatMinutes = 32 | LatSeconds = 05.9 | DirectionN = N |Longitude = 135 | LongMinutes = 54 | LongSeconds = 41.6 | DirectionE = E |MapCode = JP |Map= Japan Nara |LabelPosition= left |deity= {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} |ShrineRank= Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |consecration_year= 946 CE (Tenkei 9) |MainHall= |Alternatename= |AnnualFestival= |Rituals= }} '''Yoki Tenman Shrine''' (與喜天満神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Hase, Sakurai|ja|初瀬}}, {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|ja|桜井市}}, [[Nara Prefecture|奈良県]]. Its former rank was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. == Deities == ; Main deity * [[Sugawara no Michizane]] (菅原道真) ; Additional Deities * [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]] (天照大神) * [[Ōkurahime no Kami]] (大倉姫神) == History == The shrine is located in {{ill|Hase, Sakurai|ja|初瀬}}, believed to be the birthplace of {{ill|Nomino Sukune|ja|野見宿禰}}, the ancestor of the {{ill|Haji clan|ja|土師氏}}, later known as the Sugawara Clan. This historical connection made the area significant for the Sugawara family.{{Cite web|url=https://sakurai-kankou.jimdo.com/%E5%90%8D%E6%89%80%E6%97%A7%E8%B7%A1/%E5%88%9D%E7%80%AC%E9%81%93%E5%91%A8%E8%BE%BA/%E8%88%87%E5%96%9C%E5%A4%A9%E6%BA%80%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/|title=Yoki Tenman Shrine (Sakurai, Hase)|publisher=Sakurai City Tourism Association|access-date=April 23, 2019}} According to the ''{{ill|Hasedera Genki|ja|長谷寺験記}}'', the shrine was founded in 946 CE (Tenkei 9) and later became venerated as the guardian shrine of Hase. == Sub-Shrines == * Taki-no-Kura Gongen Shrine (deity: [[Hayatama-no-Mikoto]], [[Izanagi]], [[Izanami]]) * Shiradaijin Shrine (deity: {{ill|Watarai Haruhiko|ja|度会春彦}}) * Sakuraha Shrine (deity: {{ill|Iyo Shinnō|ja|伊予親王}}) * Hachioji Shrine (deity: [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]) == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Wooden Seated Statue of Tenjin : Known as "Wrathful Tenjin," this seated statue in court attire is made of {{ill|yosegi-zukuri|ja|寄木造}} and features an intense expression. It bears an inscription from Shogen 1 (1259), making it the oldest surviving sculpture of Tenjin in Japan.{{Cite web|url=https://www.nara-np.co.jp/news/20120421085721.html|title=Important Cultural Properties Newly Designated: Tenjin Seated Statue (Yoki Tenman Shrine)|publisher=Nara Newspaper|access-date=April 23, 2019}} Within the statue's head is a mirror engraved with an image of {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|ja|十一面観音|Ekādaśamukha=Ekādaśamukha|lt=Jūichimen Kannon}}, symbolizing Tenjin's connection to the deity.{{Cite journal|title=New Cultural Property Designations|journal=Monthly Cultural Properties|issue=585|page=17|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|year=2012}} Previously enshrined in the main hall, it is now housed in the {{ill|Nara National Museum|ja|奈良国立博物館}}. === Additional Cultural Assets === [[File:Samurai O-Yoroi 2.jpg|thumb|200px|Reference: White-Threaded Yoroi (owned by {{ill|Hasedera|ja|長谷寺}}, displayed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}})]] Five sets of important cultural property armor (White-Threaded Yoroi, Red-Threaded Yoroi, Hawk Feather Yoroi, Three-Eyed Plate Yoroi, Blue-Laced Armor) were once associated with Yoki Tenman Shrine, which served as {{ill|Hasedera|ja|長谷寺}}'s {{ill|Chinjugami|ja|鎮守神}}. These were gifted by {{ill|Kitabatake Norikata|ja|北畠教謙}} in 1560 (Eiroku 3) for services rendered in battles against {{ill|Matsunaga Hisahide|ja|松永久秀}}.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/7051|title=White-Threaded Yoroi (Detailed Listing)|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|access-date=December 1, 2019}}{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/7047|title=Red-Threaded Yoroi (Detailed Listing)|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|access-date=December 1, 2019}} == Access == * Nearest station: 25 minutes on foot from {{ill|Hasedera Station|ja|長谷寺駅}}, {{ill|Kintetsu Osaka Line|ja|近鉄大阪線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yoki-tenman-jinja}} * Main Hall |location= [[Sakurai, Nara|桜井市]], [[Nara Prefecture|奈良県]], {{ill|Hase, Sakurai|ja|初瀬}} 14 |Latitude = 34 | LatMinutes = 32 | LatSeconds = 05.9 | DirectionN = N |Longitude = 135 | LongMinutes = 54 | LongSeconds = 41.6 | DirectionE = E |MapCode = JP |Map= Japan Nara |LabelPosition= left |deity= {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} |ShrineRank= Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |consecration_year= 946 CE (Tenkei 9) |MainHall= |Alternatename= |AnnualFestival= |Rituals= }} '''Yoki Tenman Shrine''' (與喜天満神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Hase, Sakurai|ja|初瀬}}, {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|ja|桜井市}}, [[Nara Prefecture|奈良県]]. Its former rank was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. == Deities == ; Main deity * [[Sugawara no Michizane]] (菅原道真) ; Additional Deities * [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]] (天照大神) * [[Ōkurahime no Kami]] (大倉姫神) == History == The shrine is located in {{ill|Hase, Sakurai|ja|初瀬}}, believed to be the birthplace of {{ill|Nomino Sukune|ja|野見宿禰}}, the ancestor of the {{ill|Haji clan|ja|土師氏}}, later known as the Sugawara Clan. This historical connection made the area significant for the Sugawara family.{{Cite web|url=https://sakurai-kankou.jimdo.com/%E5%90%8D%E6%89%80%E6%97%A7%E8%B7%A1/%E5%88%9D%E7%80%AC%E9%81%93%E5%91%A8%E8%BE%BA/%E8%88%87%E5%96%9C%E5%A4%A9%E6%BA%80%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/|title=Yoki Tenman Shrine (Sakurai, Hase)|publisher=Sakurai City Tourism Association|access-date=April 23, 2019}} According to the ''{{ill|Hasedera Genki|ja|長谷寺験記}}'', the shrine was founded in 946 CE (Tenkei 9) and later became venerated as the guardian shrine of Hase. == Sub-Shrines == * Taki-no-Kura Gongen Shrine (deity: [[Hayatama-no-Mikoto]], [[Izanagi]], [[Izanami]]) * Shiradaijin Shrine (deity: {{ill|Watarai Haruhiko|ja|度会春彦}}) * Sakuraha Shrine (deity: {{ill|Iyo Shinnō|ja|伊予親王}}) * Hachioji Shrine (deity: [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]) == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Wooden Seated Statue of Tenjin : Known as "Wrathful Tenjin," this seated statue in court attire is made of {{ill|yosegi-zukuri|ja|寄木造}} and features an intense expression. It bears an inscription from Shogen 1 (1259), making it the oldest surviving sculpture of Tenjin in Japan.{{Cite web|url=https://www.nara-np.co.jp/news/20120421085721.html|title=Important Cultural Properties Newly Designated: Tenjin Seated Statue (Yoki Tenman Shrine)|publisher=Nara Newspaper|access-date=April 23, 2019}} Within the statue's head is a mirror engraved with an image of {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|ja|十一面観音|Ekādaśamukha=Ekādaśamukha|lt=Jūichimen Kannon}}, symbolizing Tenjin's connection to the deity.{{Cite journal|title=New Cultural Property Designations|journal=Monthly Cultural Properties|issue=585|page=17|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|year=2012}} Previously enshrined in the main hall, it is now housed in the {{ill|Nara National Museum|ja|奈良国立博物館}}. === Additional Cultural Assets === [[File:Samurai O-Yoroi 2.jpg|thumb|200px|Reference: White-Threaded Yoroi (owned by {{ill|Hasedera|ja|長谷寺}}, displayed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}})]] Five sets of important cultural property armor (White-Threaded Yoroi, Red-Threaded Yoroi, Hawk Feather Yoroi, Three-Eyed Plate Yoroi, Blue-Laced Armor) were once associated with Yoki Tenman Shrine, which served as {{ill|Hasedera|ja|長谷寺}}'s {{ill|Chinjugami|ja|鎮守神}}. These were gifted by {{ill|Kitabatake Norikata|ja|北畠教謙}} in 1560 (Eiroku 3) for services rendered in battles against {{ill|Matsunaga Hisahide|ja|松永久秀}}.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/7051|title=White-Threaded Yoroi (Detailed Listing)|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|access-date=December 1, 2019}}{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/7047|title=Red-Threaded Yoroi (Detailed Listing)|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|access-date=December 1, 2019}} == Access == * Nearest station: 25 minutes on foot from {{ill|Hasedera Station|ja|長谷寺駅}}, {{ill|Kintetsu Osaka Line|ja|近鉄大阪線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yoki-tenman-jinja}} * web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/7051|title=White-Threaded Yoroi (Detailed Listing)|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|access-date=December 1, 2019}}{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/7047|title=Red-Threaded Yoroi (Detailed Listing)|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|access-date=December 1, 2019}} == Access == * Nearest [[ja:與喜天満神社]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Yoki Tenman Shrine (與喜天満神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Yoki-tenman-jinja honden.jpg|250px]]
Main Hall |location= [[Sakurai, Nara|桜井市]], [[Nara Prefecture|奈良県]], {{ill|Hase, Sakurai|ja|初瀬}} 14 |Latitude = 34 | LatMinutes = 32 | LatSeconds = 05.9 | DirectionN = N |Longitude = 135 | LongMinutes = 54 | LongSeconds = 41.6 | DirectionE = E |MapCode = JP |Map= Japan Nara |LabelPosition= left |deity= {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} |ShrineRank= Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |consecration_year= 946 CE (Tenkei 9) |MainHall= |Alternatename= |AnnualFestival= |Rituals= }} '''Yoki Tenman Shrine''' (與喜天満神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Hase, Sakurai|ja|初瀬}}, {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|ja|桜井市}}, [[Nara Prefecture|奈良県]]. Its former rank was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. == Deities == ; Main deity * [[Sugawara no Michizane]] (菅原道真) ; Additional Deities * [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]] (天照大神) * [[Ōkurahime no Kami]] (大倉姫神) == History == The shrine is located in {{ill|Hase, Sakurai|ja|初瀬}}, believed to be the birthplace of {{ill|Nomino Sukune|ja|野見宿禰}}, the ancestor of the {{ill|Haji clan|ja|土師氏}}, later known as the Sugawara Clan. This historical connection made the area significant for the Sugawara family.{{Cite web|url=https://sakurai-kankou.jimdo.com/%E5%90%8D%E6%89%80%E6%97%A7%E8%B7%A1/%E5%88%9D%E7%80%AC%E9%81%93%E5%91%A8%E8%BE%BA/%E8%88%87%E5%96%9C%E5%A4%A9%E6%BA%80%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/|title=Yoki Tenman Shrine (Sakurai, Hase)|publisher=Sakurai City Tourism Association|access-date=April 23, 2019}} According to the ''{{ill|Hasedera Genki|ja|長谷寺験記}}'', the shrine was founded in 946 CE (Tenkei 9) and later became venerated as the guardian shrine of Hase. == Sub-Shrines == * Taki-no-Kura Gongen Shrine (deity: [[Hayatama-no-Mikoto]], [[Izanagi]], [[Izanami]]) * Shiradaijin Shrine (deity: {{ill|Watarai Haruhiko|ja|度会春彦}}) * Sakuraha Shrine (deity: {{ill|Iyo Shinnō|ja|伊予親王}}) * Hachioji Shrine (deity: [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]) == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Wooden Seated Statue of Tenjin : Known as "Wrathful Tenjin," this seated statue in court attire is made of {{ill|yosegi-zukuri|ja|寄木造}} and features an intense expression. It bears an inscription from Shogen 1 (1259), making it the oldest surviving sculpture of Tenjin in Japan.{{Cite web|url=https://www.nara-np.co.jp/news/20120421085721.html|title=Important Cultural Properties Newly Designated: Tenjin Seated Statue (Yoki Tenman Shrine)|publisher=Nara Newspaper|access-date=April 23, 2019}} Within the statue's head is a mirror engraved with an image of {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|ja|十一面観音|Ekādaśamukha=Ekādaśamukha|lt=Jūichimen Kannon}}, symbolizing Tenjin's connection to the deity.{{Cite journal|title=New Cultural Property Designations|journal=Monthly Cultural Properties|issue=585|page=17|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|year=2012}} Previously enshrined in the main hall, it is now housed in the {{ill|Nara National Museum|ja|奈良国立博物館}}. === Additional Cultural Assets === [[File:Samurai O-Yoroi 2.jpg|thumb|200px|Reference: White-Threaded Yoroi (owned by {{ill|Hasedera|ja|長谷寺}}, displayed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}})]] Five sets of important cultural property armor (White-Threaded Yoroi, Red-Threaded Yoroi, Hawk Feather Yoroi, Three-Eyed Plate Yoroi, Blue-Laced Armor) were once associated with Yoki Tenman Shrine, which served as {{ill|Hasedera|ja|長谷寺}}'s {{ill|Chinjugami|ja|鎮守神}}. These were gifted by {{ill|Kitabatake Norikata|ja|北畠教謙}} in 1560 (Eiroku 3) for services rendered in battles against {{ill|Matsunaga Hisahide|ja|松永久秀}}.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/7051|title=White-Threaded Yoroi (Detailed Listing)|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|access-date=December 1, 2019}}{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/7047|title=Red-Threaded Yoroi (Detailed Listing)|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|access-date=December 1, 2019}} == Access == * Nearest {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Yoki Tenman Shrine (與喜天満神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Yoki-tenman-jinja honden.jpg|250px]]
Main Hall |location= [[Sakurai, Nara|桜井市]], [[Nara Prefecture|奈良県]], {{ill|Hase, Sakurai|ja|初瀬}} 14 |Latitude = 34 | LatMinutes = 32 | LatSeconds = 05.9 | DirectionN = N |Longitude = 135 | LongMinutes = 54 | LongSeconds = 41.6 | DirectionE = E |MapCode = JP |Map= Japan Nara |LabelPosition= left |deity= {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} |ShrineRank= Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |consecration_year= 946 CE (Tenkei 9) |MainHall= |Alternatename= |AnnualFestival= |Rituals= }} '''Yoki Tenman Shrine''' (與喜天満神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Hase, Sakurai|ja|初瀬}}, {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|ja|桜井市}}, [[Nara Prefecture|奈良県]]. Its former rank was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. == Deities == ; Main deity * [[Sugawara no Michizane]] (菅原道真) ; Additional Deities * [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]] (天照大神) * [[Ōkurahime no Kami]] (大倉姫神) == History == The shrine is located in {{ill|Hase, Sakurai|ja|初瀬}}, believed to be the birthplace of {{ill|Nomino Sukune|ja|野見宿禰}}, the ancestor of the {{ill|Haji clan|ja|土師氏}}, later known as the Sugawara Clan. This historical connection made the area significant for the Sugawara family.{{Cite web|url=https://sakurai-kankou.jimdo.com/%E5%90%8D%E6%89%80%E6%97%A7%E8%B7%A1/%E5%88%9D%E7%80%AC%E9%81%93%E5%91%A8%E8%BE%BA/%E8%88%87%E5%96%9C%E5%A4%A9%E6%BA%80%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/|title=Yoki Tenman Shrine (Sakurai, Hase)|publisher=Sakurai City Tourism Association|access-date=April 23, 2019}} According to the ''{{ill|Hasedera Genki|ja|長谷寺験記}}'', the shrine was founded in 946 CE (Tenkei 9) and later became venerated as the guardian shrine of Hase. == Sub-Shrines == * Taki-no-Kura Gongen Shrine (deity: [[Hayatama-no-Mikoto]], [[Izanagi]], [[Izanami]]) * Shiradaijin Shrine (deity: {{ill|Watarai Haruhiko|ja|度会春彦}}) * Sakuraha Shrine (deity: {{ill|Iyo Shinnō|ja|伊予親王}}) * Hachioji Shrine (deity: [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]) == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Wooden Seated Statue of Tenjin : Known as "Wrathful Tenjin," this seated statue in court attire is made of {{ill|yosegi-zukuri|ja|寄木造}} and features an intense expression. It bears an inscription from Shogen 1 (1259), making it the oldest surviving sculpture of Tenjin in Japan.{{Cite web|url=https://www.nara-np.co.jp/news/20120421085721.html|title=Important Cultural Properties Newly Designated: Tenjin Seated Statue (Yoki Tenman Shrine)|publisher=Nara Newspaper|access-date=April 23, 2019}} Within the statue's head is a mirror engraved with an image of {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|ja|十一面観音|Ekādaśamukha=Ekādaśamukha|lt=Jūichimen Kannon}}, symbolizing Tenjin's connection to the deity.{{Cite journal|title=New Cultural Property Designations|journal=Monthly Cultural Properties|issue=585|page=17|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|year=2012}} Previously enshrined in the main hall, it is now housed in the {{ill|Nara National Museum|ja|奈良国立博物館}}. === Additional Cultural Assets === [[File:Samurai O-Yoroi 2.jpg|thumb|200px|Reference: White-Threaded Yoroi (owned by {{ill|Hasedera|ja|長谷寺}}, displayed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}})]] Five sets of important cultural property armor (White-Threaded Yoroi, Red-Threaded Yoroi, Hawk Feather Yoroi, Three-Eyed Plate Yoroi, Blue-Laced Armor) were once associated with Yoki Tenman Shrine, which served as {{ill|Hasedera|ja|長谷寺}}'s {{ill|Chinjugami|ja|鎮守神}}. These were gifted by {{ill|Kitabatake Norikata|ja|北畠教謙}} in 1560 (Eiroku 3) for services rendered in battles against {{ill|Matsunaga Hisahide|ja|松永久秀}}.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/7051|title=White-Threaded Yoroi (Detailed Listing)|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|access-date=December 1, 2019}}{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/7047|title=Red-Threaded Yoroi (Detailed Listing)|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|access-date=December 1, 2019}} == Access == * Nearest station: 25 minutes on foot from {{ill|Hasedera Station|ja|長谷寺駅}}, {{ill|Kintetsu Osaka Line|ja|近鉄大阪線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yoki-tenman-jinja}} * [https://www.yokitenmanjinja.jp/ Official Website of Yoki Tenman Shrine] {{citation needed|Yoiki Tenma Shrine}} [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Yoiki Tenma Shrine}} [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category:Tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Yoki Tenman Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Yoki Tenman Shrine]][[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yoki Tenman {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Yoki Tenman Shrine (與喜天満神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Yoki-tenman-jinja honden.jpg|250px]]
Main Hall |location= [[Sakurai, Nara|桜井市]], [[Nara Prefecture|奈良県]], {{ill|Hase, Sakurai|ja|初瀬}} 14 |Latitude = 34 | LatMinutes = 32 | LatSeconds = 05.9 | DirectionN = N |Longitude = 135 | LongMinutes = 54 | LongSeconds = 41.6 | DirectionE = E |MapCode = JP |Map= Japan Nara |LabelPosition= left |deity= {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} |ShrineRank= Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |consecration_year= 946 CE (Tenkei 9) |MainHall= |Alternatename= |AnnualFestival= |Rituals= }} '''Yoki Tenman Shrine''' (與喜天満神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Hase, Sakurai|ja|初瀬}}, {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|ja|桜井市}}, [[Nara Prefecture|奈良県]]. Its former rank was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. == Deities == ; Main deity * [[Sugawara no Michizane]] (菅原道真) ; Additional Deities * [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]] (天照大神) * [[Ōkurahime no Kami]] (大倉姫神) == History == The shrine is located in {{ill|Hase, Sakurai|ja|初瀬}}, believed to be the birthplace of {{ill|Nomino Sukune|ja|野見宿禰}}, the ancestor of the {{ill|Haji clan|ja|土師氏}}, later known as the Sugawara Clan. This historical connection made the area significant for the Sugawara family.{{Cite web|url=https://sakurai-kankou.jimdo.com/%E5%90%8D%E6%89%80%E6%97%A7%E8%B7%A1/%E5%88%9D%E7%80%AC%E9%81%93%E5%91%A8%E8%BE%BA/%E8%88%87%E5%96%9C%E5%A4%A9%E6%BA%80%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/|title=Yoki Tenman Shrine (Sakurai, Hase)|publisher=Sakurai City Tourism Association|access-date=April 23, 2019}} According to the ''{{ill|Hasedera Genki|ja|長谷寺験記}}'', the shrine was founded in 946 CE (Tenkei 9) and later became venerated as the guardian shrine of Hase. == Sub-Shrines == * Taki-no-Kura Gongen Shrine (deity: [[Hayatama-no-Mikoto]], [[Izanagi]], [[Izanami]]) * Shiradaijin Shrine (deity: {{ill|Watarai Haruhiko|ja|度会春彦}}) * Sakuraha Shrine (deity: {{ill|Iyo Shinnō|ja|伊予親王|1=Iyo Shinnō}}) * Hachioji Shrine (deity: [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]) == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Wooden Seated Statue of Tenjin : Known as "Wrathful Tenjin," this seated statue in court attire is made of {{ill|yosegi-zukuri|ja|寄木造}} and features an intense expression. It bears an inscription from Shogen 1 (1259), making it the oldest surviving sculpture of Tenjin in Japan.{{Cite web|url=https://www.nara-np.co.jp/news/20120421085721.html|title=Important Cultural Properties Newly Designated: Tenjin Seated Statue (Yoki Tenman Shrine)|publisher=Nara Newspaper|access-date=April 23, 2019}} Within the statue's head is a mirror engraved with an image of {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|ja|十一面観音|Ekādaśamukha=Ekādaśamukha|lt=Jūichimen Kannon}}, symbolizing Tenjin's connection to the deity.{{Cite journal|title=New Cultural Property Designations|journal=Monthly Cultural Properties|issue=585|page=17|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|year=2012}} Previously enshrined in the main hall, it is now housed in the {{ill|Nara National Museum|ja|奈良国立博物館}}. === Additional Cultural Assets === [[File:Samurai O-Yoroi 2.jpg|thumb|200px|Reference: White-Threaded Yoroi (owned by {{ill|Hasedera|ja|長谷寺}}, displayed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}})]] Five sets of important cultural property armor (White-Threaded Yoroi, Red-Threaded Yoroi, Hawk Feather Yoroi, Three-Eyed Plate Yoroi, Blue-Laced Armor) were once associated with Yoki Tenman Shrine, which served as {{ill|Hasedera|ja|長谷寺}}'s {{ill|Chinjugami|ja|鎮守神}}. These were gifted by {{ill|Kitabatake Norikata|ja|北畠教謙}} in 1560 (Eiroku 3) for services rendered in battles against {{ill|Matsunaga Hisahide|ja|松永久秀}}.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/7051|title=White-Threaded Yoroi (Detailed Listing)|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|access-date=December 1, 2019}}{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/7047|title=Red-Threaded Yoroi (Detailed Listing)|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|access-date=December 1, 2019}} == Access == * Nearest station: 25 minutes on foot from {{ill|Hasedera Station|ja|長谷寺駅}}, {{ill|Kintetsu Osaka Line|ja|近鉄大阪線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yoki-tenman-jinja}} * Main Hall |location= [[Sakurai, Nara|桜井市]], [[Nara Prefecture|奈良県]], {{ill|Hase, Sakurai|ja|初瀬}} 14 |Latitude = 34 | LatMinutes = 32 | LatSeconds = 05.9 | DirectionN = N |Longitude = 135 | LongMinutes = 54 | LongSeconds = 41.6 | DirectionE = E |MapCode = JP |Map= Japan Nara |LabelPosition= left |deity= {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} |ShrineRank= Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |consecration_year= 946 CE (Tenkei 9) |MainHall= |Alternatename= |AnnualFestival= |Rituals= }} '''Yoki Tenman Shrine''' (與喜天満神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Hase, Sakurai|ja|初瀬}}, {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|ja|桜井市}}, [[Nara Prefecture|奈良県]]. Its former rank was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. == Deities == ; Main deity * [[Sugawara no Michizane]] (菅原道真) ; Additional Deities * [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]] (天照大神) * [[Ōkurahime no Kami]] (大倉姫神) == History == The shrine is located in {{ill|Hase, Sakurai|ja|初瀬}}, believed to be the birthplace of {{ill|Nomino Sukune|ja|野見宿禰}}, the ancestor of the {{ill|Haji clan|ja|土師氏}}, later known as the Sugawara Clan. This historical connection made the area significant for the Sugawara family.{{Cite web|url=https://sakurai-kankou.jimdo.com/%E5%90%8D%E6%89%80%E6%97%A7%E8%B7%A1/%E5%88%9D%E7%80%AC%E9%81%93%E5%91%A8%E8%BE%BA/%E8%88%87%E5%96%9C%E5%A4%A9%E6%BA%80%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/|title=Yoki Tenman Shrine (Sakurai, Hase)|publisher=Sakurai City Tourism Association|access-date=April 23, 2019}} According to the ''{{ill|Hasedera Genki|ja|長谷寺験記}}'', the shrine was founded in 946 CE (Tenkei 9) and later became venerated as the guardian shrine of Hase. == Sub-Shrines == * Taki-no-Kura Gongen Shrine (deity: [[Hayatama-no-Mikoto]], [[Izanagi]], [[Izanami]]) * Shiradaijin Shrine (deity: {{ill|Watarai Haruhiko|ja|度会春彦}}) * Sakuraha Shrine (deity: {{ill|Iyo Shinnō|ja|伊予親王|1=Iyo Shinnō}}) * Hachioji Shrine (deity: [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]) == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Wooden Seated Statue of Tenjin : Known as "Wrathful Tenjin," this seated statue in court attire is made of {{ill|yosegi-zukuri|ja|寄木造}} and features an intense expression. It bears an inscription from Shogen 1 (1259), making it the oldest surviving sculpture of Tenjin in Japan.{{Cite web|url=https://www.nara-np.co.jp/news/20120421085721.html|title=Important Cultural Properties Newly Designated: Tenjin Seated Statue (Yoki Tenman Shrine)|publisher=Nara Newspaper|access-date=April 23, 2019}} Within the statue's head is a mirror engraved with an image of {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|ja|十一面観音|Ekādaśamukha=Ekādaśamukha|lt=Jūichimen Kannon}}, symbolizing Tenjin's connection to the deity.{{Cite journal|title=New Cultural Property Designations|journal=Monthly Cultural Properties|issue=585|page=17|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|year=2012}} Previously enshrined in the main hall, it is now housed in the {{ill|Nara National Museum|ja|奈良国立博物館}}. === Additional Cultural Assets === [[File:Samurai O-Yoroi 2.jpg|thumb|200px|Reference: White-Threaded Yoroi (owned by {{ill|Hasedera|ja|長谷寺}}, displayed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}})]] Five sets of important cultural property armor (White-Threaded Yoroi, Red-Threaded Yoroi, Hawk Feather Yoroi, Three-Eyed Plate Yoroi, Blue-Laced Armor) were once associated with Yoki Tenman Shrine, which served as {{ill|Hasedera|ja|長谷寺}}'s {{ill|Chinjugami|ja|鎮守神}}. These were gifted by {{ill|Kitabatake Norikata|ja|北畠教謙}} in 1560 (Eiroku 3) for services rendered in battles against {{ill|Matsunaga Hisahide|ja|松永久秀}}.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/7051|title=White-Threaded Yoroi (Detailed Listing)|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|access-date=December 1, 2019}}{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/7047|title=Red-Threaded Yoroi (Detailed Listing)|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|access-date=December 1, 2019}} == Access == * Nearest station: 25 minutes on foot from {{ill|Hasedera Station|ja|長谷寺駅}}, {{ill|Kintetsu Osaka Line|ja|近鉄大阪線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yoki-tenman-jinja}} * Main Hall |location= [[Sakurai, Nara|桜井市]], [[Nara Prefecture|奈良県]], {{ill|Hase, Sakurai|ja|初瀬}} 14 |Latitude = 34 | LatMinutes = 32 | LatSeconds = 05.9 | DirectionN = N |Longitude = 135 | LongMinutes = 54 | LongSeconds = 41.6 | DirectionE = E |MapCode = JP |Map= Japan Nara |LabelPosition= left |deity= {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} |ShrineRank= Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |consecration_year= 946 CE (Tenkei 9) |MainHall= |Alternatename= |AnnualFestival= |Rituals= }} '''Yoki Tenman Shrine''' (與喜天満神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Hase, Sakurai|ja|初瀬}}, {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|ja|桜井市}}, [[Nara Prefecture|奈良県]]. Its former rank was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. == Deities == ; Main deity * [[Sugawara no Michizane]] (菅原道真) ; Additional Deities * [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]] (天照大神) * [[Ōkurahime no Kami]] (大倉姫神) == History == The shrine is located in {{ill|Hase, Sakurai|ja|初瀬}}, believed to be the birthplace of {{ill|Nomino Sukune|ja|野見宿禰}}, the ancestor of the {{ill|Haji clan|ja|土師氏}}, later known as the Sugawara Clan. This historical connection made the area significant for the Sugawara family.{{Cite web|url=https://sakurai-kankou.jimdo.com/%E5%90%8D%E6%89%80%E6%97%A7%E8%B7%A1/%E5%88%9D%E7%80%AC%E9%81%93%E5%91%A8%E8%BE%BA/%E8%88%87%E5%96%9C%E5%A4%A9%E6%BA%80%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/|title=Yoki Tenman Shrine (Sakurai, Hase)|publisher=Sakurai City Tourism Association|access-date=April 23, 2019}} According to the ''{{ill|Hasedera Genki|ja|長谷寺験記}}'', the shrine was founded in 946 CE (Tenkei 9) and later became venerated as the guardian shrine of Hase. == Sub-Shrines == * Taki-no-Kura Gongen Shrine (deity: [[Hayatama-no-Mikoto]], [[Izanagi]], [[Izanami]]) * Shiradaijin Shrine (deity: {{ill|Watarai Haruhiko|ja|度会春彦}}) * Sakuraha Shrine (deity: {{ill|Iyo Shinnō|ja|伊予親王|1=Iyo Shinnō}}) * Hachioji Shrine (deity: [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]) == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Wooden Seated Statue of Tenjin : Known as "Wrathful Tenjin," this seated statue in court attire is made of {{ill|yosegi-zukuri|ja|寄木造}} and features an intense expression. It bears an inscription from Shogen 1 (1259), making it the oldest surviving sculpture of Tenjin in Japan.{{Cite web|url=https://www.nara-np.co.jp/news/20120421085721.html|title=Important Cultural Properties Newly Designated: Tenjin Seated Statue (Yoki Tenman Shrine)|publisher=Nara Newspaper|access-date=April 23, 2019}} Within the statue's head is a mirror engraved with an image of {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|ja|十一面観音|Ekādaśamukha=Ekādaśamukha|lt=Jūichimen Kannon}}, symbolizing Tenjin's connection to the deity.{{Cite journal|title=New Cultural Property Designations|journal=Monthly Cultural Properties|issue=585|page=17|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|year=2012}} Previously enshrined in the main hall, it is now housed in the {{ill|Nara National Museum|ja|奈良国立博物館}}. === Additional Cultural Assets === [[File:Samurai O-Yoroi 2.jpg|thumb|200px|Reference: White-Threaded Yoroi (owned by {{ill|Hasedera|ja|長谷寺}}, displayed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}})]] Five sets of important cultural property armor (White-Threaded Yoroi, Red-Threaded Yoroi, Hawk Feather Yoroi, Three-Eyed Plate Yoroi, Blue-Laced Armor) were once associated with Yoki Tenman Shrine, which served as {{ill|Hasedera|ja|長谷寺}}'s {{ill|Chinjugami|ja|鎮守神}}. These were gifted by {{ill|Kitabatake Norikata|ja|北畠教謙}} in 1560 (Eiroku 3) for services rendered in battles against {{ill|Matsunaga Hisahide|ja|松永久秀}}.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/7051|title=White-Threaded Yoroi (Detailed Listing)|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|access-date=December 1, 2019}}{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/7047|title=Red-Threaded Yoroi (Detailed Listing)|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|access-date=December 1, 2019}} == Access == * Nearest station: 25 minutes on foot from {{ill|Hasedera Station|ja|長谷寺駅}}, {{ill|Kintetsu Osaka Line|ja|近鉄大阪線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yoki-tenman-jinja}} * Its former rank was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. == Deities == ; Main deity * [[Sugawara no Michizane]] (Sugawara no Michizane) ; Additional Deities * [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]] (天照大神) * [[Ōkurahime no Kami]] (大倉姫神) == History == The shrine is located in {{ill|Hase, Sakurai|ja|初瀬}}, believed to be the birthplace of {{ill|Nomino Sukune|ja|野見宿禰}}, the ancestor of the {{ill|Haji clan|ja|土師氏}}, later known as the Sugawara Clan. This historical connection made the area significant for the Sugawara family.{{Cite web|url=https://sakurai-kankou.jimdo.com/%E5%90%8D%E6%89%80%E6%97%A7%E8%B7%A1/%E5%88%9D%E7%80%AC%E9%81%93%E5%91%A8%E8%BE%BA/%E8%88%87%E5%96%9C%E5%A4%A9%E6%BA%80%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/|title=Yoki Tenman Shrine (Sakurai, Hase)|publisher=Sakurai City Tourism Association|access-date=April 23, 2019}} According to the ''{{ill|Hasedera Genki|ja|長谷寺験記}}'', the shrine was founded in 946 CE (Tenkei 9) and later became venerated as the guardian shrine of Hase. == Sub-Shrines == * Taki-no-Kura Gongen Shrine (deity: [[Hayatama-no-Mikoto]], [[Izanagi]], [[Izanami]]) * Shiradaijin Shrine (deity: {{ill|Watarai Haruhiko|ja|度会春彦}}) * Sakuraha Shrine (deity: {{ill|Iyo Shinnō|ja|伊予親王|1=Iyo Shinnō}}) * Hachioji Shrine (deity: [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]) == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Wooden Seated Statue of Tenjin : Known as "Wrathful Tenjin," this seated statue in court attire is made of {{ill|yosegi-zukuri|ja|寄木造}} and features an intense expression. It bears an inscription from Shogen 1 (1259), making it the oldest surviving sculpture of Tenjin in Japan.{{Cite web|url=https://www.nara-np.co.jp/news/20120421085721.html|title=Important Cultural Properties Newly Designated: Tenjin Seated Statue (Yoki Tenman Shrine)|publisher=Nara Newspaper|access-date=April 23, 2019}} Within the statue's head is a mirror engraved with an image of {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|ja|十一面観音|Ekādaśamukha=Ekādaśamukha|lt=Jūichimen Kannon}}, symbolizing Tenjin's connection to the deity.{{Cite journal|title=New Cultural Property Designations|journal=Monthly Cultural Properties|issue=585|page=17|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|year=2012}} Previously enshrined in the main hall, it is now housed in the {{ill|Nara National Museum|ja|奈良国立博物館}}. === Additional Cultural Assets === [[File:Samurai O-Yoroi 2.jpg|thumb|200px|Reference: White-Threaded Yoroi (owned by {{ill|Hasedera|ja|長谷寺}}, displayed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}})]] Five sets of important cultural property armor (White-Threaded Yoroi, Red-Threaded Yoroi, Hawk Feather Yoroi, Three-Eyed Plate Yoroi, Blue-Laced Armor) were once associated with Yoki Tenman Shrine, which served as {{ill|Hasedera|ja|長谷寺}}'s {{ill|Chinjugami|ja|鎮守神}}. These were gifted by {{ill|Kitabatake Norikata|ja|北畠教謙}} in 1560 (Eiroku 3) for services rendered in battles against {{ill|Matsunaga Hisahide|ja|松永久秀}}.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/7051|title=White-Threaded Yoroi (Detailed Listing)|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|access-date=December 1, 2019}}{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/7047|title=Red-Threaded Yoroi (Detailed Listing)|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|access-date=December 1, 2019}} == Access == * Nearest station: 25 minutes on foot from {{ill|Hasedera Station|ja|長谷寺駅}}, {{ill|Kintetsu Osaka Line|ja|近鉄大阪線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yoki-tenman-jinja}} * Its former rank was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. == Deities == ; Main deity * [[Sugawara no Michizane]] (Sugawara no Michizane) ; Additional Deities * [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]] (天照大神) * [[Ōkurahime no Kami]] (大倉姫神) == History == The shrine is located in {{ill|Hase, Sakurai|ja|初瀬}}, believed to be the birthplace of {{ill|Nomino Sukune|ja|野見宿禰}}, the ancestor of the {{ill|Haji clan|ja|土師氏}}, later known as the Sugawara Clan. This historical connection made the area significant for the Sugawara family.{{Cite web|url=https://sakurai-kankou.jimdo.com/%E5%90%8D%E6%89%80%E6%97%A7%E8%B7%A1/%E5%88%9D%E7%80%AC%E9%81%93%E5%91%A8%E8%BE%BA/%E8%88%87%E5%96%9C%E5%A4%A9%E6%BA%80%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/|title=Yoki Tenman Shrine (Sakurai, Hase)|publisher=Sakurai City Tourism Association|access-date=April 23, 2019}} According to the ''{{ill|Hasedera Genki|ja|長谷寺験記}}'', the shrine was founded in 946 CE (Tenkei 9) and later became venerated as the guardian shrine of Hase. == Sub-Shrines == * Taki-no-Kura Gongen Shrine (deity: [[Hayatama-no-Mikoto]], [[Izanagi]], [[Izanami]]) * Shiradaijin Shrine (deity: {{ill|Watarai Haruhiko|ja|度会春彦}}) * Sakuraha Shrine (deity: {{ill|Iyo Shinnō|ja|伊予親王|1=Iyo Shinnō}}) * Hachioji Shrine (deity: [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]) == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Wooden Seated Statue of Tenjin : Known as "Wrathful Tenjin," this seated statue in court attire is made of {{ill|yosegi-zukuri|ja|寄木造}} and features an intense expression. It bears an inscription from Shogen 1 (1259), making it the oldest surviving sculpture of Tenjin in Japan.{{Cite web|url=https://www.nara-np.co.jp/news/20120421085721.html|title=Important Cultural Properties Newly Designated: Tenjin Seated Statue (Yoki Tenman Shrine)|publisher=Nara Newspaper|access-date=April 23, 2019}} Within the statue's head is a mirror engraved with an image of {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|ja|十一面観音|Ekādaśamukha=Ekādaśamukha|lt=Jūichimen Kannon}}, symbolizing Tenjin's connection to the deity.{{Cite journal|title=New Cultural Property Designations|journal=Monthly Cultural Properties|issue=585|page=17|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|year=2012}} Previously enshrined in the main hall, it is now housed in the {{ill|Nara National Museum|ja|奈良国立博物館}}. === Additional Cultural Assets === [[File:Samurai O-Yoroi 2.jpg|thumb|200px|Reference: White-Threaded Yoroi (owned by {{ill|Hasedera|ja|長谷寺}}, displayed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}})]] Five sets of important cultural property armor (White-Threaded Yoroi, Red-Threaded Yoroi, Hawk Feather Yoroi, Three-Eyed Plate Yoroi, Blue-Laced Armor) were once associated with Yoki Tenman Shrine, which served as {{ill|Hasedera|ja|長谷寺}}'s {{ill|Chinjugami|ja|鎮守神}}. These were gifted by {{ill|Kitabatake Norikata|ja|北畠教謙}} in 1560 (Eiroku 3) for services rendered in battles against {{ill|Matsunaga Hisahide|ja|松永久秀}}.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/7051|title=White-Threaded Yoroi (Detailed Listing)|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|access-date=December 1, 2019}}{{Cite These were gifted by {{ill|Kitabatake Norikata|ja|北畠教謙}} in 1560 (Eiroku 3) for services rendered in battles against {{ill|Matsunaga Hisahide|ja|松永久秀}}.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/7051|title=White-Threaded Yoroi (Detailed Listing)|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|access-date=December 1, 2019}}{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/7047|title=Red-Threaded Yoroi (Detailed Listing)|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|access-date=December 1, 2019}} == Access == * Nearest station: 25 minutes on foot from {{ill|Hasedera Station|ja|長谷寺駅}}, {{ill|Kintetsu Osaka Line|ja|近鉄大阪線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yoki-tenman-jinja}} * {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Yoki Tenman Shrine (與喜天満神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Yoki-tenman-jinja honden.jpg|250px]]
Main Hall |location= [[Sakurai, Nara|桜井市]], [[Nara Prefecture|奈良県]], {{ill|Hase, Sakurai|ja|初瀬}} 14 |Latitude = 34 | LatMinutes = 32 | LatSeconds = 05.9 | DirectionN = N |Longitude = 135 | LongMinutes = 54 | LongSeconds = 41.6 | DirectionE = E |MapCode = JP |Map= Japan Nara |LabelPosition= left |deity= {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}} |ShrineRank= Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |consecration_year= 946 CE (Tenkei 9) |MainHall= |Alternatename= |AnnualFestival= |Rituals= }} '''Yoki Tenman Shrine''' (與喜天満神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Hase, Sakurai|ja|初瀬}}, {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|ja|桜井市}}, [[Nara Prefecture|奈良県]]. Its former rank was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. == Deities == ; Main deity * [[Sugawara no Michizane]] (Sugawara no Michizane) ; Additional Deities * [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]] (天照大神) * [[Ōkurahime no Kami]] (大倉姫神) == History == The shrine is located in {{ill|Hase, Sakurai|ja|初瀬}}, believed to be the birthplace of {{ill|Nomino Sukune|ja|野見宿禰}}, the ancestor of the {{ill|Haji clan|ja|土師氏}}, later known as the Sugawara Clan. This historical connection made the area significant for the Sugawara family.{{Cite web|url=https://sakurai-kankou.jimdo.com/%E5%90%8D%E6%89%80%E6%97%A7%E8%B7%A1/%E5%88%9D%E7%80%AC%E9%81%93%E5%91%A8%E8%BE%BA/%E8%88%87%E5%96%9C%E5%A4%A9%E6%BA%80%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/|title=Yoki Tenman Shrine (Sakurai, Hase)|publisher=Sakurai City Tourism Association|access-date=April 23, 2019}} According to the ''{{ill|Hasedera Genki|ja|長谷寺験記}}'', the shrine was founded in 946 CE (Tenkei 9) and later became venerated as the guardian shrine of Hase. == Sub-Shrines == * Taki-no-Kura Gongen Shrine (deity: [[Hayatama-no-Mikoto]], [[Izanagi]], [[Izanami]]) * Shiradaijin Shrine (deity: {{ill|Watarai Haruhiko|ja|度会春彦}}) * Sakuraha Shrine (deity: {{ill|Iyo Shinnō|ja|伊予親王|1=Iyo Shinnō}}) * Hachioji Shrine (deity: [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]) == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Wooden Seated Statue of Tenjin : Known as "Wrathful Tenjin," this seated statue in court attire is made of {{ill|yosegi-zukuri|ja|寄木造}} and features an intense expression. It bears an inscription from Shogen 1 (1259), making it the oldest surviving sculpture of Tenjin in Japan.{{Cite web|url=https://www.nara-np.co.jp/news/20120421085721.html|title=Important Cultural Properties Newly Designated: Tenjin Seated Statue (Yoki Tenman Shrine)|publisher=Nara Newspaper|access-date=April 23, 2019}} Within the statue's head is a mirror engraved with an image of {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|ja|十一面観音|Ekādaśamukha=Ekādaśamukha|lt=Jūichimen Kannon}}, symbolizing Tenjin's connection to the deity.{{Cite journal|title=New Cultural Property Designations|journal=Monthly Cultural Properties|issue=585|page=17|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|year=2012}} Previously enshrined in the main hall, it is now housed in the {{ill|Nara National Museum|ja|奈良国立博物館}}. === Additional Cultural Assets === [[File:Samurai O-Yoroi 2.jpg|thumb|200px|Reference: White-Threaded Yoroi (owned by {{ill|Hasedera|ja|長谷寺}}, displayed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}})]] Five sets of important cultural property armor (White-Threaded Yoroi, Red-Threaded Yoroi, Hawk Feather Yoroi, Three-Eyed Plate Yoroi, Blue-Laced Armor) were once associated with Yoki Tenman Shrine, which served as {{ill|Hasedera|ja|長谷寺}}'s {{ill|Chinjugami|ja|鎮守神}}. These were gifted by {{ill|Kitabatake Norikata|ja|北畠教謙}} in 1560 (Eiroku 3) for services rendered in battles against {{ill|Matsunaga Hisahide|ja|松永久秀}}.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/7051|title=White-Threaded Yoroi (Detailed Listing)|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|access-date=December 1, 2019}}{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/7047|title=Red-Threaded Yoroi (Detailed Listing)|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|access-date=December 1, 2019}} == Access == * Nearest station: 25 minutes on foot from {{ill|Hasedera Station|ja|長谷寺駅}}, {{ill|Kintetsu Osaka Line|ja|近鉄大阪線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yoki-tenman-jinja}} *
