{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Yoki Tenman Shrine (與喜天満神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Yoki-tenman-jinja honden.jpg|250px]]
Main Hall |location= [[Sakurai, Nara|桜井市]], [[Nara Prefecture|奈良県]], {{ill|Hase, Sakurai|ja|初瀬}} 14 |Latitude = 34 | LatMinutes = 32 | LatSeconds = 05.9 | DirectionN = N |Longitude = 135 | LongMinutes = 54 | LongSeconds = 41.6 | DirectionE = E |MapCode = JP |Map= Japan Nara |LabelPosition= left |deity= {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}} |ShrineRank= Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |consecration_year= 946 CE (Tenkei 9) |MainHall= |Alternatename= |AnnualFestival= |Rituals= }} '''Yoki Tenman Shrine''' (與喜天満神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Hase, Sakurai|ja|初瀬}}, {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|ja|桜井市}}, [[Nara Prefecture|奈良県]]. Its former rank was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. == Deities == ; Main deity * [[Sugawara no Michizane]] (Sugawara no Michizane) ; Additional Deities * [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]] (天照大神) * [[Ōkurahime no Kami]] (大倉姫神) == History == The shrine is located in {{ill|Hase, Sakurai|ja|初瀬}}, believed to be the birthplace of {{ill|Nomino Sukune|ja|野見宿禰}}, the ancestor of the {{ill|Haji clan|ja|土師氏}}, later known as the Sugawara Clan. This historical connection made the area significant for the Sugawara family.{{Cite web|url=https://sakurai-kankou.jimdo.com/%E5%90%8D%E6%89%80%E6%97%A7%E8%B7%A1/%E5%88%9D%E7%80%AC%E9%81%93%E5%91%A8%E8%BE%BA/%E8%88%87%E5%96%9C%E5%A4%A9%E6%BA%80%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/|title=Yoki Tenman Shrine (Sakurai, Hase)|publisher=Sakurai City Tourism Association|access-date=April 23, 2019}} According to the ''{{ill|Hasedera Genki|ja|長谷寺験記}}'', the shrine was founded in 946 CE (Tenkei 9) and later became venerated as the guardian shrine of Hase. == Sub-Shrines == * Taki-no-Kura Gongen Shrine (deity: [[Hayatama-no-Mikoto]], [[Izanagi]], [[Izanami]]) * Shiradaijin Shrine (deity: {{ill|Watarai Haruhiko|ja|度会春彦}}) * Sakuraha Shrine (deity: {{ill|Iyo Shinnō|ja|伊予親王|1=Iyo Shinnō}}) * Hachioji Shrine (deity: [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]) == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Wooden Seated Statue of Tenjin : Known as "Wrathful Tenjin," this seated statue in court attire is made of {{ill|yosegi-zukuri|ja|寄木造}} and features an intense expression. It bears an inscription from Shogen 1 (1259), making it the oldest surviving sculpture of Tenjin in Japan.{{Cite web|url=https://www.nara-np.co.jp/news/20120421085721.html|title=Important Cultural Properties Newly Designated: Tenjin Seated Statue (Yoki Tenman Shrine)|publisher=Nara Newspaper|access-date=April 23, 2019}} Within the statue's head is a mirror engraved with an image of {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|ja|十一面観音|Ekādaśamukha=Ekādaśamukha|lt=Jūichimen Kannon}}, symbolizing Tenjin's connection to the deity.{{Cite journal|title=New Cultural Property Designations|journal=Monthly Cultural Properties|issue=585|page=17|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|year=2012}} Previously enshrined in the main hall, it is now housed in the {{ill|Nara National Museum|ja|奈良国立博物館}}. === Additional Cultural Assets === [[File:Samurai O-Yoroi 2.jpg|thumb|200px|Reference: White-Threaded Yoroi (owned by {{ill|Hasedera|ja|長谷寺}}, displayed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}})]] Five sets of important cultural property armor (White-Threaded Yoroi, Red-Threaded Yoroi, Hawk Feather Yoroi, Three-Eyed Plate Yoroi, Blue-Laced Armor) were once associated with Yoki Tenman Shrine, which served as {{ill|Hasedera|ja|長谷寺}}'s {{ill|Chinjugami|ja|鎮守神}}. These were gifted by {{ill|Kitabatake Norikata|ja|北畠教謙}} in 1560 (Eiroku 3) for services rendered in battles against {{ill|Matsunaga Hisahide|ja|松永久秀}}.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/7051|title=White-Threaded Yoroi (Detailed Listing)|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|access-date=December 1, 2019}}{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/7047|title=Red-Threaded Yoroi (Detailed Listing)|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|access-date=December 1, 2019}} == Access == * Nearest station: 25 minutes on foot from {{ill|Hasedera Station|ja|長谷寺駅}}, {{ill|Kintetsu Osaka Line|ja|近鉄大阪線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yoki-tenman-jinja}} * revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 與喜天満神社 |画像 = [[File:Yoki-tenman-jinja honden.jpg|250px]]
本殿 |所在地 = [[奈良県]][[桜井市]][[初瀬]]14 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 32 | 緯度秒 = 05.9 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 54 | 経度秒 = 41.6 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP | 地図 = Japan Nara | ラベル位置 = left |祭神 = [[菅原道真]] |社格 = 旧[[郷社]] |創建 = 946年（天慶9年） |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = |神事 = }} '''與喜天満神社'''（よきてんまんじんじゃ）は、[[奈良県]][[桜井市]][[初瀬]]にある[[神社]]。旧社格は[[郷社]]。 ==祭神== ;主祭神 *[[菅原道真]]（すがわら の みちざね） ;配祀神 *[[天照大神]]（あまてらすおおかみ） *[[大倉姫神]]（おおくらひめのかみ） == 歴史 == 神社の所在地にある[[初瀬]]は、道真の先祖にあたる[[野見宿禰]]の出身地とされており、次いで[[土師氏]]となり、菅原氏にとってゆかりある土地であったとされている[https://sakurai-kankou.jimdo.com/%E5%90%8D%E6%89%80%E6%97%A7%E8%B7%A1/%E5%88%9D%E7%80%AC%E9%81%93%E5%91%A8%E8%BE%BA/%E8%88%87%E5%96%9C%E5%A4%A9%E6%BA%80%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/　與喜天満神社（桜井市 初瀬） - 一般社団法人 桜井市観光協会]2019年4月23日　閲覧。『[[長谷寺験記]]』によると、創建は[[946年]]（[[天慶]]9年）とされており、後に初瀬の産土神としても信仰されるようになった。 == 摂末社 == *瀧蔵権現社 （祭神：[[速玉命]]、[[伊弉諾尊]]、[[伊弉冉尊]]） *白太夫社（祭神：[[白太夫|度会春彦]]） *櫻葉社 （祭神：[[伊予親王]]） *八王子社 （祭神：[[天照大神]]、[[素戔鳴尊]]） == 文化財 == === 重要文化財 === * 木造天神坐像 : 衣冠束帯姿に「怒り天神」とも呼ばれる引き締めたような表情をした[[寄木造]]の坐像であり、像内に「正元元年」(1259年)の銘があることから、現存する国内最古の天神像の彫刻とされている[https://www.nara-np.co.jp/news/20120421085721.html　重文、新たに2件 - 弥勒仏坐像（弥勒寺）、天神坐像（與喜天満） - 奈良新聞]2019年4月23日　閲覧。像の頭部内に十一面観音鏡像（鏡面に[[十一面観音]]像を刻んだ銅鏡）を納めており、天神の本地が十一面観音であることを示している文化庁文化財部「新指定の文化財」『月刊文化財』585号、p.17、第一法規、2012。かつては本殿にて人目に触れぬよう祀られていたが、現在は[[奈良国立博物館]]に寄託されている。 === その他 === [[File:Samurai O-Yoroi 2.jpg|thumb|200px|（参考画像）白糸威鎧（長谷寺所蔵、[[東京国立博物館]]にて展示）。当初は與喜天満神社に伝来した。]] [[長谷寺]]が所蔵する重要文化財の甲冑5点（白糸威鎧（大袖付）・赤糸威鎧（大袖付）・鷹羽威鎧（大袖付）・三目札鎧・藍韋威肩赤大袖）は、かつては同寺の[[鎮守神|鎮守社]]であった與喜天満神社に伝来しており、[[1560年]]（[[永禄]]3年）に北畠教謙が廊坊宗賢へ、[[松永久秀]]との合戦における戦功を賞して自分の鎧を与えたものを[[1592年]]（[[天正]]20年）に同社へ奉納したとされる{{国指定文化財等データベース|201|7051|白糸威鎧〈大袖付／〉}}、2019年12月1日閲覧。{{国指定文化財等データベース|201|7047|赤糸威鎧〈大袖付／〉}}、2019年12月1日閲覧。{{国指定文化財等データベース|201|7050|鷹羽威鎧〈大袖付／〉}}、2019年12月1日閲覧。{{国指定文化財等データベース|201|7049|三目札鎧}}、2019年12月1日閲覧。{{国指定文化財等データベース|201|7048|藍韋威肩赤大袖}}、2019年12月1日閲覧。。 == 現地情報 == ;交通アクセス * 最寄駅：[[近鉄大阪線]][[長谷寺駅]]下車後、徒歩約25分 == 脚注 == === 出典 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} ==外部リンク== {{Commonscat|Yoki-tenman-jinja}} *[https://www.yokitenmanjinja.jp/ 與喜天満神社（公式サイト）] {{菅公聖蹟二十五拝}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:よきてんまんしんしや}} [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧郷社]] [[Category:奈良県の重要文化財]] [[Category:桜井市の歴史]] [[Category:桜井市の建築物]] [[Category:天満宮]]

弥勒仏坐像（弥勒寺）、天神坐像（與喜天満） - 奈良新聞]2019年4月23日　閲覧。像の頭部内に十一面観音鏡像（鏡面に[[十一面観音]]像を刻んだ銅鏡）を納めており、天神の本地が十一面観音であることを示している文化庁文化財部「新指定の文化財」『月刊文化財』585号、p.17、第一法規、2012。かつては本殿にて人目に触れぬよう祀られていたが、現在は[[奈良国立博物館]]に寄託されている。 === その他 === [[File:Samurai O-Yoroi 2.jpg|thumb|200px|（参考画像）白糸威鎧（長谷寺所蔵、[[東京国立博物館]]にて展示）。当初は與喜天満神社に伝来した。]] [[長谷寺]]が所蔵する重要文化財の甲冑5点（白糸威鎧（大袖付）・赤糸威鎧（大袖付）・鷹羽威鎧（大袖付）・三目札鎧・藍韋威肩赤大袖）は、かつては同寺の[[鎮守神|鎮守社]]であった與喜天満神社に伝来しており、[[1560年]]（[[永禄]]3年）に北畠教謙が廊坊宗賢へ、[[松永久秀]]との合戦における戦功を賞して自分の鎧を与えたものを[[1592年]]（[[天正]]20年）に同社へ奉納したとされる{{国指定文化財等データベース|201|7051|白糸威鎧〈大袖付／〉}}、2019年12月1日閲覧。{{国指定文化財等データベース|201|7047|赤糸威鎧〈大袖付／〉}}、2019年12月1日閲覧。{{国指定文化財等データベース|201|7050|鷹羽威鎧〈大袖付／〉}}、2019年12月1日閲覧。{{国指定文化財等データベース|201|7049|三目札鎧}}、2019年12月1日閲覧。{{国指定文化財等データベース|201|7048|藍韋威肩赤大袖}}、2019年12月1日閲覧。。 == 現地情報 == ;交通アクセス * 最寄駅：[[近鉄大阪線]][[長谷寺駅]]下車後、徒歩約25分 == 脚注 == === 出典 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} ==外部リンク== {{Commonscat|Yoki-tenman-jinja}} *[https://www.yokitenmanjinja.jp/ 與喜天満神社（公式サイト）] {{菅公聖蹟二十五拝}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:よきてんまんしんしや}} [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧郷社]] [[Category:奈良県の重要文化財]] [[Category:桜井市の歴史]] [[Category:桜井市の建築物]] [[Category:天満宮]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Yoki Tenman Shrine (與喜天満神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Yoki-tenman-jinja honden.jpg|250px]]
Main Hall |location= [[Sakurai, Nara|桜井市]], [[Nara Prefecture|奈良県]], {{ill|Hase, Sakurai|ja|初瀬|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 14 |Latitude = 34 | LatMinutes = 32 | LatSeconds = 05.9 | DirectionN = N |Longitude = 135 | LongMinutes = 54 | LongSeconds = 41.6 | DirectionE = E |MapCode = JP |Map= Japan Nara |LabelPosition= left |deity= {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |ShrineRank= Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |consecration_year= 946 CE (Tenkei 9) |MainHall= |Alternatename= |AnnualFestival= |Rituals= }} '''Yoki Tenman Shrine''' (與喜天満神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Hase, Sakurai|ja|初瀬|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|ja|桜井市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, [[Nara Prefecture|奈良県]]. Its former Rank was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Deities == ; Main deity * [[Sugawara no Michizane]] (Sugawara no Michizane) ; Additional Deities * [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]] (天照大神) * [[Ōkurahime no Kami]] (大倉姫神) == History == The shrine is located in {{ill|Hase, Sakurai|ja|初瀬|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, believed to be the birthplace of {{ill|Nomino Sukune|ja|野見宿禰|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the ancestor of the {{ill|Haji clan|ja|土師氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, later known as the Sugawara Clan. This historical connection made the area significant for the Sugawara family.{{Cite web|url=https://sakurai-kankou.jimdo.com/%E5%90%8D%E6%89%80%E6%97%A7%E8%B7%A1/%E5%88%9D%E7%80%AC%E9%81%93%E5%91%A8%E8%BE%BA/%E8%88%87%E5%96%9C%E5%A4%A9%E6%BA%80%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/|title=Yoki Tenman Shrine (Sakurai, Hase)|publisher=Sakurai City Tourism Association|access-date=April 23, 2019}} According to the ''{{ill|Hasedera Genki|ja|長谷寺験記|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'', the shrine was founded in 946 CE (Tenkei 9) and later became venerated as the guardian shrine of Hase. == Sub-Shrines == * Taki-no-Kura Gongen Shrine (deity: [[Hayatama-no-Mikoto]], [[Izanagi]], [[Izanami]]) * Shiradaijin Shrine (deity: {{ill|Watarai Haruhiko|ja|度会春彦|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) * Sakuraha Shrine (deity: {{ill|Iyo Shinnō|ja|伊予親王|1=Iyo Shinnō|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) * Hachioji Shrine (deity: [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]) == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Wooden Seated Statue of Tenjin : Known as "Wrathful Tenjin," this seated statue in court attire is made of {{ill|yosegi-zukuri|ja|寄木造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and features an intense expression. It bears an inscription from Shogen 1 (1259), making it the oldest surviving sculpture of Tenjin in Japan.{{Cite web|url=https://www.nara-np.co.jp/news/20120421085721.html|title=Important Cultural Properties Newly Designated: Tenjin Seated Statue (Yoki Tenman Shrine)|publisher=Nara Newspaper|access-date=April 23, 2019}} Within the statue's head is a mirror engraved with an image of {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|ja|十一面観音|Ekādaśamukha=Ekādaśamukha|lt=Jūichimen Kannon|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, symbolizing Tenjin's connection to the deity.{{Cite journal|title=New Cultural Property Designations|journal=Monthly Cultural Properties|issue=585|page=17|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|year=2012}} Previously enshrined in the main hall, it is now housed in the {{ill|Nara National Museum|ja|奈良国立博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. === Additional Cultural Assets === [[File:Samurai O-Yoroi 2.jpg|thumb|200px|Reference: White-Threaded Yoroi (owned by {{ill|Hasedera|ja|長谷寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, displayed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}})]] Five sets of important cultural property armor (White-Threaded Yoroi, Red-Threaded Yoroi, Hawk Feather Yoroi, Three-Eyed Plate Yoroi, Blue-Laced Armor) were once associated with Yoki Tenman Shrine, which served as {{ill|Hasedera|ja|長谷寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'s {{ill|Chinjugami|ja|鎮守神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. These were gifted by {{ill|Kitabatake Norikata|ja|北畠教謙|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in 1560 (Eiroku 3) for services rendered in battles against {{ill|Matsunaga Hisahide|ja|松永久秀|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/7051|title=White-Threaded Yoroi (Detailed Listing)|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|access-date=December 1, 2019}}{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/7047|title=Red-Threaded Yoroi (Detailed Listing)|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|access-date=December 1, 2019}} == Access == * Nearest station: 25 minutes on foot from {{ill|Hasedera Station|ja|長谷寺駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kintetsu Osaka Line|ja|近鉄大阪線|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yoki-tenman-jinja}} * [https://www.yokitenmanjinja.jp/ Official Website of Yoki Tenman Shrine] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yoki Tenman Shrine}} {{translated page|ja|與喜天満神社|version=78784964|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Yoki Tenman Shrine]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gōsha shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Sakurai, Nara]] [[Category:Tenman-gū]] [[Category:Yoiki Tenma Shrine]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Yoki Tenman Shrine]] [[en:Yoki Tenman Shrine]] [[sn:oki Tenman Shrine]] [[ja:與喜天満神社]]

Its former Rank was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Deities == ; Main deity * [[Sugawara no Michizane]] (Sugawara no Michizane) ; Additional Deities * [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]] (天照大神) * [[Ōkurahime no Kami]] (大倉姫神) == History == The shrine is located in {{ill|Hase, Sakurai|ja|初瀬|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, believed to be the birthplace of {{ill|Nomino Sukune|ja|野見宿禰|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the ancestor of the {{ill|Haji clan|ja|土師氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, later known as the Sugawara Clan. This historical connection made the area significant for the Sugawara family.{{Cite web|url=https://sakurai-kankou.jimdo.com/%E5%90%8D%E6%89%80%E6%97%A7%E8%B7%A1/%E5%88%9D%E7%80%AC%E9%81%93%E5%91%A8%E8%BE%BA/%E8%88%87%E5%96%9C%E5%A4%A9%E6%BA%80%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/|title=Yoki Tenman Shrine (Sakurai, Hase)|publisher=Sakurai City Tourism Association|access-date=April 23, 2019}} According to the ''{{ill|Hasedera Genki|ja|長谷寺験記|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'', the shrine was founded in 946 CE (Tenkei 9) and later became venerated as the guardian shrine of Hase. == Sub-Shrines == * Taki-no-Kura Gongen Shrine (deity: [[Hayatama-no-Mikoto]], [[Izanagi]], [[Izanami]]) * Shiradaijin Shrine (deity: {{ill|Watarai Haruhiko|ja|度会春彦|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) * Sakuraha Shrine (deity: {{ill|Iyo Shinnō|ja|伊予親王|1=Iyo Shinnō|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) * Hachioji Shrine (deity: [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]) == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Wooden Seated Statue of Tenjin : Known as "Wrathful Tenjin," this seated statue in court attire is made of {{ill|yosegi-zukuri|ja|寄木造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and features an intense expression. It bears an inscription from Shogen 1 (1259), making it the oldest surviving sculpture of Tenjin in Japan.{{Cite web|url=https://www.nara-np.co.jp/news/20120421085721.html|title=Important Cultural Properties Newly Designated: Tenjin Seated Statue (Yoki Tenman Shrine)|publisher=Nara Newspaper|access-date=April 23, 2019}} Within the statue's head is a mirror engraved with an image of {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|ja|十一面観音|Ekādaśamukha=Ekādaśamukha|lt=Jūichimen Kannon|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, symbolizing Tenjin's connection to the deity.{{Cite journal|title=New Cultural Property Designations|journal=Monthly Cultural Properties|issue=585|page=17|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|year=2012}} Previously enshrined in the main hall, it is now housed in the {{ill|Nara National Museum|ja|奈良国立博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. === Additional Cultural Assets === [[File:Samurai O-Yoroi 2.jpg|thumb|200px|Reference: White-Threaded Yoroi (owned by {{ill|Hasedera|ja|長谷寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, displayed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}})]] Five sets of important cultural property armor (White-Threaded Yoroi, Red-Threaded Yoroi, Hawk Feather Yoroi, Three-Eyed Plate Yoroi, Blue-Laced Armor) were once associated with Yoki Tenman Shrine, which served as {{ill|Hasedera|ja|長谷寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'s {{ill|Chinjugami|ja|鎮守神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. These were gifted by {{ill|Kitabatake Norikata|ja|北畠教謙|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in 1560 (Eiroku 3) for services rendered in battles against {{ill|Matsunaga Hisahide|ja|松永久秀|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/7051|title=White-Threaded Yoroi (Detailed Listing)|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|access-date=December 1, 2019}}{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/7047|title=Red-Threaded Yoroi (Detailed Listing)|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|access-date=December 1, 2019}} == Access == * Nearest station: 25 minutes on foot from {{ill|Hasedera Station|ja|長谷寺駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kintetsu Osaka Line|ja|近鉄大阪線|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yoki-tenman-jinja}} * [https://www.yokitenmanjinja.jp/ Official Website of Yoki Tenman Shrine] {{translated page|ja|與喜天満神社|version=78784964|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Yoki Tenman Shrine]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gōsha shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Sakurai, Nara]] [[Category:Tenman-gū]] [[Category:Yoiki Tenma Shrine]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Yoki Tenman Shrine]] [[en:Yoki Tenman Shrine]] [[sn:oki Tenman Shrine]] [[ja:與喜天満神社]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Yoki Tenman Shrine (與喜天満神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Yoki-tenman-jinja honden.jpg|250px]]
Main Hall |location= [[Sakurai, Nara|桜井市]], [[Nara Prefecture|奈良県]], {{ill|Hase, Sakurai|ja|初瀬|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 14 |Latitude = 34 | LatMinutes = 32 | LatSeconds = 05.9 | DirectionN = N |Longitude = 135 | LongMinutes = 54 | LongSeconds = 41.6 | DirectionE = E |MapCode = JP |Map= Japan Nara |LabelPosition= left |deity= {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |ShrineRank= Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |consecration_year= 946 CE (Tenkei 9) |MainHall= |Alternatename= |AnnualFestival= |Rituals= }} '''Yoki Tenman Shrine''' (與喜天満神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Hase, Sakurai|ja|初瀬|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|ja|桜井市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, [[Nara Prefecture|奈良県]]. Its former Rank was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Deities == ; Main deity * [[Sugawara no Michizane]] (Sugawara no Michizane) ; Additional Deities * [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]] (天照大神) * [[Ōkurahime no Kami]] (大倉姫神) == History == The shrine is located in {{ill|Hase, Sakurai|ja|初瀬|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, believed to be the birthplace of {{ill|Nomino Sukune|ja|野見宿禰|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the ancestor of the {{ill|Haji clan|ja|土師氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, later known as the Sugawara Clan. This historical connection made the area significant for the Sugawara family.{{Cite web|url=https://sakurai-kankou.jimdo.com/%E5%90%8D%E6%89%80%E6%97%A7%E8%B7%A1/%E5%88%9D%E7%80%AC%E9%81%93%E5%91%A8%E8%BE%BA/%E8%88%87%E5%96%9C%E5%A4%A9%E6%BA%80%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/|title=Yoki Tenman Shrine (Sakurai, Hase)|publisher=Sakurai City Tourism Association|access-date=April 23, 2019}} According to the ''{{ill|Hasedera Genki|ja|長谷寺験記|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'', the shrine was founded in 946 CE (Tenkei 9) and later became venerated as the guardian shrine of Hase. == Sub-Shrines == * Taki-no-Kura Gongen Shrine (deity: [[Hayatama-no-Mikoto]], [[Izanagi]], [[Izanami]]) * Shiradaijin Shrine (deity: {{ill|Watarai Haruhiko|ja|度会春彦|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) * Sakuraha Shrine (deity: {{ill|Iyo Shinnō|ja|伊予親王|1=Iyo Shinnō|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) * Hachioji Shrine (deity: [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]) == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Wooden Seated Statue of Tenjin : Known as "Wrathful Tenjin," this seated statue in court attire is made of {{ill|yosegi-zukuri|ja|寄木造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and features an intense expression. It bears an inscription from Shogen 1 (1259), making it the oldest surviving sculpture of Tenjin in Japan.{{Cite web|url=https://www.nara-np.co.jp/news/20120421085721.html|title=Important Cultural Properties Newly Designated: Tenjin Seated Statue (Yoki Tenman Shrine)|publisher=Nara Newspaper|access-date=April 23, 2019}} Within the statue's head is a mirror engraved with an image of {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|ja|十一面観音|Ekādaśamukha=Ekādaśamukha|lt=Jūichimen Kannon|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, symbolizing Tenjin's connection to the deity.{{Cite journal|title=New Cultural Property Designations|journal=Monthly Cultural Properties|issue=585|page=17|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|year=2012}} Previously enshrined in the main hall, it is now housed in the {{ill|Nara National Museum|ja|奈良国立博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. === Additional Cultural Assets === [[File:Samurai O-Yoroi 2.jpg|thumb|200px|Reference: White-Threaded Yoroi (owned by {{ill|Hasedera|ja|長谷寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, displayed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}})]] Five sets of important cultural property armor (White-Threaded Yoroi, Red-Threaded Yoroi, Hawk Feather Yoroi, Three-Eyed Plate Yoroi, Blue-Laced Armor) were once associated with Yoki Tenman Shrine, which served as {{ill|Hasedera|ja|長谷寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'s {{ill|Chinjugami|ja|鎮守神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. These were gifted by {{ill|Kitabatake Norikata|ja|北畠教謙|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in 1560 (Eiroku 3) for services rendered in battles against {{ill|Matsunaga Hisahide|ja|松永久秀|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/7051|title=White-Threaded Yoroi (Detailed Listing)|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|access-date=December 1, 2019}}{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/7047|title=Red-Threaded Yoroi (Detailed Listing)|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|access-date=December 1, 2019}} == Access == * Nearest station: 25 minutes on foot from {{ill|Hasedera Station|ja|長谷寺駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kintetsu Osaka Line|ja|近鉄大阪線|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yoki-tenman-jinja}} * [https://www.yokitenmanjinja.jp/ Official Website of Yoki Tenman Shrine] {{translated page|ja|與喜天満神社|version=78784964|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Yoki Tenman Shrine]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Sakurai, Nara]] [[Category:Tenman-gū]] [[Category:Yoiki Tenma Shrine]] }} [[sn:Yoki Tenman Shrine]] [[en:Yoki Tenman Shrine]] [[sn:oki Tenman Shrine]] [[ja:與喜天満神社]]

Main Hall |location= [[Sakurai, Nara|桜井市]], [[Nara Prefecture|奈良県]], {{ill|Hase, Sakurai|ja|初瀬|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hase, Sakurai|qq=}} 14 |Latitude = 34 | LatMinutes = 32 | LatSeconds = 05.9 | DirectionN = N |Longitude = 135 | LongMinutes = 54 | LongSeconds = 41.6 | DirectionE = E |MapCode = JP |Map= Japan Nara |LabelPosition= left |deity= {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=}} |ShrineRank= Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}} |consecration_year= 946 CE (Tenkei 9) |MainHall= |Alternatename= |AnnualFestival= |Rituals= }} '''Yoki Tenman Shrine''' (與喜天満神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Hase, Sakurai|ja|初瀬|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hase, Sakurai|qq=}}, {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|ja|桜井市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai, Nara|qq=}}, [[Nara Prefecture|奈良県]]. Its former Rank was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}}. == Deities == ; Main deity * [[Sugawara no Michizane]] (Sugawara no Michizane) ; Additional Deities * [[Amaterasu]] (天照大神) * [[Ōkurahime no Kami]] (大倉姫神) == History == The shrine is located in {{ill|Hase, Sakurai|ja|初瀬|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hase, Sakurai|qq=}}, believed to be the birthplace of {{ill|Nomino Sukune|ja|野見宿禰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nomino Sukune|qq=}}, the ancestor of the {{ill|Haji clan|ja|土師氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haji clan|qq=}}, later known as the Sugawara Clan. This historical connection made the area significant for the Sugawara family.{{Cite web|url=https://sakurai-kankou.jimdo.com/%E5%90%8D%E6%89%80%E6%97%A7%E8%B7%A1/%E5%88%9D%E7%80%AC%E9%81%93%E5%91%A8%E8%BE%BA/%E8%88%87%E5%96%9C%E5%A4%A9%E6%BA%80%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/|title=Yoki Tenman Shrine (Sakurai, Hase)|publisher=Sakurai City Tourism Association|access-date=April 23, 2019}} According to the ''{{ill|Hasedera Genki|ja|長谷寺験記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasedera Genki|qq=}}'', the shrine was founded in 946 CE (Tenkei 9) and later became venerated as the guardian shrine of Hase. == Sub-Shrines == * Taki-no-Kura Gongen Shrine (deity: [[Hayatama-no-Mikoto]], [[Izanagi]], [[Izanami]]) * Shiradaijin Shrine (deity: {{ill|Watarai Haruhiko|ja|度会春彦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai Haruhiko|qq=}}) * Sakuraha Shrine (deity: {{ill|Iyo Shinnō|ja|伊予親王|1=Iyo Shinnō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Shinnō|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Shinnō|qq=}}) * Hachioji Shrine (deity: [[Amaterasu]], [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]) == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Wooden Seated Statue of Tenjin : Known as "Wrathful Tenjin," this seated statue in court attire is made of {{ill|yosegi-zukuri|ja|寄木造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yosegi-zukuri|qq=}} and features an intense expression. It bears an inscription from Shogen 1 (1259), making it the oldest surviving sculpture of Tenjin in Japan.{{Cite web|url=https://www.nara-np.co.jp/news/20120421085721.html|title=Important Cultural Properties Newly Designated: Tenjin Seated Statue (Yoki Tenman Shrine)|publisher=Nara Newspaper|access-date=April 23, 2019}} Within the statue's head is a mirror engraved with an image of {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|ja|十一面観音|Ekādaśamukha=Ekādaśamukha|lt=Jūichimen Kannon|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ekādaśamukha|qq=}}, symbolizing Tenjin's connection to the deity.{{Cite journal|title=New Cultural Property Designations|journal=Monthly Cultural Properties|issue=585|page=17|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|year=2012}} Previously enshrined in the main hall, it is now housed in the {{ill|Nara National Museum|ja|奈良国立博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara National Museum|qq=}}. === Additional Cultural Assets === [[File:Samurai O-Yoroi 2.jpg|thumb|200px|Reference: White-Threaded Yoroi (owned by {{ill|Hasedera|ja|長谷寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasedera|qq=}}, displayed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo National Museum|qq=}})]] Five sets of important cultural property armor (White-Threaded Yoroi, Red-Threaded Yoroi, Hawk Feather Yoroi, Three-Eyed Plate Yoroi, Blue-Laced Armor) were once associated with Yoki Tenman Shrine, which served as {{ill|Hasedera|ja|長谷寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasedera|qq=}}'s {{ill|Chinjugami|ja|鎮守神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinjugami|qq=}}. These were gifted by {{ill|Kitabatake Norikata|ja|北畠教謙|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitabatake Norikata|qq=}} in 1560 (Eiroku 3) for services rendered in battles against {{ill|Matsunaga Hisahide|ja|松永久秀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsunaga Hisahide|qq=}}.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/7051|title=White-Threaded Yoroi (Detailed Listing)|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|access-date=December 1, 2019}}{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/7047|title=Red-Threaded Yoroi (Detailed Listing)|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|access-date=December 1, 2019}} == Access == * Nearest station: 25 minutes on foot from {{ill|Hasedera Station|ja|長谷寺駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasedera Station|qq=}}, {{ill|Kintetsu Osaka Line|ja|近鉄大阪線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Osaka Line|qq=}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yoki-tenman-jinja}} * [https://www.yokitenmanjinja.jp/ Official Website of Yoki Tenman Shrine] {{translated page|ja|與喜天満神社|version=78784964|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Yoki Tenman Shrine]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Sakurai, Nara]] [[Category:Tenman-gū]] [[Category:Yoiki Tenma Shrine]] }} [[sn:Yoki Tenman Shrine]] [[en:Yoki Tenman Shrine]] [[sn:oki Tenman Shrine]] [[ja:與喜天満神社]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Yoki Tenman Shrine (與喜天満神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Yoki-tenman-jinja honden.jpg|250px]]
Main Hall |location= [[Sakurai, Nara|桜井市]], [[Nara Prefecture|奈良県]], {{ill|Hase, Sakurai|ja|初瀬|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hase, Sakurai|qq=}} 14 |Latitude = 34 | LatMinutes = 32 | LatSeconds = 05.9 | DirectionN = N |Longitude = 135 | LongMinutes = 54 | LongSeconds = 41.6 | DirectionE = E |MapCode = JP |Map= Japan Nara |LabelPosition= left |deity= {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=}} |ShrineRank= Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}} |consecration_year= 946 CE (Tenkei 9) |MainHall= |Alternatename= |AnnualFestival= |Rituals= }} '''Yoki Tenman Shrine''' (與喜天満神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Hase, Sakurai|ja|初瀬|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hase, Sakurai|qq=}}, {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|ja|桜井市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai, Nara|qq=}}, [[Nara Prefecture|奈良県]]. Its former Rank was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}}. == Deities == ; Main deity * [[Sugawara no Michizane]] (Sugawara no Michizane) ; Additional Deities * [[Amaterasu]] (天照大神) * [[Ōkurahime no Kami]] (大倉姫神) == History == The shrine is located in {{ill|Hase, Sakurai|ja|初瀬|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hase, Sakurai|qq=}}, believed to be the birthplace of {{ill|Nomino Sukune|ja|野見宿禰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nomino Sukune|qq=}}, the ancestor of the {{ill|Haji clan|ja|土師氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haji clan|qq=}}, later known as the Sugawara Clan. This historical connection made the area significant for the Sugawara family.{{Cite web|url=https://sakurai-kankou.jimdo.com/%E5%90%8D%E6%89%80%E6%97%A7%E8%B7%A1/%E5%88%9D%E7%80%AC%E9%81%93%E5%91%A8%E8%BE%BA/%E8%88%87%E5%96%9C%E5%A4%A9%E6%BA%80%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/|title=Yoki Tenman Shrine (Sakurai, Hase)|publisher=Sakurai City Tourism Association|access-date=April 23, 2019}} According to the ''{{ill|Hasedera Genki|ja|長谷寺験記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasedera Genki|qq=}}'', the shrine was founded in 946 CE (Tenkei 9) and later became venerated as the guardian shrine of Hase. == Sub-Shrines == * Taki-no-Kura Gongen Shrine (deity: [[Kumano Gongen|Hayatama-no-Mikoto]], [[Izanagi]], [[Izanami]]) * Shiradaijin Shrine (deity: {{ill|Watarai Haruhiko|ja|度会春彦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai Haruhiko|qq=}}) * Sakuraha Shrine (deity: {{ill|Iyo Shinnō|ja|伊予親王|1=Iyo Shinnō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Shinnō|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Shinnō|qq=}}) * Hachioji Shrine (deity: [[Amaterasu]], [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]) == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Wooden Seated Statue of Tenjin : Known as "Wrathful Tenjin," this seated statue in court attire is made of {{ill|yosegi-zukuri|ja|寄木造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yosegi-zukuri|qq=}} and features an intense expression. It bears an inscription from Shogen 1 (1259), making it the oldest surviving sculpture of Tenjin in Japan.{{Cite web|url=https://www.nara-np.co.jp/news/20120421085721.html|title=Important Cultural Properties Newly Designated: Tenjin Seated Statue (Yoki Tenman Shrine)|publisher=Nara Newspaper|access-date=April 23, 2019}} Within the statue's head is a mirror engraved with an image of {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|ja|十一面観音|Ekādaśamukha=Ekādaśamukha|lt=Jūichimen Kannon|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ekādaśamukha|qq=}}, symbolizing Tenjin's connection to the deity.{{Cite journal|title=New Cultural Property Designations|journal=Monthly Cultural Properties|issue=585|page=17|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|year=2012}} Previously enshrined in the main hall, it is now housed in the {{ill|Nara National Museum|ja|奈良国立博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara National Museum|qq=}}. === Additional Cultural Assets === [[File:Samurai O-Yoroi 2.jpg|thumb|200px|Reference: White-Threaded Yoroi (owned by {{ill|Hasedera|ja|長谷寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasedera|qq=}}, displayed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo National Museum|qq=}})]] Five sets of important cultural property armor (White-Threaded Yoroi, Red-Threaded Yoroi, Hawk Feather Yoroi, Three-Eyed Plate Yoroi, Blue-Laced Armor) were once associated with Yoki Tenman Shrine, which served as {{ill|Hasedera|ja|長谷寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasedera|qq=}}'s {{ill|Chinjugami|ja|鎮守神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinjugami|qq=}}. These were gifted by {{ill|Kitabatake Norikata|ja|北畠教謙|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitabatake Norikata|qq=}} in 1560 (Eiroku 3) for services rendered in battles against {{ill|Matsunaga Hisahide|ja|松永久秀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsunaga Hisahide|qq=}}.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/7051|title=White-Threaded Yoroi (Detailed Listing)|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|access-date=December 1, 2019}}{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/7047|title=Red-Threaded Yoroi (Detailed Listing)|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|access-date=December 1, 2019}} == Access == * Nearest station: 25 minutes on foot from {{ill|Hasedera Station|ja|長谷寺駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasedera Station|qq=}}, {{ill|Kintetsu Osaka Line|ja|近鉄大阪線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Osaka Line|qq=}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yoki-tenman-jinja}} * [https://www.yokitenmanjinja.jp/ Official Website of Yoki Tenman Shrine] {{translated page|ja|與喜天満神社|version=78784964|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Sakurai, Nara]] [[Category:Tenman-gū]] }} [[sn:Yoki Tenman Shrine]] [[en:Yoki Tenman Shrine]] [[sn:oki Tenman Shrine]] [[ja:與喜天満神社]]

It bears an inscription from Shogen 1 (1259), making it the oldest surviving sculpture of Tenjin in Japan.{{Cite web|url=https://www.nara-np.co.jp/news/20120421085721.html|title=Important Cultural Properties Newly Designated: Tenjin Seated Statue (Yoki Tenman Shrine)|publisher=Nara Newspaper|access-date=April 23, 2019}} Within the statue's head is a mirror engraved with an image of [[:en:Ekādaśamukha|Jūichimen Kannon]], symbolizing Tenjin's connection to the deity.{{Cite journal|title=New Cultural Property Designations|journal=Monthly Cultural Properties|issue=585|page=17|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|year=2012}} Previously enshrined in the main hall, it is now housed in the [[:en:Nara National Museum|Nara National Museum]]. === Additional Cultural Assets === [[File:Samurai O-Yoroi 2.jpg|thumb|200px|Reference: White-Threaded Yoroi (owned by [[:en:Hase-dera|Hasedera]], displayed at the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]])]] Five sets of important cultural property armor (White-Threaded Yoroi, Red-Threaded Yoroi, Hawk Feather Yoroi, Three-Eyed Plate Yoroi, Blue-Laced Armor) were once associated with Yoki Tenman Shrine, which served as [[:en:Hase-dera|Hasedera]]'s [[:en:Chinjugami|Chinjugami]]. It bears an inscription from Shogen 1 (1259), making it the oldest surviving sculpture of Tenjin in Japan.{{Cite web|url=https://www.nara-np.co.jp/news/20120421085721.html|title=Important Cultural Properties Newly Designated: Tenjin Seated Statue (Yoki Tenman Shrine)|publisher=Nara Newspaper|access-date=April 23, 2019}} Within the statue's head is a mirror engraved with an image of [[:en:Ekādaśamukha|Jūichimen Kannon]], symbolizing Tenjin's connection to the deity.{{Cite journal|title=New Cultural Property Designations|journal=Monthly Cultural Properties|issue=585|page=17|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|year=2012}} Previously enshrined in the main hall, it is now housed in the [[:en:Nara National Museum|Nara National Museum]]. === Additional Cultural Assets === [[File:Samurai O-Yoroi 2.jpg|thumb|200px|Reference: White-Threaded Yoroi (owned by [[:en:Hase-dera|Hasedera]], displayed at the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]])]] Five sets of important cultural property armor (White-Threaded Yoroi, Red-Threaded Yoroi, Hawk Feather Yoroi, Three-Eyed Plate Yoroi, Blue-Laced Armor) were once associated with Yoki Tenman Shrine, which served as [[:en:Hase-dera|Hasedera]]'s [[:en:Chinjugami|Chinjugami]]. These were gifted by {{ill|Kitabatake Norikata|ja|北畠教謙|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitabatake Norikata|qq=}} in 1560 (Eiroku 3) for services rendered in battles against [[:en:Matsunaga Hisahide|Matsunaga Hisahide]].{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/7051|title=White-Threaded Yoroi (Detailed Listing)|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|access-date=December 1, 2019}}{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/7047|title=Red-Threaded Yoroi (Detailed Listing)|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|access-date=December 1, 2019}} == Access == * Nearest station: 25 minutes on foot from [[:en:Hasedera Station|Hasedera Station]], [[:en:Osaka Line|Kintetsu Osaka Line]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yoki-tenman-jinja}} * [https://www.yokitenmanjinja.jp/ Official Website of Yoki Tenman Shrine] {{translated page|ja|與喜天満神社|version=78784964|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Sakurai, Nara]] [[Category:Tenman-gū]] }} [[sn:Yoki Tenman Shrine]] [[en:Yoki Tenman Shrine]] [[sn:oki Tenman Shrine]] [[ja:與喜天満神社]] [[Category:Tenjin (kami)]] [[Category:奈良県の旧郷社]]

These were gifted by {{ill|Kitabatake Norikata|ja|北畠教謙|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitabatake Norikata|qq=}} in 1560 (Eiroku 3) for services rendered in battles against [[:en:Matsunaga Hisahide|Matsunaga Hisahide]].{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/7051|title=White-Threaded Yoroi (Detailed Listing)|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|access-date=December 1, 2019}}{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/7047|title=Red-Threaded Yoroi (Detailed Listing)|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|access-date=December 1, 2019}} == Access == * Nearest station: 25 minutes on foot from [[:en:Hasedera Station|Hasedera Station]], [[:en:Osaka Line|Kintetsu Osaka Line]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yoki-tenman-jinja}} * [https://www.yokitenmanjinja.jp/ Official Website of Yoki Tenman Shrine] {{translated page|ja|與喜天満神社|version=78784964|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Sakurai, Nara]] [[Category:Tenman-gū]] }} [[sn:Yoki Tenman Shrine]] [[en:Yoki Tenman Shrine]] [[sn:oki Tenman Shrine]] [[ja:與喜天満神社]] [[Category:Tenjin (kami)]] [[Category:奈良県の旧郷社]] [[Category:奈良県の重要文化財]] [[Category:桜井市の建築物]] [[Category:桜井市の歴史]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Yoki Tenman Shrine (與喜天満神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Yoki-tenman-jinja honden.jpg|250px]]
Main Hall |location= [[Sakurai, Nara|桜井市]], [[Nara Prefecture|奈良県]], {{ill|Hase, Sakurai|ja|初瀬|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hase, Sakurai|qq=}} 14 |Latitude = 34 | LatMinutes = 32 | LatSeconds = 05.9 | DirectionN = N |Longitude = 135 | LongMinutes = 54 | LongSeconds = 41.6 | DirectionE = E |MapCode = JP |Map= Japan Nara |LabelPosition= left |deity= {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=}} |ShrineRank= Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}} |consecration_year= 946 CE (Tenkei 9) |MainHall= |Alternatename= |AnnualFestival= |Rituals= }} '''Yoki Tenman Shrine''' (與喜天満神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Hase, Sakurai|ja|初瀬|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hase, Sakurai|qq=}}, [[:en:Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai, Nara]], [[Nara Prefecture|奈良県]]. Its former Rank was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}}. == Deities == ; Main deity * [[Sugawara no Michizane]] (Sugawara no Michizane) ; Additional Deities * [[Amaterasu]] (天照大神) * [[Ōkurahime no Kami]] (大倉姫神) == History == The shrine is located in {{ill|Hase, Sakurai|ja|初瀬|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hase, Sakurai|qq=}}, believed to be the birthplace of [[:en:Nomi no Sukune|Nomino Sukune]], the ancestor of the [[:en:Haji clan|Haji clan]], later known as the Sugawara Clan. This historical connection made the area significant for the Sugawara family.{{Cite web|url=https://sakurai-kankou.jimdo.com/%E5%90%8D%E6%89%80%E6%97%A7%E8%B7%A1/%E5%88%9D%E7%80%AC%E9%81%93%E5%91%A8%E8%BE%BA/%E8%88%87%E5%96%9C%E5%A4%A9%E6%BA%80%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/|title=Yoki Tenman Shrine (Sakurai, Hase)|publisher=Sakurai City Tourism Association|access-date=April 23, 2019}} According to the ''{{ill|Hasedera Genki|ja|長谷寺験記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasedera Genki|qq=}}'', the shrine was founded in 946 CE (Tenkei 9) and later became venerated as the guardian shrine of Hase. == Sub-Shrines == * Taki-no-Kura Gongen Shrine (deity: [[Kumano Gongen|Hayatama-no-Mikoto]], [[Izanagi]], [[Izanami]]) * Shiradaijin Shrine (deity: {{ill|Watarai Haruhiko|ja|度会春彦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai Haruhiko|qq=}}) * Sakuraha Shrine (deity: {{ill|Iyo Shinnō|ja|伊予親王|1=Iyo Shinnō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Shinnō|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Shinnō|qq=}}) * Hachioji Shrine (deity: [[Amaterasu]], [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]) == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Wooden Seated Statue of Tenjin : Known as "Wrathful Tenjin," this seated statue in court attire is made of {{ill|yosegi-zukuri|ja|寄木造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yosegi-zukuri|qq=}} and features an intense expression. It bears an inscription from Shogen 1 (1259), making it the oldest surviving sculpture of Tenjin in Japan.{{Cite web|url=https://www.nara-np.co.jp/news/20120421085721.html|title=Important Cultural Properties Newly Designated: Tenjin Seated Statue (Yoki Tenman Shrine)|publisher=Nara Newspaper|access-date=April 23, 2019}} Within the statue's head is a mirror engraved with an image of [[:en:Ekādaśamukha|Jūichimen Kannon]], symbolizing Tenjin's connection to the deity.{{Cite journal|title=New Cultural Property Designations|journal=Monthly Cultural Properties|issue=585|page=17|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|year=2012}} Previously enshrined in the main hall, it is now housed in the [[:en:Nara National Museum|Nara National Museum]]. === Additional Cultural Assets === [[File:Samurai O-Yoroi 2.jpg|thumb|200px|Reference: White-Threaded Yoroi (owned by [[:en:Hase-dera|Hasedera]], displayed at the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]])]] Five sets of important cultural property armor (White-Threaded Yoroi, Red-Threaded Yoroi, Hawk Feather Yoroi, Three-Eyed Plate Yoroi, Blue-Laced Armor) were once associated with Yoki Tenman Shrine, which served as [[:en:Hase-dera|Hasedera]]'s [[:en:Chinjugami|Chinjugami]]. These were gifted by {{ill|Kitabatake Norikata|ja|北畠教謙|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitabatake Norikata|qq=}} in 1560 (Eiroku 3) for services rendered in battles against [[:en:Matsunaga Hisahide|Matsunaga Hisahide]].{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/7051|title=White-Threaded Yoroi (Detailed Listing)|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|access-date=December 1, 2019}}{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/7047|title=Red-Threaded Yoroi (Detailed Listing)|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|access-date=December 1, 2019}} == Access == * Nearest station: 25 minutes on foot from [[:en:Hasedera Station|Hasedera Station]], [[:en:Osaka Line|Kintetsu Osaka Line]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yoki-tenman-jinja}} * [https://www.yokitenmanjinja.jp/ Official Website of Yoki Tenman Shrine] {{translated page|ja|與喜天満神社|version=78784964|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Sakurai, Nara]] [[Category:Tenman-gū]] }} [[sn:Yoki Tenman Shrine]] [[sn:oki Tenman Shrine]] [[ja:與喜天満神社]] [[Category:Tenjin (kami)]] [[Category:奈良県の旧郷社]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Nara prefecture]] [[Category:桜井市の建築物]] [[Category:History of Sakurai, Nara�]] {{Commons category|Yoki-tenman-jinja}}

It bears an inscription from Shogen 1 (1259), making it the oldest surviving sculpture of Tenjin in Japan.{{Cite web|url=https://www.nara-np.co.jp/news/20120421085721.html|title=Important Cultural Properties Newly Designated: Tenjin Seated Statue (Yoki Tenman Shrine)|publisher=Nara Newspaper|access-date=April 23, 2019}} Within the statue's head is a mirror engraved with an image of [[:en:Ekādaśamukha|Jūichimen Kannon]], symbolizing Tenjin's connection to the deity.{{Cite journal|title=New Cultural Property Designations|journal=Monthly Cultural Properties|issue=585|page=17|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|year=2012}} Previously enshrined in the main hall, it is now housed in the [[:en:Nara National Museum|Nara National Museum]]. === Additional Cultural Assets === [[File:Samurai O-Yoroi 2.jpg|thumb|200px|Reference: White-Threaded Yoroi (owned by [[:en:Hase-dera|Hasedera]], displayed at the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]])]] Five sets of important cultural property armor (White-Threaded Yoroi, Red-Threaded Yoroi, Hawk Feather Yoroi, Three-Eyed Plate Yoroi, Blue-Laced Armor) were once associated with Yoki Tenman Shrine, which served as [[:en:Hase-dera|Hasedera]]'s [[:en:Chinjugami|Chinjugami]]. These were gifted by {{ill|Kitabatake Norikata|ja|北畠教謙|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitabatake Norikata|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in 1560 (Eiroku 3) for services rendered in battles against [[:en:Matsunaga Hisahide|Matsunaga Hisahide]].{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/7051|title=White-Threaded Yoroi (Detailed Listing)|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|access-date=December 1, 2019}}{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/7047|title=Red-Threaded Yoroi (Detailed Listing)|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|access-date=December 1, 2019}} == Access == * Nearest station: 25 minutes on foot from [[:en:Hasedera Station|Hasedera Station]], [[:en:Osaka Line|Kintetsu Osaka Line]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yoki-tenman-jinja}} * [https://www.yokitenmanjinja.jp/ Official Website of Yoki Tenman Shrine] {{translated page|ja|與喜天満神社|version=78784964|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Sakurai, Nara]] [[Category:Tenman-gū]] }} [[sn:Yoki Tenman Shrine]] [[sn:oki Tenman Shrine]] [[ja:與喜天満神社]] [[Category:Tenjin (kami)]] [[Category:奈良県の旧郷社]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Sakurai]] [[Category:History of Sakurai, Nara]] {{Commons category|Yoki-tenman-jinja}}

These were gifted by {{ill|Kitabatake Norikata|ja|北畠教謙|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitabatake Norikata|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in 1560 (Eiroku 3) for services rendered in battles against [[:en:Matsunaga Hisahide|Matsunaga Hisahide]].{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/7051|title=White-Threaded Yoroi (Detailed Listing)|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|access-date=December 1, 2019}}{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/7047|title=Red-Threaded Yoroi (Detailed Listing)|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|access-date=December 1, 2019}} == Access == * Nearest station: 25 minutes on foot from [[:en:Hasedera Station|Hasedera Station]], [[:en:Osaka Line|Kintetsu Osaka Line]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yoki-tenman-jinja}} * [https://www.yokitenmanjinja.jp/ Official Website of Yoki Tenman Shrine] {{translated page|ja|與喜天満神社|version=78784964|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Sakurai, Nara]] }} [[sn:Yoki Tenman Shrine]] [[sn:oki Tenman Shrine]] [[ja:與喜天満神社]] [[Category:Tenjin (kami)]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Sakurai]] [[Category:History of Sakurai, Nara]] {{Commons category|Yoki-tenman-jinja}}

Its former Rank was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}. == Deities == ; Main deity * [[Sugawara no Michizane]] (Sugawara no Michizane) ; Additional Deities * [[Amaterasu]] (天照大神) * [[Ōkurahime no Kami]] (大倉姫神) == History == The shrine is located in {{ill|Hase, Sakurai|ja|初瀬|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hase, Sakurai|qq=}}, believed to be the birthplace of [[:en:Nomi no Sukune|Nomino Sukune]], the ancestor of the [[:en:Haji clan|Haji clan]], later known as the Sugawara Clan. It bears an inscription from Shogen 1 (1259), making it the oldest surviving sculpture of Tenjin in Japan.{{Cite web|url=https://www.nara-np.co.jp/news/20120421085721.html|title=Important Cultural Properties Newly Designated: Tenjin Seated Statue (Yoki Tenman Shrine)|publisher=Nara Newspaper|access-date=April 23, 2019}} Within the statue's head is a mirror engraved with an image of [[:en:Ekādaśamukha|Jūichimen Kannon]], symbolizing Tenjin's connection to the deity.{{Cite journal|title=New Cultural Property Designations|journal=Monthly Cultural Properties|issue=585|page=17|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|year=2012}} Previously enshrined in the main hall, it is now housed in the [[:en:Nara National Museum|Nara National Museum]]. === Additional Cultural Assets === [[File:Samurai O-Yoroi 2.jpg|thumb|200px|Reference: White-Threaded Yoroi (owned by [[:en:Hase-dera|Hasedera]], displayed at the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]])]] Five sets of important cultural property armor (White-Threaded Yoroi, Red-Threaded Yoroi, Hawk Feather Yoroi, Three-Eyed Plate Yoroi, Blue-Laced Armor) were once associated with Yoki Tenman Shrine, which served as [[:en:Hase-dera|Hasedera]]'s [[:en:Chinjugami|Chinjugami]]. These were gifted by {{ill|Kitabatake Norikata|ja|北畠教謙|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitabatake Norikata|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in 1560 (Eiroku 3) for services rendered in battles against [[:en:Matsunaga Hisahide|Matsunaga Hisahide]].{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/7051|title=White-Threaded Yoroi (Detailed Listing)|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|access-date=December 1, 2019}}{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/7047|title=Red-Threaded Yoroi (Detailed Listing)|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|access-date=December 1, 2019}} == Access == * Nearest station: 25 minutes on foot from [[:en:Hasedera Station|Hasedera Station]], [[:en:Osaka Line|Kintetsu Osaka Line]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yoki-tenman-jinja}} * [https://www.yokitenmanjinja.jp/ Official Website of Yoki Tenman Shrine] {{translated page|ja|與喜天満神社|version=78784964|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Sakurai, Nara]] }} [[sn:Yoki Tenman Shrine]] [[sn:oki Tenman Shrine]] [[ja:與喜天満神社]] [[Category:Tenjin (kami)]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Sakurai]] [[Category:History of Sakurai, Nara]] {{Commons category|Yoki-tenman-jinja}}

Its former Rank was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}. == Deities == ; Main deity * [[Sugawara no Michizane]] (Sugawara no Michizane) ; Additional Deities * [[Amaterasu]] (天照大神) * [[Ōkurahime no Kami]] (大倉姫神) == History == The shrine is located in {{ill|Hase, Sakurai|ja|初瀬|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hase, Sakurai|qq=}}, believed to be the birthplace of [[:en:Nomi no Sukune|Nomino Sukune]], the ancestor of the [[:en:Haji clan|Haji clan]], later known as the Sugawara Clan. This historical connection made the area significant for the Sugawara family.{{Cite web|url=https://sakurai-kankou.jimdo.com/%E5%90%8D%E6%89%80%E6%97%A7%E8%B7%A1/%E5%88%9D%E7%80%AC%E9%81%93%E5%91%A8%E8%BE%BA/%E8%88%87%E5%96%9C%E5%A4%A9%E6%BA%80%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/|title=Yoki Tenman Shrine (Sakurai, Hase)|publisher=Sakurai City Tourism Association|access-date=April 23, 2019}} According to the ''{{ill|Hasedera Genki|ja|長谷寺験記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasedera Genki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'', the shrine was founded in 946 CE (Tenkei 9) and later became venerated as the guardian shrine of Hase. == Sub-Shrines == * Taki-no-Kura Gongen Shrine (deity: [[Kumano Gongen|Hayatama-no-Mikoto]], [[Izanagi]], [[:en:Izanami|Izanami]]) * Shiradaijin Shrine (deity: {{ill|Watarai Haruhiko|ja|度会春彦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai Haruhiko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) * Sakuraha Shrine (deity: {{ill|Iyo Shinnō|ja|伊予親王|1=Iyo Shinnō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Shinnō|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Shinnō|qq=}}) * Hachioji Shrine (deity: [[Amaterasu]], [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]) == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Wooden Seated Statue of Tenjin : Known as "Wrathful Tenjin," this seated statue in court attire is made of {{ill|yosegi-zukuri|ja|寄木造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yosegi-zukuri|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 仏像}} and features an intense expression. It bears an inscription from Shogen 1 (1259), making it the oldest surviving sculpture of Tenjin in Japan.{{Cite web|url=https://www.nara-np.co.jp/news/20120421085721.html|title=Important Cultural Properties Newly Designated: Tenjin Seated Statue (Yoki Tenman Shrine)|publisher=Nara Newspaper|access-date=April 23, 2019}} Within the statue's head is a mirror engraved with an image of [[:en:Ekādaśamukha|Jūichimen Kannon]], symbolizing Tenjin's connection to the deity.{{Cite journal|title=New Cultural Property Designations|journal=Monthly Cultural Properties|issue=585|page=17|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|year=2012}} Previously enshrined in the main hall, it is now housed in the [[:en:Nara National Museum|Nara National Museum]]. === Additional Cultural Assets === [[File:Samurai O-Yoroi 2.jpg|thumb|200px|Reference: White-Threaded Yoroi (owned by [[:en:Hase-dera|Hasedera]], displayed at the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]])]] Five sets of important cultural property armor (White-Threaded Yoroi, Red-Threaded Yoroi, Hawk Feather Yoroi, Three-Eyed Plate Yoroi, Blue-Laced Armor) were once associated with Yoki Tenman Shrine, which served as [[:en:Hase-dera|Hasedera]]'s [[:en:Chinjugami|Chinjugami]]. These were gifted by {{ill|Kitabatake Norikata|ja|北畠教謙|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitabatake Norikata|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in 1560 (Eiroku 3) for services rendered in battles against [[:en:Matsunaga Hisahide|Matsunaga Hisahide]].{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/7051|title=White-Threaded Yoroi (Detailed Listing)|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|access-date=December 1, 2019}}{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/7047|title=Red-Threaded Yoroi (Detailed Listing)|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|access-date=December 1, 2019}} == Access == * Nearest station: 25 minutes on foot from [[:en:Hasedera Station|Hasedera Station]], [[:en:Osaka Line|Kintetsu Osaka Line]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yoki-tenman-jinja}} * [https://www.yokitenmanjinja.jp/ Official Website of Yoki Tenman Shrine] {{translated page|ja|與喜天満神社|version=78784964|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Sakurai, Nara]] }} [[sn:Yoki Tenman Shrine]] [[sn:oki Tenman Shrine]] [[ja:與喜天満神社]] [[Category:Tenjin (kami)]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Sakurai]] [[Category:History of Sakurai, Nara]] {{Commons category|Yoki-tenman-jinja}}

This historical connection made the area significant for the Sugawara family.{{Cite web|url=https://sakurai-kankou.jimdo.com/%E5%90%8D%E6%89%80%E6%97%A7%E8%B7%A1/%E5%88%9D%E7%80%AC%E9%81%93%E5%91%A8%E8%BE%BA/%E8%88%87%E5%96%9C%E5%A4%A9%E6%BA%80%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/|title=Yoki Tenman Shrine (Sakurai, Hase)|publisher=Sakurai City Tourism Association|access-date=April 23, 2019}} According to the ''{{ill|Hasedera Genki|ja|長谷寺験記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasedera Genki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'', the shrine was founded in 946 CE (Tenkei 9) and later became venerated as the guardian shrine of Hase. == Sub-Shrines == * Taki-no-Kura Gongen Shrine (deity: [[Kumano Gongen|Hayatama-no-Mikoto]], [[Izanagi]], [[:en:Izanami|Izanami]]) * Shiradaijin Shrine (deity: {{ill|Watarai Haruhiko|ja|度会春彦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai Haruhiko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) * Sakuraha Shrine (deity: {{ill|Iyo Shinnō|ja|伊予親王|1=Iyo Shinnō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Shinnō|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Shinnō|qq=}}) * Hachioji Shrine (deity: [[Amaterasu]], [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]) == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Wooden Seated Statue of Tenjin : Known as "Wrathful Tenjin," this seated statue in court attire is made of {{ill|yosegi-zukuri|ja|寄木造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yosegi-zukuri|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 仏像}} and features an intense expression. It bears an inscription from Shogen 1 (1259), making it the oldest surviving sculpture of Tenjin in Japan.{{Cite web|url=https://www.nara-np.co.jp/news/20120421085721.html|title=Important Cultural Properties Newly Designated: Tenjin Seated Statue (Yoki Tenman Shrine)|publisher=Nara Newspaper|access-date=April 23, 2019}} Within the statue's head is a mirror engraved with an image of [[:en:Ekādaśamukha|Jūichimen Kannon]], symbolizing Tenjin's connection to the deity.{{Cite journal|title=New Cultural Property Designations|journal=Monthly Cultural Properties|issue=585|page=17|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|year=2012}} Previously enshrined in the main hall, it is now housed in the [[:en:Nara National Museum|Nara National Museum]]. === Additional Cultural Assets === [[File:Samurai O-Yoroi 2.jpg|thumb|200px|Reference: White-Threaded Yoroi (owned by [[:en:Hase-dera|Hasedera]], displayed at the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]])]] Five sets of important cultural property armor (White-Threaded Yoroi, Red-Threaded Yoroi, Hawk Feather Yoroi, Three-Eyed Plate Yoroi, Blue-Laced Armor) were once associated with Yoki Tenman Shrine, which served as [[:en:Hase-dera|Hasedera]]'s [[:en:Chinjugami|Chinjugami]]. These were gifted by {{ill|Kitabatake Norikata|ja|北畠教謙|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitabatake Norikata|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in 1560 (Eiroku 3) for services rendered in battles against [[:en:Matsunaga Hisahide|Matsunaga Hisahide]].{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/7051|title=White-Threaded Yoroi (Detailed Listing)|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|access-date=December 1, 2019}}{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/7047|title=Red-Threaded Yoroi (Detailed Listing)|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|access-date=December 1, 2019}} == Access == * Nearest station: 25 minutes on foot from [[:en:Hasedera Station|Hasedera Station]], [[:en:Osaka Line|Kintetsu Osaka Line]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yoki-tenman-jinja}} * [https://www.yokitenmanjinja.jp/ Official Website of Yoki Tenman Shrine] {{translated page|ja|與喜天満神社|version=78784964|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Sakurai, Nara]] }} [[sn:Yoki Tenman Shrine]] [[sn:oki Tenman Shrine]] [[ja:與喜天満神社]] [[Category:Tenjin (kami)]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Sakurai]] [[Category:History of Sakurai, Nara]] {{Commons category|Yoki-tenman-jinja}}

Main Hall |location= [[:en:Sakurai, Nara|桜井市]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|奈良県]], {{ill|Hase, Sakurai|ja|初瀬|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hase, Sakurai|qq=|1=Hase|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11396531}} 14 |Latitude = 34 | LatMinutes = 32 | LatSeconds = 05.9 | DirectionN = N |Longitude = 135 | LongMinutes = 54 | LongSeconds = 41.6 | DirectionE = E |MapCode = JP |Map= Japan Nara |LabelPosition= left |deity= {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} |ShrineRank= Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |consecration_year= 946 CE (Tenkei 9) |MainHall= |Alternatename= |AnnualFestival= |Rituals= }} '''Yoki Tenman Shrine''' (與喜天満神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Hase, Sakurai|ja|初瀬|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hase, Sakurai|qq=|1=Hase|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11396531}}, [[:en:Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai, Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|奈良県]]. Its former Rank was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == Deities == ; Main deity * [[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]] (Sugawara no Michizane) ; Additional Deities * [[Amaterasu]] (天照大神) * [[Ōkurahime no Kami]] (大倉姫神) == History == The shrine is located in {{ill|Hase, Sakurai|ja|初瀬|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hase, Sakurai|qq=|1=Hase|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11396531}}, believed to be the birthplace of [[:en:Nomi no Sukune|Nomino Sukune]], the ancestor of the [[:en:Haji clan|Haji clan]], later known as the Sugawara Clan. This historical connection made the area significant for the Sugawara family.{{Cite web|url=https://sakurai-kankou.jimdo.com/%E5%90%8D%E6%89%80%E6%97%A7%E8%B7%A1/%E5%88%9D%E7%80%AC%E9%81%93%E5%91%A8%E8%BE%BA/%E8%88%87%E5%96%9C%E5%A4%A9%E6%BA%80%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/|title=Yoki Tenman Shrine (Sakurai, Hase)|publisher=Sakurai City Tourism Association|access-date=April 23, 2019}} According to the ''{{ill|Hasedera Genki|ja|長谷寺験記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasedera Genki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'', the shrine was founded in 946 CE (Tenkei 9) and later became venerated as the guardian shrine of Hase. == Sub-Shrines == * Taki-no-Kura Gongen Shrine (deity: [[Kumano Gongen|Hayatama-no-Mikoto]], [[Izanagi]], [[:en:Izanami|Izanami]]) * Shiradaijin Shrine (deity: {{ill|Watarai Haruhiko|ja|度会春彦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai Haruhiko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) * Sakuraha Shrine (deity: {{ill|Iyo Shinnō|ja|伊予親王|1=Iyo Shinnō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Shinnō|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Shinnō|qq=}}) * Hachioji Shrine (deity: [[Amaterasu]], [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]) == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Wooden Seated Statue of Tenjin : Known as "Wrathful Tenjin," this seated statue in court attire is made of {{ill|yosegi-zukuri|ja|寄木造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yosegi-zukuri|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 仏像|1=Buddharupa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1000809}} and features an intense expression. It bears an inscription from Shogen 1 (1259), making it the oldest surviving sculpture of Tenjin in Japan.{{Cite web|url=https://www.nara-np.co.jp/news/20120421085721.html|title=Important Cultural Properties Newly Designated: Tenjin Seated Statue (Yoki Tenman Shrine)|publisher=Nara Newspaper|access-date=April 23, 2019}} Within the statue's head is a mirror engraved with an image of [[:en:Ekādaśamukha|Jūichimen Kannon]], symbolizing Tenjin's connection to the deity.{{Cite journal|title=New Cultural Property Designations|journal=Monthly Cultural Properties|issue=585|page=17|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|year=2012}} Previously enshrined in the main hall, it is now housed in the [[:en:Nara National Museum|Nara National Museum]]. === Additional Cultural Assets === [[File:Samurai O-Yoroi 2.jpg|thumb|200px|Reference: White-Threaded Yoroi (owned by [[:en:Hase-dera|Hasedera]], displayed at the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]])]] Five sets of important cultural property armor (White-Threaded Yoroi, Red-Threaded Yoroi, Hawk Feather Yoroi, Three-Eyed Plate Yoroi, Blue-Laced Armor) were once associated with Yoki Tenman Shrine, which served as [[:en:Hase-dera|Hasedera]]'s [[:en:Chinjugami|Chinjugami]]. These were gifted by {{ill|Kitabatake Norikata|ja|北畠教謙|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitabatake Norikata|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in 1560 (Eiroku 3) for services rendered in battles against [[:en:Matsunaga Hisahide|Matsunaga Hisahide]].{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/7051|title=White-Threaded Yoroi (Detailed Listing)|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|access-date=December 1, 2019}}{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/7047|title=Red-Threaded Yoroi (Detailed Listing)|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|access-date=December 1, 2019}} == Access == * Nearest station: 25 minutes on foot from [[:en:Hasedera Station|Hasedera Station]], [[:en:Osaka Line|Kintetsu Osaka Line]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yoki-tenman-jinja}} * [https://www.yokitenmanjinja.jp/ Official Website of Yoki Tenman Shrine] {{translated page|ja|與喜天満神社|version=78784964|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Sakurai, Nara]] }} [[sn:Yoki Tenman Shrine]] [[sn:oki Tenman Shrine]] [[ja:與喜天満神社]] [[Category:Tenjin (kami)]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Sakurai]] [[Category:History of Sakurai, Nara]] {{Commons category|Yoki-tenman-jinja}}

These were gifted by {{ill|Kitabatake Norikata|ja|北畠教謙|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitabatake Norikata|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in 1560 (Eiroku 3) for services rendered in battles against [[:en:Matsunaga Hisahide|Matsunaga Hisahide]].{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/7051|title=White-Threaded Yoroi (Detailed Listing)|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|access-date=December 1, 2019}}{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/7047|title=Red-Threaded Yoroi (Detailed Listing)|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|access-date=December 1, 2019}} == Access == * Nearest station: 25 minutes on foot from [[:en:Hasedera Station|Hasedera Station]], [[:en:Osaka Line|Kintetsu Osaka Line]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yoki-tenman-jinja}} * [https://www.yokitenmanjinja.jp/ Official Website of Yoki Tenman Shrine] {{translated page|ja|與喜天満神社|version=78784964|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Sakurai, Nara]] }} [[sn:Yoki Tenman Shrine]] [[sn:oki Tenman Shrine]] [[ja:與喜天満神社]] [[Category:Tenjin (kami)]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Sakurai]] [[Category:History of Sakurai, Nara]] {{Commons category|Yoki-tenman-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q65279652}}

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q65279652]] Wikidata name: Yoki Tenman Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:32, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Yoki Tenman Shrine (與喜天満神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Yoki-tenman-jinja honden.jpg|250px]]
Main Hall |location= [[:en:Sakurai, Nara|桜井市]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|奈良県]], {{ill|Hase, Sakurai|ja|初瀬|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hase, Sakurai|qq=|1=Hase|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11396531}} 14 |Latitude = 34 | LatMinutes = 32 | LatSeconds = 05.9 | DirectionN = N |Longitude = 135 | LongMinutes = 54 | LongSeconds = 41.6 | DirectionE = E |MapCode = JP |Map= Japan Nara |LabelPosition= left |deity= {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} |ShrineRank= Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |consecration_year= 946 CE (Tenkei 9) |MainHall= |Alternatename= |AnnualFestival= |Rituals= }} '''Yoki Tenman Shrine''' (與喜天満神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Hase, Sakurai|ja|初瀬|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hase, Sakurai|qq=|1=Hase|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11396531}}, [[:en:Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai, Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|奈良県]]. Its former Rank was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == Deities == ; Main deity * [[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]] (Sugawara no Michizane) ; Additional Deities * [[Amaterasu]] (天照大神) * [[Ōkurahime no Kami]] (大倉姫神) == History == The shrine is located in {{ill|Hase, Sakurai|ja|初瀬|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hase, Sakurai|qq=|1=Hase|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11396531}}, believed to be the birthplace of [[:en:Nomi no Sukune|Nomino Sukune]], the ancestor of the [[:en:Haji clan|Haji clan]], later known as the Sugawara Clan. This historical connection made the area significant for the Sugawara family.{{Cite web|url=https://sakurai-kankou.jimdo.com/%E5%90%8D%E6%89%80%E6%97%A7%E8%B7%A1/%E5%88%9D%E7%80%AC%E9%81%93%E5%91%A8%E8%BE%BA/%E8%88%87%E5%96%9C%E5%A4%A9%E6%BA%80%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/|title=Yoki Tenman Shrine (Sakurai, Hase)|publisher=Sakurai City Tourism Association|access-date=April 23, 2019}} According to the ''{{ill|Hasedera Genki|ja|長谷寺験記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasedera Genki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'', the shrine was founded in 946 CE (Tenkei 9) and later became venerated as the guardian shrine of Hase. == Sub-Shrines == * Taki-no-Kura Gongen Shrine (deity: [[Kumano Gongen|Hayatama-no-Mikoto]], [[Izanagi]], [[:en:Izanami|Izanami]]) * Shiradaijin Shrine (deity: {{ill|Watarai Haruhiko|ja|度会春彦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai Haruhiko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) * Sakuraha Shrine (deity: {{ill|Iyo Shinnō|ja|伊予親王|1=Iyo Shinnō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Shinnō|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Shinnō|qq=}}) * Hachioji Shrine (deity: [[Amaterasu]], [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]) == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Wooden Seated Statue of Tenjin : Known as "Wrathful Tenjin," this seated statue in court attire is made of {{ill|yosegi-zukuri|ja|寄木造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yosegi-zukuri|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 仏像|1=Buddharupa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1000809}} and features an intense expression. It bears an inscription from Shogen 1 (1259), making it the oldest surviving sculpture of Tenjin in Japan.{{Cite web|url=https://www.nara-np.co.jp/news/20120421085721.html|title=Important Cultural Properties Newly Designated: Tenjin Seated Statue (Yoki Tenman Shrine)|publisher=Nara Newspaper|access-date=April 23, 2019}} Within the statue's head is a mirror engraved with an image of [[:en:Ekādaśamukha|Jūichimen Kannon]], symbolizing Tenjin's connection to the deity.{{Cite journal|title=New Cultural Property Designations|journal=Monthly Cultural Properties|issue=585|page=17|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|year=2012}} Previously enshrined in the main hall, it is now housed in the [[:en:Nara National Museum|Nara National Museum]]. === Additional Cultural Assets === [[File:Samurai O-Yoroi 2.jpg|thumb|200px|Reference: White-Threaded Yoroi (owned by [[:en:Hase-dera|Hasedera]], displayed at the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]])]] Five sets of important cultural property armor (White-Threaded Yoroi, Red-Threaded Yoroi, Hawk Feather Yoroi, Three-Eyed Plate Yoroi, Blue-Laced Armor) were once associated with Yoki Tenman Shrine, which served as [[:en:Hase-dera|Hasedera]]'s [[:en:Chinjugami|Chinjugami]]. These were gifted by {{ill|Kitabatake Norikata|ja|北畠教謙|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitabatake Norikata|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in 1560 (Eiroku 3) for services rendered in battles against [[:en:Matsunaga Hisahide|Matsunaga Hisahide]].{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/7051|title=White-Threaded Yoroi (Detailed Listing)|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|access-date=December 1, 2019}}{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/7047|title=Red-Threaded Yoroi (Detailed Listing)|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|access-date=December 1, 2019}} == Access == * Nearest station: 25 minutes on foot from [[:en:Hasedera Station|Hasedera Station]], [[:en:Osaka Line|Kintetsu Osaka Line]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yoki-tenman-jinja}} * [https://www.yokitenmanjinja.jp/ Official Website of Yoki Tenman Shrine] {{translated page|ja|與喜天満神社|version=78784964|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Sakurai, Nara]] }} [[sn:Yoki Tenman Shrine]] [[sn:oki Tenman Shrine]] [[ja:與喜天満神社]] [[Category:Tenjin (kami)]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Sakurai]] [[Category:History of Sakurai, Nara]] [[Category:Qq 4]] {{Commons category|Yoki-tenman-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q65279652}}

This historical connection made the area significant for the Sugawara family.{{Cite web|url=https://sakurai-kankou.jimdo.com/%E5%90%8D%E6%89%80%E6%97%A7%E8%B7%A1/%E5%88%9D%E7%80%AC%E9%81%93%E5%91%A8%E8%BE%BA/%E8%88%87%E5%96%9C%E5%A4%A9%E6%BA%80%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/|title=Yoki Tenman Shrine (Sakurai, Hase)|publisher=Sakurai City Tourism Association|access-date=April 23, 2019}} According to the ''{{ill|Hasedera Genki|ja|長谷寺験記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasedera Genki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'', the shrine was founded in 946 CE (Tenkei 9) and later became venerated as the guardian shrine of Hase. == Sub-Shrines == * Taki-no-Kura Gongen Shrine (deity: [[Kumano Gongen|Hayatama-no-Mikoto]], [[Izanagi]], [[:en:Izanami|Izanami]]) * Shiradaijin Shrine (deity: {{ill|Watarai Haruhiko|ja|度会春彦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai Haruhiko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) * Sakuraha Shrine (deity: {{ill|Iyo Shinnō|ja|伊予親王|1=Iyo Shinnō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Shinnō|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Shinnō|qq=}}) * Hachioji Shrine (deity: [[Amaterasu]], [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]) == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Wooden Seated Statue of Tenjin : Known as "Wrathful Tenjin," this seated statue in court attire is made of {{ill|yosegi-zukuri|ja|寄木造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yosegi-zukuri|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 仏像|1=Buddharupa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1000809}} and features an intense expression.
