This historical connection made the area significant for the Sugawara family.{{Cite web|url=https://sakurai-kankou.jimdo.com/%E5%90%8D%E6%89%80%E6%97%A7%E8%B7%A1/%E5%88%9D%E7%80%AC%E9%81%93%E5%91%A8%E8%BE%BA/%E8%88%87%E5%96%9C%E5%A4%A9%E6%BA%80%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/|title=Yoki Tenman Shrine (Sakurai, Hase)|publisher=Sakurai City Tourism Association|access-date=April 23, 2019}} According to the ''{{ill|Hasedera Genki|ja|長谷寺験記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasedera Genki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'', the shrine was founded in 946 CE (Tenkei 9) and later became venerated as the guardian shrine of Hase. == Sub-Shrines == * Taki-no-Kura Gongen Shrine (deity: [[Kumano Gongen|Hayatama-no-Mikoto]], [[Izanagi]], [[:en:Izanami|Izanami]]) * Shiradaijin Shrine (deity: {{ill|Watarai Haruhiko|ja|度会春彦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai Haruhiko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) * Sakuraha Shrine (deity: {{ill|Iyo Shinnō|ja|伊予親王|1=Iyo Shinnō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Shinnō|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Shinnō|qq=}}) * Hachioji Shrine (deity: [[Amaterasu]], [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]) == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Wooden Seated Statue of Tenjin : Known as "Wrathful Tenjin," this seated statue in court attire is made of {{ill|yosegi-zukuri|ja|寄木造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yosegi-zukuri|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 仏像|1=Buddharupa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1000809}} and features an intense expression. It bears an inscription from Shogen 1 (1259), making it the oldest surviving sculpture of Tenjin in Japan.{{Cite web|url=https://www.nara-np.co.jp/news/20120421085721.html|title=Important Cultural Properties Newly Designated: Tenjin Seated Statue (Yoki Tenman Shrine)|publisher=Nara Newspaper|access-date=April 23, 2019}} Within the statue's head is a mirror engraved with an image of [[:en:Ekādaśamukha|Jūichimen Kannon]], symbolizing Tenjin's connection to the deity.{{Cite journal|title=New Cultural Property Designations|journal=Monthly Cultural Properties|issue=585|page=17|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|year=2012}} Previously enshrined in the main hall, it is now housed in the [[:en:Nara National Museum|Nara National Museum]]. === Additional Cultural Assets === [[File:Samurai O-Yoroi 2.jpg|thumb|200px|Reference: White-Threaded Yoroi (owned by [[:en:Hase-dera|Hasedera]], displayed at the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]])]] Five sets of important cultural property armor (White-Threaded Yoroi, Red-Threaded Yoroi, Hawk Feather Yoroi, Three-Eyed Plate Yoroi, Blue-Laced Armor) were once associated with Yoki Tenman Shrine, which served as [[:en:Hase-dera|Hasedera]]'s [[:en:Chinjugami|Chinjugami]]. These were gifted by {{ill|Kitabatake Norikata|ja|北畠教謙|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitabatake Norikata|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in 1560 (Eiroku 3) for services rendered in battles against [[:en:Matsunaga Hisahide|Matsunaga Hisahide]].{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/7051|title=White-Threaded Yoroi (Detailed Listing)|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|access-date=December 1, 2019}}{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/7047|title=Red-Threaded Yoroi (Detailed Listing)|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|access-date=December 1, 2019}} == Access == * Nearest station: 25 minutes on foot from [[:en:Hasedera Station|Hasedera Station]], [[:en:Osaka Line|Kintetsu Osaka Line]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yoki-tenman-jinja}} * [https://www.yokitenmanjinja.jp/ Official Website of Yoki Tenman Shrine] {{translated page|ja|與喜天満神社|version=78784964|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Sakurai, Nara]] }} [[ja:與喜天満神社]] [[Category:Tenjin (kami)]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Sakurai]] [[Category:History of Sakurai, Nara]] {{Commons category|Yoki-tenman-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q65279652}}

This historical connection made the area significant for the Sugawara family.{{Cite web|url=https://sakurai-kankou.jimdo.com/%E5%90%8D%E6%89%80%E6%97%A7%E8%B7%A1/%E5%88%9D%E7%80%AC%E9%81%93%E5%91%A8%E8%BE%BA/%E8%88%87%E5%96%9C%E5%A4%A9%E6%BA%80%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/|title=Yoki Tenman Shrine (Sakurai, Hase)|publisher=Sakurai City Tourism Association|access-date=April 23, 2019}} According to the ''{{ill|Hasedera Genki|ja|長谷寺験記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasedera Genki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'', the shrine was founded in 946 CE (Tenkei 9) and later became venerated as the guardian shrine of Hase. == Sub-Shrines == * Taki-no-Kura Gongen Shrine (deity: [[Kumano Gongen|Hayatama-no-Mikoto]], [[Izanagi]], [[:en:Izanami|Izanami]]) * Shiradaijin Shrine (deity: {{ill|Watarai Haruhiko|ja|度会春彦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai Haruhiko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) * Sakuraha Shrine (deity: {{ill|Iyo Shinnō|ja|伊予親王|1=Iyo Shinnō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Shinnō|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Shinnō|qq=}}) * Hachioji Shrine (deity: [[Amaterasu]], [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]) == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Wooden Seated Statue of Tenjin : Known as "Wrathful Tenjin," this seated statue in court attire is made of {{ill|yosegi-zukuri|ja|寄木造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yosegi-zukuri|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 仏像|1=Buddharupa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1000809}} and features an intense expression. It bears an inscription from Shogen 1 (1259), making it the oldest surviving sculpture of Tenjin in Japan.{{Cite web|url=https://www.nara-np.co.jp/news/20120421085721.html|title=Important Cultural Properties Newly Designated: Tenjin Seated Statue (Yoki Tenman Shrine)|publisher=Nara Newspaper|access-date=April 23, 2019}} Within the statue's head is a mirror engraved with an image of [[:en:Ekādaśamukha|Jūichimen Kannon]], symbolizing Tenjin's connection to the deity.{{Cite journal|title=New Cultural Property Designations|journal=Monthly Cultural Properties|issue=585|page=17|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|year=2012}} Previously enshrined in the main hall, it is now housed in the [[:en:Nara National Museum|Nara National Museum]]. === Additional Cultural Assets === [[File:Samurai O-Yoroi 2.jpg|thumb|200px|Reference: White-Threaded Yoroi (owned by [[:en:Hase-dera|Hasedera]], displayed at the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]])]] Five sets of important cultural property armor (White-Threaded Yoroi, Red-Threaded Yoroi, Hawk Feather Yoroi, Three-Eyed Plate Yoroi, Blue-Laced Armor) were once associated with Yoki Tenman Shrine, which served as [[:en:Hase-dera|Hasedera]]'s [[:en:Chinjugami|Chinjugami]]. These were gifted by {{ill|Kitabatake Norikata|ja|北畠教謙|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitabatake Norikata|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in 1560 (Eiroku 3) for services rendered in battles against [[:en:Matsunaga Hisahide|Matsunaga Hisahide]].{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/7051|title=White-Threaded Yoroi (Detailed Listing)|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|access-date=December 1, 2019}}{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/7047|title=Red-Threaded Yoroi (Detailed Listing)|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|access-date=December 1, 2019}} == Access == * Nearest station: 25 minutes on foot from [[:en:Hasedera Station|Hasedera Station]], [[:en:Osaka Line|Kintetsu Osaka Line]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yoki-tenman-jinja}} * [https://www.yokitenmanjinja.jp/ Official Website of Yoki Tenman Shrine] {{translated page|ja|與喜天満神社|version=78784964|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Sakurai, Nara]] }} [[ja:與喜天満神社]] [[Category:Tenjin (kami)]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Sakurai]] [[Category:History of Sakurai, Nara]] {{Commons category|Yoki-tenman-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q65279652}}

Main Hall |location= [[:en:Sakurai, Nara|桜井市]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|奈良県]], {{ill|Hase, Sakurai|ja|初瀬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hase, Sakurai|1=Hase|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11396531}} 14 |Latitude = 34 | LatMinutes = 32 | LatSeconds = 05.9 | DirectionN = N |Longitude = 135 | LongMinutes = 54 | LongSeconds = 41.6 | DirectionE = E |MapCode = JP |Map= Japan Nara |LabelPosition= left |deity= {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} |ShrineRank= Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |consecration_year= 946 CE (Tenkei 9) |MainHall= |Alternatename= |AnnualFestival= |Rituals= }} '''Yoki Tenman Shrine''' (與喜天満神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Hase, Sakurai|ja|初瀬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hase, Sakurai|1=Hase|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11396531}}, [[:en:Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai, Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|奈良県]]. Its former Rank was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == Deities == ; Main deity * [[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]] (Sugawara no Michizane) ; Additional Deities * [[Amaterasu]] (天照大神) * [[Ōkurahime no Kami]] (大倉姫神) == History == The shrine is located in {{ill|Hase, Sakurai|ja|初瀬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hase, Sakurai|1=Hase|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11396531}}, believed to be the birthplace of [[:en:Nomi no Sukune|Nomino Sukune]], the ancestor of the [[:en:Haji clan|Haji clan]], later known as the Sugawara Clan. This historical connection made the area significant for the Sugawara family.{{Cite web|url=https://sakurai-kankou.jimdo.com/%E5%90%8D%E6%89%80%E6%97%A7%E8%B7%A1/%E5%88%9D%E7%80%AC%E9%81%93%E5%91%A8%E8%BE%BA/%E8%88%87%E5%96%9C%E5%A4%A9%E6%BA%80%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/|title=Yoki Tenman Shrine (Sakurai, Hase)|publisher=Sakurai City Tourism Association|access-date=April 23, 2019}} According to the ''{{ill|Hasedera Genki|ja|長谷寺験記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasedera Genki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'', the shrine was founded in 946 CE (Tenkei 9) and later became venerated as the guardian shrine of Hase. == Sub-Shrines == * Taki-no-Kura Gongen Shrine (deity: [[Kumano Gongen|Hayatama-no-Mikoto]], [[Izanagi]], [[:en:Izanami|Izanami]]) * Shiradaijin Shrine (deity: {{ill|Watarai Haruhiko|ja|度会春彦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai Haruhiko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) * Sakuraha Shrine (deity: {{ill|Iyo Shinnō|ja|伊予親王|1=Iyo Shinnō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Shinnō|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Shinnō|qq=}}) * Hachioji Shrine (deity: [[Amaterasu]], [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]) == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Wooden Seated Statue of Tenjin : Known as "Wrathful Tenjin," this seated statue in court attire is made of {{ill|yosegi-zukuri|ja|寄木造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yosegi-zukuri|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 仏像|1=Buddharupa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1000809}} and features an intense expression. It bears an inscription from Shogen 1 (1259), making it the oldest surviving sculpture of Tenjin in Japan.{{Cite web|url=https://www.nara-np.co.jp/news/20120421085721.html|title=Important Cultural Properties Newly Designated: Tenjin Seated Statue (Yoki Tenman Shrine)|publisher=Nara Newspaper|access-date=April 23, 2019}} Within the statue's head is a mirror engraved with an image of [[:en:Ekādaśamukha|Jūichimen Kannon]], symbolizing Tenjin's connection to the deity.{{Cite journal|title=New Cultural Property Designations|journal=Monthly Cultural Properties|issue=585|page=17|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|year=2012}} Previously enshrined in the main hall, it is now housed in the [[:en:Nara National Museum|Nara National Museum]]. === Additional Cultural Assets === [[File:Samurai O-Yoroi 2.jpg|thumb|200px|Reference: White-Threaded Yoroi (owned by [[:en:Hase-dera|Hasedera]], displayed at the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]])]] Five sets of important cultural property armor (White-Threaded Yoroi, Red-Threaded Yoroi, Hawk Feather Yoroi, Three-Eyed Plate Yoroi, Blue-Laced Armor) were once associated with Yoki Tenman Shrine, which served as [[:en:Hase-dera|Hasedera]]'s [[:en:Chinjugami|Chinjugami]]. These were gifted by {{ill|Kitabatake Norikata|ja|北畠教謙|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitabatake Norikata|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in 1560 (Eiroku 3) for services rendered in battles against [[:en:Matsunaga Hisahide|Matsunaga Hisahide]].{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/7051|title=White-Threaded Yoroi (Detailed Listing)|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|access-date=December 1, 2019}}{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/7047|title=Red-Threaded Yoroi (Detailed Listing)|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|access-date=December 1, 2019}} == Access == * Nearest station: 25 minutes on foot from [[:en:Hasedera Station|Hasedera Station]], [[:en:Osaka Line|Kintetsu Osaka Line]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yoki-tenman-jinja}} * [https://www.yokitenmanjinja.jp/ Official Website of Yoki Tenman Shrine] {{translated page|ja|與喜天満神社|version=78784964|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Sakurai, Nara]] }} [[ja:與喜天満神社]] [[Category:Tenjin (kami)]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Sakurai]] [[Category:History of Sakurai, Nara]] {{Commons category|Yoki-tenman-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q65279652}}

[[sn:commons:Category:Yoki-tenman-jinja]][[ja:與喜天満神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q65279652]] Wikidata name: Yoki Tenman Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:32, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Yoki Tenman Shrine (與喜天満神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Yoki-tenman-jinja honden.jpg|250px]]
Main Hall |location= [[:en:Sakurai, Nara|桜井市]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|奈良県]], {{ill|Hase, Sakurai|ja|初瀬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hase, Sakurai|1=Hase|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11396531}} 14 |Latitude = 34 | LatMinutes = 32 | LatSeconds = 05.9 | DirectionN = N |Longitude = 135 | LongMinutes = 54 | LongSeconds = 41.6 | DirectionE = E |MapCode = JP |Map= Japan Nara |LabelPosition= left |deity= {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} |ShrineRank= Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |consecration_year= 946 CE (Tenkei 9) |MainHall= |Alternatename= |AnnualFestival= |Rituals= }} '''Yoki Tenman Shrine''' (與喜天満神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Hase, Sakurai|ja|初瀬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hase, Sakurai|1=Hase|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11396531}}, [[:en:Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai, Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|奈良県]]. Its former Rank was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == Deities == ; Main deity * [[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]] (Sugawara no Michizane) ; Additional Deities * [[Amaterasu]] (天照大神) * [[Ōkurahime no Kami]] (大倉姫神) == History == The shrine is located in {{ill|Hase, Sakurai|ja|初瀬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hase, Sakurai|1=Hase|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11396531}}, believed to be the birthplace of [[:en:Nomi no Sukune|Nomino Sukune]], the ancestor of the [[:en:Haji clan|Haji clan]], later known as the Sugawara Clan. This historical connection made the area significant for the Sugawara family.{{Cite web|url=https://sakurai-kankou.jimdo.com/%E5%90%8D%E6%89%80%E6%97%A7%E8%B7%A1/%E5%88%9D%E7%80%AC%E9%81%93%E5%91%A8%E8%BE%BA/%E8%88%87%E5%96%9C%E5%A4%A9%E6%BA%80%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/|title=Yoki Tenman Shrine (Sakurai, Hase)|publisher=Sakurai City Tourism Association|access-date=April 23, 2019}} According to the ''{{ill|Hasedera Genki|ja|長谷寺験記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasedera Genki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'', the shrine was founded in 946 CE (Tenkei 9) and later became venerated as the guardian shrine of Hase. == Sub-Shrines == * Taki-no-Kura Gongen Shrine (deity: [[Kumano Gongen|Hayatama-no-Mikoto]], [[Izanagi]], [[:en:Izanami|Izanami]]) * Shiradaijin Shrine (deity: {{ill|Watarai Haruhiko|ja|度会春彦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai Haruhiko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) * Sakuraha Shrine (deity: {{ill|Iyo Shinnō|ja|伊予親王|1=Iyo Shinnō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Shinnō|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Shinnō|qq=}}) * Hachioji Shrine (deity: [[Amaterasu]], [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]) == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Wooden Seated Statue of Tenjin : Known as "Wrathful Tenjin," this seated statue in court attire is made of {{ill|yosegi-zukuri|ja|寄木造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yosegi-zukuri|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 仏像|1=Buddharupa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1000809}} and features an intense expression. It bears an inscription from Shogen 1 (1259), making it the oldest surviving sculpture of Tenjin in Japan.{{Cite web|url=https://www.nara-np.co.jp/news/20120421085721.html|title=Important Cultural Properties Newly Designated: Tenjin Seated Statue (Yoki Tenman Shrine)|publisher=Nara Newspaper|access-date=April 23, 2019}} Within the statue's head is a mirror engraved with an image of [[:en:Ekādaśamukha|Jūichimen Kannon]], symbolizing Tenjin's connection to the deity.{{Cite journal|title=New Cultural Property Designations|journal=Monthly Cultural Properties|issue=585|page=17|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|year=2012}} Previously enshrined in the main hall, it is now housed in the [[:en:Nara National Museum|Nara National Museum]]. === Additional Cultural Assets === [[File:Samurai O-Yoroi 2.jpg|thumb|200px|Reference: White-Threaded Yoroi (owned by [[:en:Hase-dera|Hasedera]], displayed at the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]])]] Five sets of important cultural property armor (White-Threaded Yoroi, Red-Threaded Yoroi, Hawk Feather Yoroi, Three-Eyed Plate Yoroi, Blue-Laced Armor) were once associated with Yoki Tenman Shrine, which served as [[:en:Hase-dera|Hasedera]]'s [[:en:Chinjugami|Chinjugami]]. These were gifted by {{ill|Kitabatake Norikata|ja|北畠教謙|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitabatake Norikata|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in 1560 (Eiroku 3) for services rendered in battles against [[:en:Matsunaga Hisahide|Matsunaga Hisahide]].{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/7051|title=White-Threaded Yoroi (Detailed Listing)|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|access-date=December 1, 2019}}{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/7047|title=Red-Threaded Yoroi (Detailed Listing)|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|access-date=December 1, 2019}} == Access == * Nearest station: 25 minutes on foot from [[:en:Hasedera Station|Hasedera Station]], [[:en:Osaka Line|Kintetsu Osaka Line]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yoki-tenman-jinja}} * [https://www.yokitenmanjinja.jp/ Official Website of Yoki Tenman Shrine] {{translated page|ja|與喜天満神社|version=78784964|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Sakurai, Nara]] }} [[ja:與喜天満神社]] [[Category:Tenjin (kami)]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Sakurai]] [[Category:History of Sakurai, Nara]] {{Commons category|Yoki-tenman-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q65279652}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

These were gifted by {{ill|Kitabatake Norikata|ja|北畠教謙|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitabatake Norikata|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in 1560 (Eiroku 3) for services rendered in battles against [[:en:Matsunaga Hisahide|Matsunaga Hisahide]].{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/7051|title=White-Threaded Yoroi (Detailed Listing)|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|access-date=December 1, 2019}}{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/7047|title=Red-Threaded Yoroi (Detailed Listing)|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|access-date=December 1, 2019}} == Access == * Nearest station: 25 minutes on foot from [[:en:Hasedera Station|Hasedera Station]], [[:en:Osaka Line|Kintetsu Osaka Line]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yoki-tenman-jinja}} * [https://www.yokitenmanjinja.jp/ Official Website of Yoki Tenman Shrine] {{translated page|ja|與喜天満神社|version=78784964|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Sakurai, Nara]] }} [[ja:與喜天満神社]] [[Category:Tenjin (kami)]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Sakurai]] [[Category:History of Sakurai, Nara]] {{Commons category|Yoki-tenman-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q65279652}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

This historical connection made the area significant for the Sugawara family.{{Cite web|url=https://sakurai-kankou.jimdo.com/%E5%90%8D%E6%89%80%E6%97%A7%E8%B7%A1/%E5%88%9D%E7%80%AC%E9%81%93%E5%91%A8%E8%BE%BA/%E8%88%87%E5%96%9C%E5%A4%A9%E6%BA%80%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/|title=Yoki Tenman Shrine (Sakurai, Hase)|publisher=Sakurai City Tourism Association|access-date=April 23, 2019}} According to the ''{{ill|Hasedera Genki|ja|長谷寺験記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasedera Genki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'', the shrine was founded in 946 CE (Tenkei 9) and later became venerated as the guardian shrine of Hase. == Sub-Shrines == * Taki-no-Kura Gongen Shrine (deity: [[Kumano Gongen|Hayatama-no-Mikoto]], [[Izanagi]], [[:en:Izanami|Izanami]]) * Shiradaijin Shrine (deity: {{ill|Watarai Haruhiko|ja|度会春彦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai Haruhiko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) * Sakuraha Shrine (deity: {{ill|Iyo Shinnō|ja|伊予親王|1=Iyo Shinnō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Shinnō|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Shinnō|qq=}}) * Hachioji Shrine (deity: [[Amaterasu]], [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]) == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Wooden Seated Statue of Tenjin : Known as "Wrathful Tenjin," this seated statue in court attire is made of {{ill|yosegi-zukuri|ja|寄木造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yosegi-zukuri|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 仏像|1=Buddharupa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1000809}} and features an intense expression. It bears an inscription from Shogen 1 (1259), making it the oldest surviving sculpture of Tenjin in Japan.{{Cite web|url=https://www.nara-np.co.jp/news/20120421085721.html|title=Important Cultural Properties Newly Designated: Tenjin Seated Statue (Yoki Tenman Shrine)|publisher=Nara Newspaper|access-date=April 23, 2019}} Within the statue's head is a mirror engraved with an image of [[:en:Ekādaśamukha|Jūichimen Kannon]], symbolizing Tenjin's connection to the deity.{{Cite journal|title=New Cultural Property Designations|journal=Monthly Cultural Properties|issue=585|page=17|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|year=2012}} Previously enshrined in the main hall, it is now housed in the [[:en:Nara National Museum|Nara National Museum]]. === Additional Cultural Assets === [[File:Samurai O-Yoroi 2.jpg|thumb|200px|Reference: White-Threaded Yoroi (owned by [[:en:Hase-dera|Hasedera]], displayed at the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]])]] Five sets of important cultural property armor (White-Threaded Yoroi, Red-Threaded Yoroi, Hawk Feather Yoroi, Three-Eyed Plate Yoroi, Blue-Laced Armor) were once associated with Yoki Tenman Shrine, which served as [[:en:Hase-dera|Hasedera]]'s [[:en:Chinjugami|Chinjugami]]. These were gifted by {{ill|Kitabatake Norikata|ja|北畠教謙|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitabatake Norikata|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in 1560 (Eiroku 3) for services rendered in battles against [[:en:Matsunaga Hisahide|Matsunaga Hisahide]].{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/7051|title=White-Threaded Yoroi (Detailed Listing)|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|access-date=December 1, 2019}}{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/7047|title=Red-Threaded Yoroi (Detailed Listing)|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|access-date=December 1, 2019}} == Access == * Nearest station: 25 minutes on foot from [[:en:Hasedera Station|Hasedera Station]], [[:en:Osaka Line|Kintetsu Osaka Line]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yoki-tenman-jinja}} * [https://www.yokitenmanjinja.jp/ Official Website of Yoki Tenman Shrine] {{translated page|ja|與喜天満神社|version=78784964|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Sakurai, Nara]] }} [[ja:與喜天満神社]] [[Category:Tenjin (kami)]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Sakurai]] [[Category:History of Sakurai, Nara]] {{Commons category|Yoki-tenman-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q65279652}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] {{wikidata link|Q65279652}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

[[sn:commons:Category:Yoki-tenman-jinja]][[ja:與喜天満神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q65279652]] Wikidata name: Yoki Tenman Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:32, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Yoki Tenman Shrine (與喜天満神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Yoki-tenman-jinja honden.jpg|250px]]
Main Hall |location= [[:en:Sakurai, Nara|桜井市]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|奈良県]], {{ill|Hase, Sakurai|ja|初瀬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hase, Sakurai|1=Hase|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11396531}} 14 |Latitude = 34 | LatMinutes = 32 | LatSeconds = 05.9 | DirectionN = N |Longitude = 135 | LongMinutes = 54 | LongSeconds = 41.6 | DirectionE = E |MapCode = JP |Map= Japan Nara |LabelPosition= left |deity= {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} |ShrineRank= Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |consecration_year= 946 CE (Tenkei 9) |MainHall= |Alternatename= |AnnualFestival= |Rituals= }} '''Yoki Tenman Shrine''' (與喜天満神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Hase, Sakurai|ja|初瀬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hase, Sakurai|1=Hase|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11396531}}, [[:en:Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara]]. Its former Rank was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == Deities == ; Main deity * [[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]] (Sugawara no Michizane) ; Additional Deities * [[Amaterasu]] (天照大神) * [[Ōkurahime no Kami]] (大倉姫神) == History == The shrine is located in {{ill|Hase, Sakurai|ja|初瀬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hase, Sakurai|1=Hase|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11396531}}, believed to be the birthplace of [[:en:Nomi no Sukune|Nomino Sukune]], the ancestor of the [[:en:Haji clan|Haji clan]], later known as the Sugawara Clan. This historical connection made the area significant for the Sugawara family.{{Cite web|url=https://sakurai-kankou.jimdo.com/%E5%90%8D%E6%89%80%E6%97%A7%E8%B7%A1/%E5%88%9D%E7%80%AC%E9%81%93%E5%91%A8%E8%BE%BA/%E8%88%87%E5%96%9C%E5%A4%A9%E6%BA%80%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/|title=Yoki Tenman Shrine (Sakurai, Hase)|publisher=Sakurai City Tourism Association|access-date=April 23, 2019}} According to the ''{{ill|Hasedera Genki|ja|長谷寺験記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasedera Genki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'', the shrine was founded in 946 CE (Tenkei 9) and later became venerated as the guardian shrine of Hase. == Sub-Shrines == * Taki-no-Kura Gongen Shrine (deity: [[Kumano Gongen|Hayatama-no-Mikoto]], [[Izanagi]], [[:en:Izanami|Izanami]]) * Shiradaijin Shrine (deity: {{ill|Watarai Haruhiko|ja|度会春彦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai Haruhiko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) * Sakuraha Shrine (deity: {{ill|Iyo Shinnō|ja|伊予親王|1=Iyo Shinnō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Shinnō|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Shinnō|qq=}}) * Hachioji Shrine (deity: [[Amaterasu]], [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]) == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Wooden Seated Statue of Tenjin : Known as "Wrathful Tenjin," this seated statue in court attire is made of {{ill|yosegi-zukuri|ja|寄木造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yosegi-zukuri|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 仏像|1=Buddharupa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1000809}} and features an intense expression. It bears an inscription from Shogen 1 (1259), making it the oldest surviving sculpture of Tenjin in Japan.{{Cite web|url=https://www.nara-np.co.jp/news/20120421085721.html|title=Important Cultural Properties Newly Designated: Tenjin Seated Statue (Yoki Tenman Shrine)|publisher=Nara Newspaper|access-date=April 23, 2019}} Within the statue's head is a mirror engraved with an image of [[:en:Ekādaśamukha|Jūichimen Kannon]], symbolizing Tenjin's connection to the deity.{{Cite journal|title=New Cultural Property Designations|journal=Monthly Cultural Properties|issue=585|page=17|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|year=2012}} Previously enshrined in the main hall, it is now housed in the [[:en:Nara National Museum|Nara National Museum]]. === Additional Cultural Assets === [[File:Samurai O-Yoroi 2.jpg|thumb|200px|Reference: White-Threaded Yoroi (owned by [[:en:Hase-dera|Hasedera]], displayed at the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]])]] Five sets of important cultural property armor (White-Threaded Yoroi, Red-Threaded Yoroi, Hawk Feather Yoroi, Three-Eyed Plate Yoroi, Blue-Laced Armor) were once associated with Yoki Tenman Shrine, which served as [[:en:Hase-dera|Hasedera]]'s [[:en:Chinjugami|Chinjugami]]. These were gifted by {{ill|Kitabatake Norikata|ja|北畠教謙|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitabatake Norikata|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in 1560 (Eiroku 3) for services rendered in battles against [[:en:Matsunaga Hisahide|Matsunaga Hisahide]].{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/7051|title=White-Threaded Yoroi (Detailed Listing)|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|access-date=December 1, 2019}}{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/7047|title=Red-Threaded Yoroi (Detailed Listing)|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|access-date=December 1, 2019}} == Access == * Nearest station: 25 minutes on foot from [[:en:Hasedera Station|Hasedera Station]], [[:en:Osaka Line|Kintetsu Osaka Line]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yoki-tenman-jinja}} * [https://www.yokitenmanjinja.jp/ Official Website of Yoki Tenman Shrine] {{translated page|ja|與喜天満神社|version=78784964|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Sakurai, Nara]] }} [[ja:與喜天満神社]] [[Category:Tenjin (kami)]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Sakurai]] [[Category:History of Sakurai, Nara]] {{Commons category|Yoki-tenman-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q65279652}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q65279652}} [[Category:Accidentally overwritten pages]][[sn:3]]

These were gifted by {{ill|Kitabatake Norikata|ja|北畠教謙|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitabatake Norikata|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in 1560 (Eiroku 3) for services rendered in battles against [[:en:Matsunaga Hisahide|Matsunaga Hisahide]].{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/7051|title=White-Threaded Yoroi (Detailed Listing)|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|access-date=December 1, 2019}}{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/7047|title=Red-Threaded Yoroi (Detailed Listing)|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|access-date=December 1, 2019}} == Access == * Nearest station: 25 minutes on foot from [[:en:Hasedera Station|Hasedera Station]], [[:en:Osaka Line|Kintetsu Osaka Line]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yoki-tenman-jinja}} * [https://www.yokitenmanjinja.jp/ Official Website of Yoki Tenman Shrine] {{translated page|ja|與喜天満神社|version=78784964|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Sakurai, Nara]] }} [[ja:與喜天満神社]] [[Category:Tenjin (kami)]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Sakurai]] [[Category:History of Sakurai, Nara]] {{Commons category|Yoki-tenman-jinja}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Accidentally overwritten pages]] [[Category:Pages likely having an accidental overwite in their history that shrank them]] [[sn:3]] {{wikidata link|Q65279652}}

[[sn:commons:Category:Yoki-tenman-jinja]][[ja:與喜天満神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q65279652]] Wikidata name: Yoki Tenman Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:32, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Yoki Tenman Shrine (與喜天満神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Yoki-tenman-jinja honden.jpg|250px]]
Main Hall |location= [[:en:Sakurai, Nara|桜井市]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|奈良県]], {{ill|Hase, Sakurai|ja|初瀬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hase, Sakurai|1=Hase|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11396531}} 14 |Latitude = 34 | LatMinutes = 32 | LatSeconds = 05.9 | DirectionN = N |Longitude = 135 | LongMinutes = 54 | LongSeconds = 41.6 | DirectionE = E |MapCode = JP |Map= Japan Nara |LabelPosition= left |deity= {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} |ShrineRank= Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |consecration_year= 946 CE (Tenkei 9) |MainHall= |Alternatename= |AnnualFestival= |Rituals= }} '''Yoki Tenman Shrine''' (與喜天満神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Hase, Sakurai|ja|初瀬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hase, Sakurai|1=Hase|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11396531}}, [[:en:Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara]]. Its former Rank was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == Deities == ; Main deity * [[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]] (Sugawara no Michizane) ; Additional Deities * [[Amaterasu]] (天照大神) * [[Ōkurahime no Kami]] (大倉姫神) == History == The shrine is located in {{ill|Hase, Sakurai|ja|初瀬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hase, Sakurai|1=Hase|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11396531}}, believed to be the birthplace of [[:en:Nomi no Sukune|Nomino Sukune]], the ancestor of the [[:en:Haji clan|Haji clan]], later known as the Sugawara Clan. This historical connection made the area significant for the Sugawara family.{{Cite web|url=https://sakurai-kankou.jimdo.com/%E5%90%8D%E6%89%80%E6%97%A7%E8%B7%A1/%E5%88%9D%E7%80%AC%E9%81%93%E5%91%A8%E8%BE%BA/%E8%88%87%E5%96%9C%E5%A4%A9%E6%BA%80%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/|title=Yoki Tenman Shrine (Sakurai, Hase)|publisher=Sakurai City Tourism Association|access-date=April 23, 2019}} According to the ''{{ill|Hasedera Genki|ja|長谷寺験記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasedera Genki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'', the shrine was founded in 946 CE (Tenkei 9) and later became venerated as the guardian shrine of Hase. == Sub-Shrines == * Taki-no-Kura Gongen Shrine (deity: [[Kumano Gongen|Hayatama-no-Mikoto]], [[Izanagi]], [[:en:Izanami|Izanami]]) * Shiradaijin Shrine (deity: {{ill|Watarai Haruhiko|ja|度会春彦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai Haruhiko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) * Sakuraha Shrine (deity: {{ill|Iyo Shinnō|ja|伊予親王|1=Iyo Shinnō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Shinnō|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Shinnō|qq=}}) * Hachioji Shrine (deity: [[Amaterasu]], [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]) == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Wooden Seated Statue of Tenjin : Known as "Wrathful Tenjin," this seated statue in court attire is made of {{ill|yosegi-zukuri|ja|寄木造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yosegi-zukuri|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 仏像|1=Buddharupa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1000809}} and features an intense expression. It bears an inscription from Shogen 1 (1259), making it the oldest surviving sculpture of Tenjin in Japan.{{Cite web|url=https://www.nara-np.co.jp/news/20120421085721.html|title=Important Cultural Properties Newly Designated: Tenjin Seated Statue (Yoki Tenman Shrine)|publisher=Nara Newspaper|access-date=April 23, 2019}} Within the statue's head is a mirror engraved with an image of [[:en:Ekādaśamukha|Jūichimen Kannon]], symbolizing Tenjin's connection to the deity.{{Cite journal|title=New Cultural Property Designations|journal=Monthly Cultural Properties|issue=585|page=17|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|year=2012}} Previously enshrined in the main hall, it is now housed in the [[:en:Nara National Museum|Nara National Museum]]. === Additional Cultural Assets === [[File:Samurai O-Yoroi 2.jpg|thumb|200px|Reference: White-Threaded Yoroi (owned by [[:en:Hase-dera|Hasedera]], displayed at the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]])]] Five sets of important cultural property armor (White-Threaded Yoroi, Red-Threaded Yoroi, Hawk Feather Yoroi, Three-Eyed Plate Yoroi, Blue-Laced Armor) were once associated with Yoki Tenman Shrine, which served as [[:en:Hase-dera|Hasedera]]'s [[:en:Chinjugami|Chinjugami]]. These were gifted by {{ill|Kitabatake Norikata|ja|北畠教謙|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitabatake Norikata|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in 1560 (Eiroku 3) for services rendered in battles against [[:en:Matsunaga Hisahide|Matsunaga Hisahide]].{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/7051|title=White-Threaded Yoroi (Detailed Listing)|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|access-date=December 1, 2019}}{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/7047|title=Red-Threaded Yoroi (Detailed Listing)|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|access-date=December 1, 2019}} == Access == * Nearest station: 25 minutes on foot from [[:en:Hasedera Station|Hasedera Station]], [[:en:Osaka Line|Kintetsu Osaka Line]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yoki-tenman-jinja}} * [https://www.yokitenmanjinja.jp/ Official Website of Yoki Tenman Shrine] {{translated page|ja|與喜天満神社|version=78784964|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Sakurai, Nara]] }} [[ja:與喜天満神社]] [[Category:Tenjin (kami)]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Sakurai]] [[Category:History of Sakurai, Nara]] {{Commons category|Yoki-tenman-jinja}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Accidentally overwritten pages]] [[Category:Pages likely having an accidental overwite in their history that shrank them]] [[sn:3]] {{wikidata link|Q65279652}}

This historical connection made the area significant for the Sugawara family.{{Cite web|url=https://sakurai-kankou.jimdo.com/%E5%90%8D%E6%89%80%E6%97%A7%E8%B7%A1/%E5%88%9D%E7%80%AC%E9%81%93%E5%91%A8%E8%BE%BA/%E8%88%87%E5%96%9C%E5%A4%A9%E6%BA%80%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/|title=Yoki Tenman Shrine (Sakurai, Hase)|publisher=Sakurai City Tourism Association|access-date=April 23, 2019}} According to the ''{{ill|Hasedera Genki|ja|長谷寺験記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasedera Genki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'', the shrine was founded in 946 CE (Tenkei 9) and later became venerated as the guardian shrine of Hase. == Sub-Shrines == * Taki-no-Kura Gongen Shrine (deity: [[Kumano Gongen|Hayatama-no-Mikoto]], [[Izanagi]], [[:en:Izanami|Izanami]]) * Shiradaijin Shrine (deity: {{ill|Watarai Haruhiko|ja|度会春彦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai Haruhiko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) * Sakuraha Shrine (deity: {{ill|Iyo Shinnō|ja|伊予親王|1=Iyo Shinnō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Shinnō|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Shinnō|qq=}}) * Hachioji Shrine (deity: [[Amaterasu]], [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]) == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Wooden Seated Statue of Tenjin : Known as "Wrathful Tenjin," this seated statue in court attire is made of {{ill|yosegi-zukuri|ja|寄木造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yosegi-zukuri|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 仏像|1=Buddharupa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1000809}} and features an intense expression. These were gifted by {{ill|Kitabatake Norikata|ja|北畠教謙|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitabatake Norikata|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in 1560 (Eiroku 3) for services rendered in battles against [[:en:Matsunaga Hisahide|Matsunaga Hisahide]].{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/7051|title=White-Threaded Yoroi (Detailed Listing)|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|access-date=December 1, 2019}}{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/7047|title=Red-Threaded Yoroi (Detailed Listing)|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|access-date=December 1, 2019}} == Access == * Nearest station: 25 minutes on foot from [[:en:Hasedera Station|Hasedera Station]], [[:en:Osaka Line|Kintetsu Osaka Line]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yoki-tenman-jinja}} * [https://www.yokitenmanjinja.jp/ Official Website of Yoki Tenman Shrine] {{translated page|ja|與喜天満神社|version=78784964|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Sakurai, Nara]] }} [[ja:與喜天満神社]] [[Category:Tenjin (kami)]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Sakurai]] [[Category:History of Sakurai, Nara]] {{Commons category|Yoki-tenman-jinja}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Accidentally overwritten pages]] [[Category:Pages likely having an accidental overwite in their history that shrank them]] [[sn:3]] {{wikidata link|Q65279652}}

It bears an inscription from Shogen 1 (1259), making it the oldest surviving sculpture of Tenjin in Japan.{{Cite web|url=https://www.nara-np.co.jp/news/20120421085721.html|title=Important Cultural Properties Newly Designated: Tenjin Seated Statue (Yoki Tenman Shrine)|publisher=Nara Newspaper|access-date=April 23, 2019}} Within the statue's head is a mirror engraved with an image of [[:en:Ekādaśamukha|Jūichimen Kannon]], symbolizing Tenjin's connection to the deity.{{Cite journal|title=New Cultural Property Designations|journal=Monthly Cultural Properties|issue=585|page=17|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|year=2012}} Previously enshrined in the main hall, it is now housed in the [[:en:Nara National Museum|Nara National Museum]]. === Additional Cultural Assets === [[File:Samurai O-Yoroi 2.jpg|thumb|200px|Reference: White-Threaded Yoroi (owned by [[:en:Hase-dera|Hasedera]], displayed at the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]])]] Five sets of important cultural property armor (White-Threaded Yoroi, Red-Threaded Yoroi, Hawk Feather Yoroi, Three-Eyed Plate Yoroi, Blue-Laced Armor) were once associated with Yoki Tenman Shrine, which served as [[:en:Hase-dera|Hasedera]]'s [[:en:Chinjugami|Chinjugami]]. Main Hall |location= [[:en:Sakurai, Nara|桜井市]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|奈良県]], {{ill|Hase, Sakurai|ja|初瀬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hase, Sakurai|1=Hase|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11396531}} 14 |Latitude = 34 | LatMinutes = 32 | LatSeconds = 05.9 | DirectionN = N |Longitude = 135 | LongMinutes = 54 | LongSeconds = 41.6 | DirectionE = E |MapCode = JP |Map= Japan Nara |LabelPosition= left |deity= {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} |ShrineRank= Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |consecration_year= 946 CE (Tenkei 9) |MainHall= |Alternatename= |AnnualFestival= |Rituals= }} '''Yoki Tenman Shrine''' (與喜天満神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Hase, Sakurai|ja|初瀬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hase, Sakurai|1=Hase|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11396531}}, [[:en:Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara]]. Its former Rank was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == Deities == ; Main deity * [[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]] (Sugawara no Michizane) ; Additional Deities * [[Amaterasu]] (天照大神) * [[Ōkurahime no Kami]] (大倉姫神) == History == The shrine is located in {{ill|Hase, Sakurai|ja|初瀬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hase, Sakurai|1=Hase|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11396531}}, believed to be the birthplace of [[:en:Nomi no Sukune|Nomino Sukune]], the ancestor of the [[:en:Haji clan|Haji clan]], later known as the Sugawara Clan. This historical connection made the area significant for the Sugawara family.{{Cite web|url=https://sakurai-kankou.jimdo.com/%E5%90%8D%E6%89%80%E6%97%A7%E8%B7%A1/%E5%88%9D%E7%80%AC%E9%81%93%E5%91%A8%E8%BE%BA/%E8%88%87%E5%96%9C%E5%A4%A9%E6%BA%80%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/|title=Yoki Tenman Shrine (Sakurai, Hase)|publisher=Sakurai City Tourism Association|access-date=April 23, 2019}} According to the ''{{ill|Hasedera Genki|ja|長谷寺験記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasedera Genki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'', the shrine was founded in 946 CE (Tenkei 9) and later became venerated as the guardian shrine of Hase. == Sub-Shrines == * Taki-no-Kura Gongen Shrine (deity: [[Kumano Gongen|Hayatama-no-Mikoto]], [[Izanagi]], [[:en:Izanami|Izanami]]) * Shiradaijin Shrine (deity: {{ill|Watarai Haruhiko|ja|度会春彦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai Haruhiko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) * Sakuraha Shrine (deity: {{ill|Iyo Shinnō|ja|伊予親王|1=Iyo Shinnō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Shinnō|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Shinnō|qq=}}) * Hachioji Shrine (deity: [[Amaterasu]], [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]) == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Wooden Seated Statue of Tenjin : Known as "Wrathful Tenjin," this seated statue in court attire is made of {{ill|yosegi-zukuri|ja|寄木造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yosegi-zukuri|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 仏像|1=Buddharupa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1000809}} and features an intense expression. It bears an inscription from Shogen 1 (1259), making it the oldest surviving sculpture of Tenjin in Japan.{{Cite web|url=https://www.nara-np.co.jp/news/20120421085721.html|title=Important Cultural Properties Newly Designated: Tenjin Seated Statue (Yoki Tenman Shrine)|publisher=Nara Newspaper|access-date=April 23, 2019}} Within the statue's head is a mirror engraved with an image of [[:en:Ekādaśamukha|Jūichimen Kannon]], symbolizing Tenjin's connection to the deity.{{Cite journal|title=New Cultural Property Designations|journal=Monthly Cultural Properties|issue=585|page=17|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|year=2012}} Previously enshrined in the main hall, it is now housed in the [[:en:Nara National Museum|Nara National Museum]]. === Additional Cultural Assets === [[File:Samurai O-Yoroi 2.jpg|thumb|200px|Reference: White-Threaded Yoroi (owned by [[:en:Hase-dera|Hasedera]], displayed at the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]])]] Five sets of important cultural property armor (White-Threaded Yoroi, Red-Threaded Yoroi, Hawk Feather Yoroi, Three-Eyed Plate Yoroi, Blue-Laced Armor) were once associated with Yoki Tenman Shrine, which served as [[:en:Hase-dera|Hasedera]]'s [[:en:Chinjugami|Chinjugami]]. These were gifted by {{ill|Kitabatake Norikata|ja|北畠教謙|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitabatake Norikata|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in 1560 (Eiroku 3) for services rendered in battles against [[:en:Matsunaga Hisahide|Matsunaga Hisahide]].{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/7051|title=White-Threaded Yoroi (Detailed Listing)|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|access-date=December 1, 2019}}{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/7047|title=Red-Threaded Yoroi (Detailed Listing)|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|access-date=December 1, 2019}} == Access == * Nearest station: 25 minutes on foot from [[:en:Hasedera Station|Hasedera Station]], [[:en:Osaka Line|Kintetsu Osaka Line]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yoki-tenman-jinja}} * [https://www.yokitenmanjinja.jp/ Official Website of Yoki Tenman Shrine] {{translated page|ja|與喜天満神社|version=78784964|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:與喜天満神社]] {{Commons category|Yoki-tenman-jinja}} [[sn:3]] {{wikidata link|Q65279652}} [[Category:Accidentally overwritten pages]] [[Category:Buildings in Sakurai]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:History of Sakurai, Nara]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Pages likely having an accidental overwite in their history that shrank them]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]] [[Category:Sakurai, Nara]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Tenjin (kami)]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

These were gifted by {{ill|Kitabatake Norikata|ja|北畠教謙|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitabatake Norikata|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in 1560 (Eiroku 3) for services rendered in battles against [[:en:Matsunaga Hisahide|Matsunaga Hisahide]].{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/7051|title=White-Threaded Yoroi (Detailed Listing)|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|access-date=December 1, 2019}}{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/7047|title=Red-Threaded Yoroi (Detailed Listing)|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|access-date=December 1, 2019}} == Access == * Nearest station: 25 minutes on foot from [[:en:Hasedera Station|Hasedera Station]], [[:en:Osaka Line|Kintetsu Osaka Line]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yoki-tenman-jinja}} * [https://www.yokitenmanjinja.jp/ Official Website of Yoki Tenman Shrine] {{translated page|ja|與喜天満神社|version=78784964|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:與喜天満神社]] {{Commons category|Yoki-tenman-jinja}} [[sn:3]] {{wikidata link|Q65279652}} [[Category:Accidentally overwritten pages]] [[Category:Buildings in Sakurai]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:History of Sakurai, Nara]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Pages likely having an accidental overwite in their history that shrank them]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]] [[Category:Sakurai, Nara]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Tenjin (kami)]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

Main Hall |location= [[:en:Sakurai, Nara|桜井市]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|奈良県]], {{ill|Hase, Sakurai|ja|初瀬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hase, Sakurai|1=Hase|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11396531}} 14 |Latitude = 34 | LatMinutes = 32 | LatSeconds = 05.9 | DirectionN = N |Longitude = 135 | LongMinutes = 54 | LongSeconds = 41.6 | DirectionE = E |MapCode = JP |Map= Japan Nara |LabelPosition= left |deity= {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} |ShrineRank= Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |consecration_year= 946 CE (Tenkei 9) |MainHall= |Alternatename= |AnnualFestival= |Rituals= }} '''Yoki Tenman Shrine''' (與喜天満神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Hase, Sakurai|ja|初瀬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hase, Sakurai|1=Hase|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11396531}}, [[:en:Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara]]. Its former Rank was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == Deities == ; Main deity * [[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]] (Sugawara no Michizane) ; Additional Deities * [[Amaterasu]] (天照大神) * [[Ōkurahime no Kami]] (大倉姫神) == History == The shrine is located in {{ill|Hase, Sakurai|ja|初瀬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hase, Sakurai|1=Hase|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11396531}}, believed to be the birthplace of [[:en:Nomi no Sukune|Nomino Sukune]], the ancestor of the [[:en:Haji clan|Haji clan]], later known as the Sugawara Clan. This historical connection made the area significant for the Sugawara family.{{Cite web|url=https://sakurai-kankou.jimdo.com/%E5%90%8D%E6%89%80%E6%97%A7%E8%B7%A1/%E5%88%9D%E7%80%AC%E9%81%93%E5%91%A8%E8%BE%BA/%E8%88%87%E5%96%9C%E5%A4%A9%E6%BA%80%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/|title=Yoki Tenman Shrine (Sakurai, Hase)|publisher=Sakurai City Tourism Association|access-date=April 23, 2019}} According to the ''{{ill|Hasedera Genki|ja|長谷寺験記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasedera Genki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'', the shrine was founded in 946 CE (Tenkei 9) and later became venerated as the guardian shrine of Hase. == Sub-Shrines == * Taki-no-Kura Gongen Shrine (deity: [[Kumano Gongen|Hayatama-no-Mikoto]], [[Izanagi]], [[:en:Izanami|Izanami]]) * Shiradaijin Shrine (deity: {{ill|Watarai Haruhiko|ja|度会春彦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai Haruhiko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) * Sakuraha Shrine (deity: {{ill|Iyo Shinnō|ja|伊予親王|1=Iyo Shinnō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Shinnō|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Shinnō|qq=}}) * Hachioji Shrine (deity: [[Amaterasu]], [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]) == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Wooden Seated Statue of Tenjin : Known as "Wrathful Tenjin," this seated statue in court attire is made of {{ill|yosegi-zukuri|ja|寄木造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yosegi-zukuri|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 仏像|1=Buddharupa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1000809}} and features an intense expression. It bears an inscription from Shogen 1 (1259), making it the oldest surviving sculpture of Tenjin in Japan.{{Cite web|url=https://www.nara-np.co.jp/news/20120421085721.html|title=Important Cultural Properties Newly Designated: Tenjin Seated Statue (Yoki Tenman Shrine)|publisher=Nara Newspaper|access-date=April 23, 2019}} Within the statue's head is a mirror engraved with an image of [[:en:Ekādaśamukha|Jūichimen Kannon]], symbolizing Tenjin's connection to the deity.{{Cite journal|title=New Cultural Property Designations|journal=Monthly Cultural Properties|issue=585|page=17|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|year=2012}} Previously enshrined in the main hall, it is now housed in the [[:en:Nara National Museum|Nara National Museum]]. === Additional Cultural Assets === [[File:Samurai O-Yoroi 2.jpg|thumb|200px|Reference: White-Threaded Yoroi (owned by [[:en:Hase-dera|Hasedera]], displayed at the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]])]] Five sets of important cultural property armor (White-Threaded Yoroi, Red-Threaded Yoroi, Hawk Feather Yoroi, Three-Eyed Plate Yoroi, Blue-Laced Armor) were once associated with Yoki Tenman Shrine, which served as [[:en:Hase-dera|Hasedera]]'s [[:en:Chinjugami|Chinjugami]]. These were gifted by {{ill|Kitabatake Norikata|ja|北畠教謙|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitabatake Norikata|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in 1560 (Eiroku 3) for services rendered in battles against [[:en:Matsunaga Hisahide|Matsunaga Hisahide]].{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/7051|title=White-Threaded Yoroi (Detailed Listing)|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|access-date=December 1, 2019}}{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/7047|title=Red-Threaded Yoroi (Detailed Listing)|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|access-date=December 1, 2019}} == Access == * Nearest station: 25 minutes on foot from [[:en:Hasedera Station|Hasedera Station]], [[:en:Osaka Line|Kintetsu Osaka Line]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yoki-tenman-jinja}} * [https://www.yokitenmanjinja.jp/ Official Website of Yoki Tenman Shrine] {{translated page|ja|與喜天満神社|version=78784964|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:與喜天満神社]] {{Commons category|Yoki-tenman-jinja}} [[sn:3]] {{wikidata link|Q65279652}} [[Category:Accidentally overwritten pages]] [[Category:Buildings in Sakurai]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:History of Sakurai, Nara]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Pages likely having an accidental overwite in their history that shrank them]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]] [[Category:Sakurai, Nara]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Tenjin (kami)]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

This historical connection made the area significant for the Sugawara family.{{Cite web|url=https://sakurai-kankou.jimdo.com/%E5%90%8D%E6%89%80%E6%97%A7%E8%B7%A1/%E5%88%9D%E7%80%AC%E9%81%93%E5%91%A8%E8%BE%BA/%E8%88%87%E5%96%9C%E5%A4%A9%E6%BA%80%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/|title=Yoki Tenman Shrine (Sakurai, Hase)|publisher=Sakurai City Tourism Association|access-date=April 23, 2019}} According to the ''{{ill|Hasedera Genki|ja|長谷寺験記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasedera Genki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'', the shrine was founded in 946 CE (Tenkei 9) and later became venerated as the guardian shrine of Hase. == Sub-Shrines == * Taki-no-Kura Gongen Shrine (deity: [[Kumano Gongen|Hayatama-no-Mikoto]], [[Izanagi]], [[:en:Izanami|Izanami]]) * Shiradaijin Shrine (deity: {{ill|Watarai Haruhiko|ja|度会春彦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai Haruhiko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) * Sakuraha Shrine (deity: {{ill|Iyo Shinnō|ja|伊予親王|1=Iyo Shinnō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Shinnō|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Shinnō|qq=}}) * Hachioji Shrine (deity: [[Amaterasu]], [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]) == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Wooden Seated Statue of Tenjin : Known as "Wrathful Tenjin," this seated statue in court attire is made of {{ill|yosegi-zukuri|ja|寄木造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yosegi-zukuri|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 仏像|1=Buddharupa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1000809}} and features an intense expression. It bears an inscription from Shogen 1 (1259), making it the oldest surviving sculpture of Tenjin in Japan.{{Cite web|url=https://www.nara-np.co.jp/news/20120421085721.html|title=Important Cultural Properties Newly Designated: Tenjin Seated Statue (Yoki Tenman Shrine)|publisher=Nara Newspaper|access-date=April 23, 2019}} Within the statue's head is a mirror engraved with an image of [[:en:Ekādaśamukha|Jūichimen Kannon]], symbolizing Tenjin's connection to the deity.{{Cite journal|title=New Cultural Property Designations|journal=Monthly Cultural Properties|issue=585|page=17|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|year=2012}} Previously enshrined in the main hall, it is now housed in the [[:en:Nara National Museum|Nara National Museum]]. === Additional Cultural Assets === [[File:Samurai O-Yoroi 2.jpg|thumb|200px|Reference: White-Threaded Yoroi (owned by [[:en:Hase-dera|Hasedera]], displayed at the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]])]] Five sets of important cultural property armor (White-Threaded Yoroi, Red-Threaded Yoroi, Hawk Feather Yoroi, Three-Eyed Plate Yoroi, Blue-Laced Armor) were once associated with Yoki Tenman Shrine, which served as [[:en:Hase-dera|Hasedera]]'s [[:en:Chinjugami|Chinjugami]]. These were gifted by {{ill|Kitabatake Norikata|ja|北畠教謙|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitabatake Norikata|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in 1560 (Eiroku 3) for services rendered in battles against [[:en:Matsunaga Hisahide|Matsunaga Hisahide]].{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/7051|title=White-Threaded Yoroi (Detailed Listing)|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|access-date=December 1, 2019}}{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/7047|title=Red-Threaded Yoroi (Detailed Listing)|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|access-date=December 1, 2019}} == Access == * Nearest station: 25 minutes on foot from [[:en:Hasedera Station|Hasedera Station]], [[:en:Osaka Line|Kintetsu Osaka Line]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yoki-tenman-jinja}} * [https://www.yokitenmanjinja.jp/ Official Website of Yoki Tenman Shrine] {{translated page|ja|與喜天満神社|version=78784964|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:與喜天満神社]] {{Commons category|Yoki-tenman-jinja}} [[sn:3]] {{wikidata link|Q65279652}} [[Category:Accidentally overwritten pages]] [[Category:Buildings in Sakurai]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:History of Sakurai, Nara]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Pages likely having an accidental overwite in their history that shrank them]] [[Category:Qq]] [[Category:Sakurai, Nara]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Tenjin (kami)]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

Main Hall |location= [[:en:Sakurai, Nara|桜井市]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|奈良県]], {{ill|Hase, Sakurai|ja|初瀬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hase, Sakurai|1=Hase|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11396531}} 14 |Latitude = 34 | LatMinutes = 32 | LatSeconds = 05.9 | DirectionN = N |Longitude = 135 | LongMinutes = 54 | LongSeconds = 41.6 | DirectionE = E |MapCode = JP |Map= Japan Nara |LabelPosition= left |deity= {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} |ShrineRank= Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |consecration_year= 946 CE (Tenkei 9) |MainHall= |Alternatename= |AnnualFestival= |Rituals= }} '''Yoki Tenman Shrine''' (與喜天満神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Hase, Sakurai|ja|初瀬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hase, Sakurai|1=Hase|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11396531}}, [[:en:Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara]]. Its former Rank was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == Deities == ; Main deity * [[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]] (Sugawara no Michizane) ; Additional Deities * [[Amaterasu]] (天照大神) * [[Ōkurahime no Kami]] (大倉姫神) == History == The shrine is located in {{ill|Hase, Sakurai|ja|初瀬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hase, Sakurai|1=Hase|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11396531}}, believed to be the birthplace of [[:en:Nomi no Sukune|Nomino Sukune]], the ancestor of the [[:en:Haji clan|Haji clan]], later known as the Sugawara Clan. This historical connection made the area significant for the Sugawara family.{{Cite web|url=https://sakurai-kankou.jimdo.com/%E5%90%8D%E6%89%80%E6%97%A7%E8%B7%A1/%E5%88%9D%E7%80%AC%E9%81%93%E5%91%A8%E8%BE%BA/%E8%88%87%E5%96%9C%E5%A4%A9%E6%BA%80%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/|title=Yoki Tenman Shrine (Sakurai, Hase)|publisher=Sakurai City Tourism Association|access-date=April 23, 2019}} According to the ''{{ill|Hasedera Genki|ja|長谷寺験記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasedera Genki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'', the shrine was founded in 946 CE (Tenkei 9) and later became venerated as the guardian shrine of Hase. == Sub-Shrines == * Taki-no-Kura Gongen Shrine (deity: [[Kumano Gongen|Hayatama-no-Mikoto]], [[Izanagi]], [[:en:Izanami|Izanami]]) * Shiradaijin Shrine (deity: {{ill|Watarai Haruhiko|ja|度会春彦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai Haruhiko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) * Sakuraha Shrine (deity: {{ill|Iyo Shinnō|ja|伊予親王|1=Iyo Shinnō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Shinnō|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Shinnō|qq=}}) * Hachioji Shrine (deity: [[Amaterasu]], [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]) == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Wooden Seated Statue of Tenjin : Known as "Wrathful Tenjin," this seated statue in court attire is made of {{ill|yosegi-zukuri|ja|寄木造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yosegi-zukuri|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 仏像|1=Buddharupa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1000809}} and features an intense expression. It bears an inscription from Shogen 1 (1259), making it the oldest surviving sculpture of Tenjin in Japan.{{Cite web|url=https://www.nara-np.co.jp/news/20120421085721.html|title=Important Cultural Properties Newly Designated: Tenjin Seated Statue (Yoki Tenman Shrine)|publisher=Nara Newspaper|access-date=April 23, 2019}} Within the statue's head is a mirror engraved with an image of [[:en:Ekādaśamukha|Jūichimen Kannon]], symbolizing Tenjin's connection to the deity.{{Cite journal|title=New Cultural Property Designations|journal=Monthly Cultural Properties|issue=585|page=17|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|year=2012}} Previously enshrined in the main hall, it is now housed in the [[:en:Nara National Museum|Nara National Museum]]. === Additional Cultural Assets === [[File:Samurai O-Yoroi 2.jpg|thumb|200px|Reference: White-Threaded Yoroi (owned by [[:en:Hase-dera|Hasedera]], displayed at the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]])]] Five sets of important cultural property armor (White-Threaded Yoroi, Red-Threaded Yoroi, Hawk Feather Yoroi, Three-Eyed Plate Yoroi, Blue-Laced Armor) were once associated with Yoki Tenman Shrine, which served as [[:en:Hase-dera|Hasedera]]'s [[:en:Chinjugami|Chinjugami]]. These were gifted by {{ill|Kitabatake Norikata|ja|北畠教謙|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitabatake Norikata|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in 1560 (Eiroku 3) for services rendered in battles against [[:en:Matsunaga Hisahide|Matsunaga Hisahide]].{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/7051|title=White-Threaded Yoroi (Detailed Listing)|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|access-date=December 1, 2019}}{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/7047|title=Red-Threaded Yoroi (Detailed Listing)|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|access-date=December 1, 2019}} == Access == * Nearest station: 25 minutes on foot from [[:en:Hasedera Station|Hasedera Station]], [[:en:Osaka Line|Kintetsu Osaka Line]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yoki-tenman-jinja}} * [https://www.yokitenmanjinja.jp/ Official Website of Yoki Tenman Shrine] {{translated page|ja|與喜天満神社|version=78784964|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:與喜天満神社]] {{Commons category|Yoki-tenman-jinja}} [[sn:3]] {{wikidata link|Q65279652}} [[Category:Accidentally overwritten pages]] [[Category:Buildings in Sakurai]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:History of Sakurai, Nara]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Pages likely having an accidental overwite in their history that shrank them]] [[Category:Sakurai, Nara]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Tenjin (kami)]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

This historical connection made the area significant for the Sugawara family.{{Cite web|url=https://sakurai-kankou.jimdo.com/%E5%90%8D%E6%89%80%E6%97%A7%E8%B7%A1/%E5%88%9D%E7%80%AC%E9%81%93%E5%91%A8%E8%BE%BA/%E8%88%87%E5%96%9C%E5%A4%A9%E6%BA%80%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/|title=Yoki Tenman Shrine (Sakurai, Hase)|publisher=Sakurai City Tourism Association|access-date=April 23, 2019}} According to the ''{{ill|Hasedera Genki|ja|長谷寺験記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasedera Genki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'', the shrine was founded in 946 CE (Tenkei 9) and later became venerated as the guardian shrine of Hase. == Sub-Shrines == * Taki-no-Kura Gongen Shrine (deity: [[Kumano Gongen|Hayatama-no-Mikoto]], [[Izanagi]], {{ill|Izanami|en|Izanami|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami}}) * Shiradaijin Shrine (deity: {{ill|Watarai Haruhiko|ja|度会春彦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai Haruhiko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) * Sakuraha Shrine (deity: {{ill|Iyo Shinnō|ja|伊予親王|1=Iyo Shinnō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Shinnō|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Shinnō|qq=}}) * Hachioji Shrine (deity: [[Amaterasu]], [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]) == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Wooden Seated Statue of Tenjin : Known as "Wrathful Tenjin," this seated statue in court attire is made of {{ill|yosegi-zukuri|ja|寄木造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yosegi-zukuri|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 仏像|1=Buddharupa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1000809}} and features an intense expression. It bears an inscription from Shogen 1 (1259), making it the oldest surviving sculpture of Tenjin in Japan.{{Cite web|url=https://www.nara-np.co.jp/news/20120421085721.html|title=Important Cultural Properties Newly Designated: Tenjin Seated Statue (Yoki Tenman Shrine)|publisher=Nara Newspaper|access-date=April 23, 2019}} Within the statue's head is a mirror engraved with an image of {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|en|Ekādaśamukha|WD=Q11404731|lt=Jūichimen Kannon}}, symbolizing Tenjin's connection to the deity.{{Cite journal|title=New Cultural Property Designations|journal=Monthly Cultural Properties|issue=585|page=17|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|year=2012}} Previously enshrined in the main hall, it is now housed in the {{ill|Nara National Museum|en|Nara National Museum|WD=Q147312|lt=Nara National Museum}}. === Additional Cultural Assets === [[File:Samurai O-Yoroi 2.jpg|thumb|200px|Reference: White-Threaded Yoroi (owned by {{ill|Hase-dera|en|Hase-dera|WD=Q1457787|lt=Hasedera}}, displayed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|en|Tokyo National Museum|WD=Q653433|lt=Tokyo National Museum}})]] Five sets of important cultural property armor (White-Threaded Yoroi, Red-Threaded Yoroi, Hawk Feather Yoroi, Three-Eyed Plate Yoroi, Blue-Laced Armor) were once associated with Yoki Tenman Shrine, which served as {{ill|Hase-dera|en|Hase-dera|WD=Q1457787|lt=Hasedera}}'s {{ill|Chinjugami|en|Chinjugami|WD=Q97479976|lt=Chinjugami}}. These were gifted by {{ill|Kitabatake Norikata|ja|北畠教謙|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitabatake Norikata|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in 1560 (Eiroku 3) for services rendered in battles against {{ill|Matsunaga Hisahide|en|Matsunaga Hisahide|WD=Q1143038|lt=Matsunaga Hisahide}}.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/7051|title=White-Threaded Yoroi (Detailed Listing)|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|access-date=December 1, 2019}}{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/7047|title=Red-Threaded Yoroi (Detailed Listing)|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|access-date=December 1, 2019}} == Access == * Nearest station: 25 minutes on foot from {{ill|Hasedera Station|en|Hasedera Station|WD=Q5678737|lt=Hasedera Station}}, {{ill|Osaka Line|en|Osaka Line|WD=Q1052545|lt=Kintetsu Osaka Line}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yoki-tenman-jinja}} * [https://www.yokitenmanjinja.jp/ Official Website of Yoki Tenman Shrine] {{translated page|ja|與喜天満神社|version=78784964|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:與喜天満神社]] {{Commons category|Yoki-tenman-jinja}} [[sn:3]] {{wikidata link|Q65279652}} [[Category:Accidentally overwritten pages]] [[Category:Buildings in Sakurai]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:History of Sakurai, Nara]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Pages likely having an accidental overwite in their history that shrank them]] [[Category:Sakurai, Nara]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Tenjin (kami)]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
